A **My uploads** area lets you edit a config's description inline or delete it any time, and uploads are ingested into the in-app catalog automatically. - **Captures your full game setup.** A shared config now saves **38 of 39 shortcut settings** (everything except one obsolete legacy fullscreen flag that's already folded into the fullscreen-mode setting) — not just the core wrapper/driver versions: - **DXVK / VKD3D:** the *full* config — version, async, asyncCache, framerate, vkd3dLevel, ddrawrapper, csmt, GPU spoof (gpuName / videoMemorySize), strict shader math, offscreen-rendering mode, renderer and custom DXVK conf. - **Graphics:** resolution, renderer, render scale, SurfaceFlinger compat, fullscreen mode, frame generation, FPS limiter, sharpness / FSR, ReShade (mode / effect / loadout / params). - **Emulator:** Box64 version + preset, FEXCore version + preset. - **Wrapper** selection (including **VEGAS**) + **GPU (Turnip) driver** version. - **Input:** xinput / dinput, exclusive / disable xinput, sim-touchscreen, controller count, control profile. - **Kept separate and private-by-default.** Bannerlator stores its configs in **its own space**, and a shared config never carries your files, your store logins, or device-specific driver tuning — just the game's settings and which device they came from. > 📖 **[Read the full plain-English guide →](https://github.com/The412Banner/Bannerlator/blob/main/docs/community-configs-guide.md)** for a friendly, non-technical walkthrough of browsing, applying, sharing, and the optional account / recovery-key system. ### 👤 Optional accounts — new A brand-new, **completely optional** account system. You never need one — browsing, applying, sharing, managing your uploads, upvoting and commenting all still work **anonymously**. But signing up adds three conveniences: - **Your uploads follow you** to a new device or after a reinstall. - **Your name and picture** appear on the configs you share (instead of "Anonymous user"). - **No email required** — just a username and password, recovered with a **one-time recovery key** instead of an email reset. Sign in from the globe browser or the navigation drawer; a friendly avatar replaces the person icon once you set one. ### 🎨 Config polish - **Every wrapper choice now round-trips.** A shared config carries the exact wrapper you picked — **VEGAS included** — instead of only inferring it from version numbers, so applying it reproduces your setup faithfully. - **Clearer "Your device" header.** The Community Configs header now shows your **device model *and* your graphics chip / SoC** as two separate values, and reads **"Unresolved"** for either one Android can't report — so you always know exactly what was detected. (Device matching is unchanged.) - **VEGAS config dialog, improved — community contribution.** Merged from **[isygold](https://github.com/isygold)** ([#84](https://github.com/The412Banner/Bannerlator/pull/84)): the VEGAS DXVK config dialog gains **delete**, **local install** and a **download progress bar**, and a duplicate VEGAS wrapper entry was cleaned up. > 🚀 **Support VEGAS Development** — low-level graphics dev & vibecoder: debugging, refactoring & improving original DXVK code for Adreno. **[❤️ Sponsor isygold →](https://github.com/sponsors/isygold)** ## 🙌 Powered by the community Community Configs is built on configs created by **real people** who have used and supported **[BannerHub](https://github.com/The412Banner/BannerHub)** and **BannerHub Lite** and shared their working per-game setups. With 2.6 the loop is complete — now **anyone can contribute back** and help the shared catalog grow. **Thank you to everyone who has shared a config.** 🙏 - 📦 **Source repo:** - 🌐 **Live catalog:** As of this release the community cache holds **10,038 configs** across **2,128 games**, of which Bannerlator matches **1,263 games** into its in-app catalog — **covering 8,942 configs (~89% of everything uploaded)** — and it keeps growing every time someone shares, now including configs uploaded straight from Bannerlator with 2.6's new sharing. ## 📥 Downloads | Variant | File | |---|---| | **Standard** | `Bannerlator-2.6-standard.apk` | | **PUBG** | `Bannerlator-2.6-pubg.apk` | | **Ludashi** | `Bannerlator-2.6-ludashi.apk` | > ℹ️ **Updating in-app?** 2.6 is **entirely app-side** — the standard APK covers everything. There's **no ImageFS reinstall**: your containers, themes, custom accent, per-game settings and control colours all carry over. Just install over 2.5.2. ## 🔗 Notes - **Community Config Sharing** and **accounts** are backed by a first-party service, kept separate from your device and from any third party. Accounts are optional and email-free; nothing about your existing containers or settings changes unless you apply a config.

releases.name: Bannerlator 2.5.2
releases.body: # Bannerlator 2.5.2 Run Windows apps and games on Android — no PC and no root required. **2.5.2 is the Community Configs release.** It adds a way to browse **community-shared, per-game / per-device tuning configs** right inside the app and apply a known-good setup to your game in **one tap** — no more hunting through Discord screenshots or guessing which DXVK/driver/FEX combo someone used. It also ships **two new Proton 11 x86-64 compatibility layers** in the downloadable catalog. Like the last few releases this is an **entirely app-side** update — **no ImageFS reinstall** — your containers, themes, custom accent and per-game settings all carry over untouched. Just install over 2.5.1. ## ✨ What's New ### 🌐 Community Configs — new A new **globe** button in the **Games** header opens a browsable catalog of community game-configs. Bannerlator matches your installed games to the catalog, and a **"Matches my device"** filter narrows it to configs shared from hardware like yours. Each shared config is its own card showing its **★ upvotes** and **↓ downloads** (best-rated first), the device it came from, and its date. - **One-tap Apply.** Tap a config → **Apply to game**, and Bannerlator **surgically merges** just that config's settings — DXVK / VKD3D / Turnip driver / FEX preset / renderer / resolution / launch args / environment variables — into your shortcut, **preserving everything else you've set** (BCn options, HUD, colour toggles and the rest stay untouched). You can even apply a config to a *different* game — it warns you first. - **Smart install of what's missing.** If a config needs a **DXVK / VKD3D / FEXCore** build or a **Turnip GPU driver** you don't have, install it inline right there — an exact match installs with one confirm, or you pick from the closest versions (or browse all) — and the config **auto-applies** once it lands, with a ✓. FEX date-stamped builds are matched by their **YYMM** monthly tag so a dated config resolves to the right build, and a component you already have is recognised instead of erroring. - **Config detail page.** Tap **View details** for a config's provenance (source device / SoC / app / date), exactly **what it sets** in Bannerlator's own component terms, and a **preview of what would change** on your shortcut before you apply. The detail page also shows the config's **description, upvotes, downloads and comments** live — and you can **upvote** and **leave a comment** yourself. > ℹ️ Community Configs is **read-only for your setup** — it only ever changes a shortcut when *you* tap Apply, and it never touches your containers, ImageFS or existing settings. ### 🍷 Two new Proton 11 x86-64 compatibility layers — new Two **Proton 11.0-1 (x86_64)** compatibility layers are now downloadable in-app from the **Compatibility Layers** menu (Wine/Proton tab), packaged per Android version: - **`proton-11.0-1-x86_64-sdk28`** — for **Android 9-era** devices (SDK 28) - **`proton-11.0-1-x86_64-sdk35`** — for **Android 15** (SDK 35) Pick the one matching your Android version. ## 🙌 Powered by the community Every config in this catalog was created by **the community** — real people who have used and supported **[BannerHub](https://github.com/The412Banner/BannerHub)** and **BannerHub Lite** and exported their working per-game setups, building up this shared cache over time. Community Configs simply brings that library into Bannerlator. **A huge thank-you to everyone who contributed a config** — this whole feature is built entirely on your work. 🙏 - 📦 **Source repo:** - 🌐 **Live catalog:** As of this release the community cache holds **9,965 configs** across **2,121 games**, of which Bannerlator matches **1,258 games** into its in-app catalog — **92% of all uploaded configs covered** — and it keeps growing as more people share. ## 🔜 Coming next — share your own configs This release is the **browse-and-apply** half of Community Configs. The **final phase** is being worked on over the coming days: the ability to **share your own configs — both locally and online — right from the same Community Configs menu** added in this release. Once it lands you'll be able to contribute your working setups back to the community and help the catalog grow even faster. ## 📥 Downloads | Variant | File | |---|---| | **Standard** | `Bannerlator-2.5.2-standard.apk` | | **PUBG** | `Bannerlator-2.5.2-pubg.apk` | | **Ludashi** | `Bannerlator-2.5.2-ludashi.apk` | > ℹ️ **Updating in-app?** 2.5.2 is **entirely app-side** — the standard APK covers everything. There's **no ImageFS reinstall**: your containers, themes, custom accent, per-game settings and control colours all carry over. The two new Proton 11 x86-64 layers are optional downloads from the Compatibility Layers menu. ## 🔗 Notes - **Community Configs** is backed by a first-party config service; browsing, applying, upvoting and commenting all happen through it. It's additive and opt-in — nothing about your existing containers or settings changes unless you apply a config.

releases.name: Bannerlator 2.5.1
releases.body: # Bannerlator 2.5.1 Run Windows apps and games on Android — no PC and no root required. **2.5.1 is a compatibility-and-tooling follow-up to 2.5.** It fixes crashes and wrong colours on the **SurfaceFlinger** renderer, adds a live **FEX runtime indicator** so you can see exactly what's translating your game, makes **FEXCore unixlib (`.so`)** handling correct when you swap FEXCore versions per game, and expands the **Environment Variable presets**. Like 2.5, this is an **entirely app-side** update — **no ImageFS reinstall** — your containers, themes, custom accent and per-game settings all carry over untouched. Just install over 2.5. ## ✨ What's New ### 🎨 SurfaceFlinger renderer: colour & crash fix — new The **SurfaceFlinger** host renderer (ASurfaceRenderer) got a crash-and-colour-accuracy fix: the red/blue channel swap is corrected, a GPU-side format converter and proper fencing were added, and the OpenGL / Vulkan / DRI3 paths are left untouched. Games that crashed or rendered with swapped colours on the SurfaceFlinger renderer now display correctly — **with no regression to the Vulkan or OpenGL renderers**. - New **"Correct SurfaceFlinger colours"** toggle — available **per-container and per-game**, shown inline under the **Renderer** picker when SurfaceFlinger is selected (on by default). - Device-verified on **Adreno 750**: *DiRT 3* on **DXVK + SurfaceFlinger** renders with correct colours, no R/B swap, and no Vulkan/OpenGL regression. > 🔁 Ported from **GameNative #1620 / #1644**. ### 🔎 FEX runtime indicator — new The **Graphics** tab now shows a small badge telling you exactly what's running under the hood, read **live from the running game** — no guessing: - **arm64ec** vs **x86-64** - the translator — **FEXCore** / **wowbox64** / **Box64** - for FEXCore, whether the native **unixlib (`.so`)** or the classic **DLL** path is active ### 🧩 FEXCore unixlib (`.so`) auto-match — new Users often run **different FEXCore versions per game or container** — some DLL-only, some with the native `.so` unixlib. 2.5.1 keeps the shared `.so` **exactly in sync with the version you selected, on every launch**: - Pick a unixlib FEXCore → its **matching** `.so` is placed automatically. - Pick a DLL-only FEXCore → the `.so` is **cleared**, so you get a true DLL run. - Flip back and forth freely — each launch re-stamps it, so there's **no stale `.so`** silently overriding a DLL-only choice and **no mismatched `.dll`/`.so` pair**. - Uninstalling a unixlib FEXCore now removes its `.so` too. ### 🧰 18 new Environment Variable presets The **Add Environment Variable** picker gained **18 presets** across **DXVK, VKD3D, Wine and Mesa** — so common tuning vars are one tap away instead of typed by hand. Highlights: `DXVK_DISABLE_TIMELINE_SEMAPHORES`, `VKD3D_SHADER_MODEL`, `DXVK_FRAME_RATE`, `VKD3D_CONFIG`, `WINEFSYNC`, `WINE_FULLSCREEN_FSR`, `MESA_VK_WSI_PRESENT_MODE` and more. > 💬 Requested by **Angel** (`DXVK_DISABLE_TIMELINE_SEMAPHORES` + `VKD3D_SHADER_MODEL`). ## 📥 Downloads | Variant | File | |---|---| | **Standard** | `Bannerlator-2.5.1-standard.apk` | | **PUBG** | `Bannerlator-2.5.1-pubg.apk` | | **Ludashi** | `Bannerlator-2.5.1-ludashi.apk` | > ℹ️ **Updating in-app?** 2.5.1 is **entirely app-side** — the standard APK covers everything. There's **no ImageFS reinstall**: your containers, themes, custom accent, per-game settings and control colours all carry over. The FEXCore `.so` sync happens automatically on your next game launch — including cleaning up any stale `.so` left from earlier installs. ## 🔗 Issues in this release - **GameNative #1620 / #1644** — SurfaceFlinger crash + colour fix 🚀 Ported & shipped --- ## 🙏 Credits - **Angel** — requested the environment-variable presets that seeded the new preset set (`DXVK_DISABLE_TIMELINE_SEMAPHORES`, `VKD3D_SHADER_MODEL`). - **[GameNative](https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative)** (utkarshdalal) — upstream source of the SurfaceFlinger crash + colour fix (#1620 / #1644) the ASurfaceRenderer fix is ported from. The SurfaceFlinger colour toggle, the FEX runtime indicator, the FEXCore `.so` auto-match, and the preset expansion — the wiring into Bannerlator — is original engineering by **The412Banner**. Full lineage and credits are in the [README](https://github.com/The412Banner/Bannerlator#-credits). ----- If Mali users have serious issues after updating to 2.5.1 , use the 2.5 apk files on THIS RELEASE to roll back. Provide logs,otherwise I have no way to fix anything

releases.name: Bannerlator 2.5
releases.body: # Bannerlator 2.5 Run Windows apps and games on Android — no PC and no root required. **2.5 is the Mali hardening release.** If you're on a **Mali** or **Xclipse** GPU, this is the big one: BC-texture games that used to crash or render as black/garbled textures now actually work. It's backed by a full sign-off on real Mali-G57 hardware, plus a round of in-game logging quality-of-life so reporting problems no longer means hunting through `Android/data`. This is an **entirely app-side** update — **no ImageFS reinstall** — your existing containers, themes, custom accent and per-game settings all carry over untouched. ## ✨ What's New ### 🟢 Mali & Xclipse: BC-texture games now work — new Mali and Xclipse GPUs don't decode BC (BCn) textures in hardware, so a huge number of games crashed, ran out of VRAM, or showed black/missing textures. **2.5 adds a new graphics driver — "Wrapper + bcn_layer"** — that transcodes those textures on the GPU at runtime so the games just run. - Pick it per container: **Container → Graphics driver → "Wrapper + bcn_layer"**. - Device-proven on **Mali-G57 (Helio G99)**: *MiSide* (Unity/BCn) went from crashing to rendering correctly at **~34 fps**, with **zero** buffer errors. - Backed by leegao's **bcn_layer shader-v3** — up to ~3.5× faster BCn/ASTC transcoding than the first version. > 💬 Driven end-to-end by **@kylinzang** on Mali-G57 — #70 (originally #54 / #53) ### 🧪 Wrapper-gamenative — new (experimental) A second BCn-capable driver with the transcoder baked into the wrapper itself. It's **Adreno-only** in practice (on Mali it initialises but the BCn path never fires), so it ships **labelled experimental** as an A/B option — `Wrapper + bcn_layer` remains the Mali path. ### 🧰 BCn Layer Settings A new section in the container's graphics-driver dialog to tune the transcoder: force-decode, **ETC2** / **ASTC** transcode toggles, image-view vs. staging mode, and debug logging. ### 🧾 In-game logging overhaul Reporting a bug from inside a game is finally painless: - **Copy button** in the in-game Debug Logs panel — copies the whole log (including the BCn transfer stats) to the clipboard, so you can paste it straight into a report instead of screenshotting. - **Selectable log location** (**Settings → Logs**) — App data, **Download/bannerlator**, **Documents/bannerlator**, or a custom folder. Grab every log straight from a file manager — **no shizuku or adb needed**. - **DXVK, DXGI and VKD3D logs are co-located** with the Wine log in one folder (they no longer hide inside the game directory), kept as separate files. - The **Wine Debug Channels** dialog now **scrolls** — you can reach `vulkan`, `heap` and every other channel past the first screenful. > 💬 All three logging ideas suggested by **@kylinzang** — #70 ## 🔧 Fixes & polish - **`wrapper_DestroyBuffer: null buffer` spam eliminated** — the bundled Vulkan wrapper was bumped to **ETC2-Milestone-2**, which cleans up buffer tracking (0 errors confirmed on Mali-G57). - **Copy-logs button now always reachable** — the in-game Debug Logs action row (Copy / Clear / Pause / Close) is pinned to the bottom of the dialog; in landscape it was previously pushed off-screen. - **Release-notes pipeline hardened** — backticks and shell metacharacters in release notes no longer break the update-manifest step. ## 📥 Downloads | Variant | File | |---|---| | **Standard** | `Bannerlator-2.5-standard.apk` | | **PUBG** | `Bannerlator-2.5-pubg.apk` | | **Ludashi** | `Bannerlator-2.5-ludashi.apk` | > ℹ️ **Updating in-app?** 2.5 is **entirely app-side** — the standard APK covers everything. There's **no ImageFS reinstall**: your containers, themes, custom accent, per-game settings and control colours all carry over. The new Mali driver assets are pulled into your existing containers automatically on next launch. ## 🔗 Issues in this release - #70 — Mali BCn layer (tracking hub for #54 / #53 / #63 / #64) 🚀 Shipped - #30 — Mali-G610 BCn candidate 🔎 In testing --- > 🍷 **Also fresh — Proton 11.0-1 (experimental, separate download):** a brand-new **bionic arm64ec** build of **Proton 11.0-1** (Wine 11.0 final, with esync / fsync / ntsync compiled in) is available as an installable Wine runtime — grab it here: **[build-p11-20260707](https://github.com/The412Banner/proton-wine/releases/tag/build-p11-20260707)**. Import it through the in-app compat-layer download / file picker as a Wine version. It's a separate, experimental runtime — **not required for 2.5**. --- ## 🙏 Credits 2.5 exists because the Mali community kept testing and reporting. Thank you: - **[@kylinzang](https://github.com/kylinzang)** — the driving force behind the entire Mali/BCn effort: the original request (#54 / #53), the full env-var spec, and iterative on-device testing on Mali-G57 through mali-v4 → mali-v6, ending in a full sign-off. The logging overhaul was his idea too (#70). - **[@rizky2-crypto](https://github.com/rizky2-crypto)** — Mali-G610 testing (#30). Built on the work of: - **[leegao](https://github.com/leegao)** — [`bcn_layer`](https://github.com/leegao/bcn_layer) (shader-v3) and [`bionic-vulkan-wrapper`](https://github.com/leegao/bionic-vulkan-wrapper) (ETC2-Milestone-2). This release would not exist without it. - **[GameNative](https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative)** (utkarshdalal) — the base for the experimental `Wrapper-gamenative` driver. Everything wiring these into Bannerlator — the driver options, the BCn Layer Settings UI, the logging overhaul and the fixes above — is original engineering by **The412Banner**. Full lineage and credits are in the [README](https://github.com/The412Banner/Bannerlator#-credits). And thanks to everyone in the Discord and on GitHub who tested these builds — your reports drove this release.

releases.name: Bannerlator 2.4
releases.body: # Bannerlator 2.4 Run Windows apps and games on Android — no PC and no root required. **2.4 is the fit & finish release.** Take control of how your game fills the screen, import files without fighting Android's system picker, and make your in-game choices stick — per game. This is an **entirely app-side** update — **no ImageFS reinstall** — your existing containers, themes, custom accent and per-game settings all carry over untouched; it's a straight in-place APK update. ## ✨ What's New ### 🖥️ Fullscreen aspect-ratio modes — new No more forced stretching on wide games. Choose exactly how the picture fits the screen, **per container *and* per game**: - **Off** — windowed, letterboxed - **Fit** — fullscreen, aspect preserved (letterbox bars) - **Stretch** — fills the screen, ignores aspect - **Fill** — fills the screen with aspect kept, cropping the overflow (no bars) - **Integer** — largest whole-number scale, pixel-perfect and centered Switch live from the in-game drawer with a new **five-button selector** that applies instantly and keeps the drawer open so you can compare modes. Works on the OpenGL, Vulkan and SurfaceFlinger renderers. > 💬 Requested by **@kylinzang** — #71 ### 📁 Import files with the built-in File Manager — new Android's system file picker (SAF) silently fails or crawls on a lot of OEM skins. Every local import now opens **Bannerlator's own File Manager** instead — the same screen you already browse containers with, in a pick mode — so importing works reliably on every device and is far faster for big files. - Covers **every import** — content packs (`.wcp`), control profiles (`.icp`), wallpapers, custom icons, saves, shortcuts, drivers, and the big **Proton / Wine / DXVK / box64 / FEXCore** assets. - Starts in **Download**, remembers your last folder, and filters to the file types you need. - **Image files show a real thumbnail** (jpg/png/webp…) instead of a generic icon — so picking a wallpaper or icon is no longer done blind. - Large imports now show a **progress bar with percentage and ETA** — no more wondering if a big Proton extract froze. - Prefer the old way? The system picker is still there as a **"Pick via system…"** option for cloud / document providers. > 💬 Requested by **@kylinzang** — #73 ### 🎚️ In-game choices that stick — per game — new Three settings you tune in-game are now remembered per game and restored on relaunch: - **Scaling mode** — SGSR / FSR / FSR-Fit / Sharpen / NIS used to reset to a plain filter every launch; now your pick is saved. - **Fullscreen mode** — your Off / Fit / Stretch / Fill / Integer choice is kept. - **FPS overlay position** — drag the classic or GameHub-style HUD where you want it and it stays put next time. ### 🧩 Choose your DLC + smarter Steam installs — new - **DLC picker** — a "Choose DLC" sheet lets you opt out of owned DLC before downloading, with the download size updating **live** as you check and uncheck. - **"Includes DLC" line** on a game's detail page, plus correct handling of DLC that ships inside a base game. - **True-size install fix** — Steam's catalog over-reported some games' sizes, so a fully-downloaded game could be wrongly flagged "incomplete" and refuse to launch. Bannerlator now fetches the real depot-manifest sizes and installs complete. - **Size breakdown** on the detail page — on-disk footprint, download size, catalog size and free space at a glance. - **Download ETA + speed** across the detail page, the Download Manager and the notification, smoothed so the numbers don't jump. > 💬 Steam install-blocker reported by **@Devaspe** ### 🖼️ Container wallpaper picker — new Pick an image as your container's desktop wallpaper — the picker now actually appears in the container editor — and choose whether a wallpaper applies to **just this container** or **globally** to all of them (and "global" now truly means global). > 💬 Reported by **@SombraShadow** — #66 ### 💾 Back up & restore game saves — new Export and import your saves as **GameHub-compatible zips**, with per-game save discovery, a confirm checklist before anything is overwritten, and a caution prompt so you verify before replacing originals. ## 🔧 Fixes & polish - **Magnifier follows the cursor on Vulkan** — parity with OpenGL, in both fullscreen and windowed. (#44) - **Magnifier no longer dims the whole screen** while zoomed (OpenGL + Vulkan). - **Settings crash fixed** — the "Enable File Provider" help button no longer force-closes. - **Scrape cover** is now available from the list-view overflow menu, not just the grid. - Assorted download-state and size-reporting corrections carried over from the storefronts work. > 💬 Magnifier reported by **@abdogm** — #44 ## 📥 Downloads | Variant | File | |---|---| | **Standard** | `Bannerlator-2.4-standard.apk` | | **PUBG** | `Bannerlator-2.4-pubg.apk` | | **Ludashi** | `Bannerlator-2.4-ludashi.apk` | > ℹ️ **Updating in-app?** 2.4 is **entirely app-side** — the standard APK covers everything. There's **no ImageFS reinstall** required: your containers, themes, custom accent, per-game settings and control colours all carry over untouched. ## 🔗 Issues in this release - #71 — Fullscreen aspect ratio ✅ Closed - #66 — Wallpaper picker ✅ Closed - #44 — Magnifier ✅ Closed - #73 — In-app file picker 🚀 Shipped --- ## 🙏 Credits 2.4 is a community-driven release — nearly every headline feature came from a report or request on GitHub. Thank you to the people who filed them, tested builds, and drove this release: - **[@kylinzang](https://github.com/kylinzang)** — Fullscreen aspect ratio (#71) **and** the in-app file picker (#73) - **[@SombraShadow](https://github.com/SombraShadow)** — Container wallpaper picker (#66) - **[@abdogm](https://github.com/abdogm)** — Magnifier fixes (#44) - **[@Devaspe](https://github.com/Devaspe)** — Steam install-blocker report that drove the true-size install fix The true-size Steam install fix builds on the depot engine from **[JavaSteam](https://github.com/Longi94/JavaSteam)** (Longi94). Everything built on top — the fullscreen aspect-ratio pipeline, the in-app file picker, the DLC picker, per-game persistence and the fixes above — is original engineering by **The412Banner**. Full lineage and credits are in the [README](https://github.com/The412Banner/Bannerlator#-credits). And thanks to everyone in the Discord and on GitHub who tested these builds and reported issues — your reports drove this release.

releases.name: Bannerlator mali-v6
releases.body: ## Changelog | Commit | Title | |--------|--------| | `547527a` | fix(debug-drawer): pin Copy/Clear/Pause/Close row on-screen in landscape | | `b9396f6` | ci(release): pass release_notes via env in update.json step so backticks/$/newlines don't trigger shell command-substitution (exit 127) | | `3ed151d` | mali-v5: label build (versionName 2.5-pre2-mali-v5, vc stays 39) — adds #70 logging batch on top of device-proven mali-v4 | | `ada05b5` | Logs UX: selectable log location, co-located DXVK/VKD3D logs, in-game Copy button (#70) | | `c178ccb` | mali-v4: hold versionCode at 39 (=2.4) for the tester pre-release so 2.4 installs over it cleanly (#70) | | `6fcf27e` | wip(steam-cloud): safe Steam Cloud Saves foundation (staging-folder; UFS→prefix mapping TODO) | | `7e5cae3` | fix(bcn): emit BCN_PROFILE_TRANSFERS with debug log + show ASTC toggle on gamenative (#70) | | `56c1233` | assets: bump wrapper-leegao.tzst to bionic-vulkan-wrapper ETC2-Milestone-2 | | `4e2b969` | Fix un-scrollable Wine Debug Channels dialog (#70) | | `eaa8168` | mali-v4: bump to 2.5-pre2 (vc41) for Mali BCn bug-fix pass (#70) | mali-v6 fixes the one open item from mali-v5: the in-game Copy-logs button. What changed - The in-game Debug Logs dialog action row (Copy / Clear / Pause / Close) is now pinned to the bottom of the panel and always visible. Previously, in landscape, the log list filled the whole height and pushed the Copy button off the bottom edge. The log list now scrolls within the space above the buttons. - Everything else is identical to mali-v5 (shader-v3 bcn_layer, ETC2-Milestone-2 wrapper, selectable log location, co-located DXVK/DXGI/VKD3D logs). Install - versionCode 39 (same as 2.4) and same signature, so it installs over 2.4 or mali-v5 with no uninstall and your containers are preserved. - This is a pre-release: it is not offered to stable users and does not touch the 2.4 in-app updater. - If you are on the earlier mali-v4 CI artifact (vc41), uninstall that first (vc41 to vc39 is a downgrade and Android will block it). Credits: leegao (bcn_layer shader-v3 + ETC2-Milestone-2).

releases.name: Bannerlator 2.5-pre2 — Mali BCn preview (mali-v5)
releases.body: ## Changelog | Commit | Title | |--------|--------| | `b9396f6` | ci(release): pass release_notes via env in update.json step so backticks/$/newlines don't trigger shell command-substitution (exit 127) | | `3ed151d` | mali-v5: label build (versionName 2.5-pre2-mali-v5, vc stays 39) — adds #70 logging batch on top of device-proven mali-v4 | | `ada05b5` | Logs UX: selectable log location, co-located DXVK/VKD3D logs, in-game Copy button (#70) | | `c178ccb` | mali-v4: hold versionCode at 39 (=2.4) for the tester pre-release so 2.4 installs over it cleanly (#70) | | `6fcf27e` | wip(steam-cloud): safe Steam Cloud Saves foundation (staging-folder; UFS→prefix mapping TODO) | | `7e5cae3` | fix(bcn): emit BCN_PROFILE_TRANSFERS with debug log + show ASTC toggle on gamenative (#70) | | `56c1233` | assets: bump wrapper-leegao.tzst to bionic-vulkan-wrapper ETC2-Milestone-2 | | `4e2b969` | Fix un-scrollable Wine Debug Channels dialog (#70) | | `eaa8168` | mali-v4: bump to 2.5-pre2 (vc41) for Mali BCn bug-fix pass (#70) | | `7038ff2` | feat(bcn): integrated-BCn Wrapper-gamenative + ASTC toggle (2.5-pre1, vc40) | **Mali BCn preview build (issue #70) — testers only.** This is a **pre-release**: it is *not* offered to stable users and does not change the 2.4 update channel. Version `2.5-pre2-mali-v5` · versionCode **39** (same as 2.4). Because every build shares one signature, **2.4 installs over this and this installs over 2.4 freely** — no uninstall needed. ⚠️ If you're on the earlier **mali-v4 CI artifact (vc41)**, uninstall it first (vc41 → vc39 is a downgrade and will be blocked). ### ✅ Confirmed on Mali (G57 / MiSide, device-tested by @kylinzang) - **`Wrapper + bcn_layer` (leegao shader-v3)** — runs correctly, ~34 fps (up from 21–31), 842 BCn-decode + 842 ASTC-encode calls, avg 311 MB/s. - **Wrapper bumped to leegao ETC2-Milestone-2** — **0 `wrapper_DestroyBuffer` errors** (was batches per scene load), + G57 r32p1 feature-faking. - **BCn debug log now writes** — the toggle now sets both `BCN_LAYER_LOG_LEVEL` and `BCN_PROFILE_TRANSFERS`. - **Wine Debug Channels dialog scrolls** — the full channel list (vulkan, heap, …) is reachable. - `Wrapper-gamenative` is confirmed **Adreno-only** on the BCn path and stays labelled experimental — use `Wrapper + bcn_layer` on Mali. ### 🆕 New in mali-v5 (logging UX, from @kylinzang's suggestions) - **Copy-logs button** on the in-game debug-logs drawer — copies the whole log to the clipboard. - **All logs saved in one folder, kept as separate files** — `wine_debug.log`, DXVK `*_d3d11.log` / `*_dxgi.log`, and `vkd3d-proton.log` now land in the same chosen folder (DXVK/VKD3D no longer hide in the game folder). - **Selectable log location** — Settings › Logs › **Log location**: App data (default) / Download/bannerlator / Documents/bannerlator / Choose folder… — so you can grab logs from a file manager without shizuku/adb. ### Credits leegao — bcn_layer **shader-v3** + **ETC2-Milestone-2** wrapper. Testing: @kylinzang (Mali-G57), @rizky2-crypto (#30, Mali-G610).

releases.name: Bannerlator 2.3
releases.body:
# Bannerlator 2.3 Run Windows apps and games on Android — no PC and no root required. **2.3 is the storefronts release.** Bannerlator now has a full **built-in Steam store** — sign in with your account (username/password **or** QR code), browse your owned library, and **download and install** your games straight to a container via a native depot engine. Alongside Steam it adds **Epic Games** and **Amazon Games** stores and wires the existing **GOG** store into a new **cross-store Download Manager** — one place to see every active download and your whole installed library across all four stores, launch, and uninstall, with **background downloads** that keep going (and show up in your **notification shade**) after you leave the app. For offline / emulated play there's an optional **Goldberg auto-patch** for Steam games, and this release also **hardens store logs** so your account credentials and tokens are scrubbed from anything you might share. This is an **entirely app-side** update — **no ImageFS reinstall** — your existing containers, themes and settings carry over untouched; it's a straight in-place APK update. ## ✨ What's New ### 🎮 Built-in Steam store — new Sign in to **Steam** right inside Bannerlator and play from your own library. - **Sign in your way** — **username + password** *or* **QR code** (scan it with the Steam mobile app). Your session is remembered between launches. - **Browse your owned library** and open a game's detail page with cover art and store metadata. - **Download + install** games through the built-in **depot engine** (built on **[JavaSteam](https://github.com/Longi94/JavaSteam)**), straight into a Wine container ready to launch. - **GameNative-style download speed — 4 tiers.** Pick **Slow / Medium / Fast / Blazing**; the tier scales concurrency by CPU cores × a ratio, so you can trade heat/battery for throughput. - **Session hardening.** Long installs used to die around the ~1-hour mark when Steam's connection manager logged the session off. Bannerlator now **detects the logoff and recovers** — reconnect, re-login, hold a wakelock and keep the session alive — so big downloads survive to the end. - **Connection / login status pill** in the store header tells you at a glance whether you're connected and signed in. - **Depot-download OOM fix** — decompress and file-write concurrency are capped so large games no longer run the app out of memory mid-download. - Optional per-download **"Log debug session"** toggle for diagnostics (off by default). ### 🕹️ Goldberg auto-patch — new On a Steam game's detail page you can apply a **[Goldberg](https://mr_goldberg.gitlab.io/goldberg_emulator/) / gbe_fork** patch for **offline / emulated** play, in three tiers — **Regular**, **Experimental** and **ColdClient** — installed for you automatically (and cleanly reverted when you switch back to Off). > ⚠️ **Use at your own risk.** The Steam Emulator patch modifies a game's shipped files to run it offline/emulated. It's provided as-is for games you own; use it at your own discretion. ### ⬇️ Cross-store Download Manager — new One unified **⬇ Download Manager** across **all four stores**. - **Everything in one place** — active downloads *and* your installed library from Steam, Epic, GOG and Amazon, side by side. - **Live two-bar progress** — a distinct download bar and install bar with byte-accurate size and percentage. - **Background downloads + shade notifications** — a foreground service keeps downloads running when you leave the app, with a live notification you can tap to jump back into the manager. Leave the app, come back, it's still going. - **Launch or uninstall** any installed game from the manager, with a **verified uninstall** (progress spinner + themed result bar). - **One source of truth for install state** — a game's installed / update-available status, cover art and metadata now stay in sync across its detail page, its download card and the store list. - New **"Default screen on launch"** setting so the app opens where you want it. ### 🛍️ Epic, GOG & Amazon stores - **Amazon Games** and **Epic Games** stores added — sign in, browse your owned library, and **download / install / launch** your games (including Epic's **free games**). - The existing **GOG** store is now wired into the Download Manager and behaves like the rest. - **All store pages restyled to Material 3** — every non-Steam store now matches the app theme and the Steam store's layout, with live download **%** on detail pages, cover art, launch fixes, a **themed container picker**, and **themed toasts** (fixing an unreadable black-box toast some ROMs showed). ### 🔒 Security & your accounts — please read Bannerlator's Steam / Epic / GOG / Amazon sign-ins are a **third-party login system, exactly like any other emulator or launcher that logs into these stores.** Bannerlator is **not affiliated with, authorised by, or endorsed by** Valve/Steam, Epic Games, GOG, or Amazon. You are **logging your real store account into a community app — use it at your own risk.** This release **actively redacts** your credentials and account identifiers from logs. A new `StoreLog.redactUrl` helper strips the sensitive parts out of everything the stores write — to **logcat *and* to the shareable diagnostic files** — including: - **signed download / manifest URLs** (Amazon, Epic, GOG CDN links carry access tokens in the query), - **OAuth authorization codes** in login redirects, - **GOG `client_secret` + `refresh_token`**, - and **account identity IDs** (Epic account ID, GOG user ID). Steam credentials were already redacted. Even so, **be careful about sharing any log or debug file publicly** — logs can still contain other diagnostic detail, so only share one if you're comfortable doing so. None of this changes how login, downloads or cloud saves actually work; it only changes what gets written to a log. ### 🔓 Steam QR sign-in re-enabled QR sign-in was temporarily greyed out while the ~1-hour session-drop issue was investigated. With the new logoff-recovery path in place, **QR login is back on** — a QR session now stores the same credentials as a password login and recovers the same way. An on-screen note advises: if downloads or the session keep dropping after a QR sign-in, sign out and use **username + password** (the more durable path).
Previously in 2.2.2 — the in-game ReShade release (already in your build if you're on 2.2.2) - **🎬 In-game ReShade effects** — run real ReShade `.fx` post-processing (colour grading, sharpen, film grain, CRT, tonemap, LUTs…) on **DXVK / VKD3D** games, compiled **on-device** via the bundled **[vkBasalt](https://github.com/DadSchoorse/vkBasalt)** layer. Configured **per game**, tuned **live** in a dedicated in-game ReShade tab with auto-generated typed controls, and run **solo or stacked**. An on-demand catalog of ~100 curated MIT/CC0 effects, or drop your own into the `ReShade/` folder. - **♻️ Existing containers auto-updated** their ReShade (vkBasalt) layer on next launch — no reinstall needed. - **🛟 In-game drawer rail now scrolls** so Exit is always reachable on short screens. - **🎛️ FPS limiter now sticks** between sessions for shortcut-launched games. - **🎨 Fixes** — white on-screen controls stay white, very light / dark custom accents stay readable, and container creation can no longer get stuck on a stale shortcut.
## 📥 Downloads | Variant | File | |---|---| | **Standard** | `Bannerlator-2.3-standard.apk` | | **PUBG** | `Bannerlator-2.3-pubg.apk` | | **Ludashi** | `Bannerlator-2.3-ludashi.apk` | > ℹ️ **Updating in-app?** This release is **entirely app-side** — the standard APK update covers everything in 2.3. There's **no ImageFS reinstall** required: your existing containers, themes, custom accent, per-game settings and control colours all carry over untouched. --- ## 🙏 2.3's storefronts are built on the work of others. The Steam store's connection client and its entire depot-download engine come from **[JavaSteam](https://github.com/Longi94/JavaSteam)** (maintainer **Longi94**, with the **javasteam-depotdownloader** fork by **joshuatam**). The optional Steam emulator patch is **[Goldberg Steam Emu](https://mr_goldberg.gitlab.io/goldberg_emulator/)** (Mr_Goldberg) and **gbe_fork** (Detanup01). The **4-tier download-speed model** and the **Steam session-hardening patterns** were ported/adapted from **[GameNative](https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative)** (utkarshdalal), with Steam-client patterns also referenced from **Pluvia**. Built on that foundation, the original engineering in 2.3 — the **cross-store Download Manager**, the **four storefront integrations**, the multi-week **Steam session-hardening** work, the **depot OOM fix**, the **Goldberg auto-patch** integration, the store **Material-3 restyle**, and the store-log **credential redaction** — is by **The412Banner**. Full lineage and credits are in the [README](https://github.com/The412Banner/Bannerlator#-credits). And thanks to everyone in the Discord and on GitHub who tested the store downloads, Goldberg tiers and session-recovery work and reported issues — your reports drove this release.

releases.name: Bannerlator 2.2.2
releases.body:
# Bannerlator 2.2.2 Run Windows apps and games on Android — no PC and no root required. **2.2.2's headline is in-game ReShade.** You can now run real ReShade `.fx` post-processing on your DirectX games, set up **per game**, tuned **live** in the in-game drawer, and **stacked** if you like. On top of that it lands a batch of fixes from GitHub and Discord: the **FPS limiter no longer resets** between sessions, on-screen controls finally honour a **white** colour, **container creation** can no longer get stuck, and very light / dark custom accents stay readable. This release is **entirely app-side** — **no ImageFS reinstall** — and your **existing containers refresh their ReShade layer automatically** on next launch, so AMOLED stays the default and updating won't change your look. ## ✨ What's New ### 🎬 In-game ReShade effects — new Run real ReShade `.fx` effects — colour grading, sharpen, film grain, CRT, tonemap, LUTs and more — on **DXVK / VKD3D** games, compiled **on-device** via the bundled **[vkBasalt](https://github.com/DadSchoorse/vkBasalt)** layer. - **Per-game setup.** Pick effects when editing a **container** or a **game shortcut**; your selection is saved with that game and restored every launch. - **On-demand catalog.** Browse and search a catalog of **~100 curated MIT / CC0 effects** and download only the ones you want — nothing is bundled into the APK. Or **drop your own** effect into the `ReShade/` folder (guide in the repo README). - **A dedicated in-game ReShade tab.** It reads each shader and auto-generates the *right* control for every parameter — **sliders, toggles, dropdowns and colour pickers** — with a **Reset-to-defaults** button. Toggle effects on/off and tune them **live**, no restart, and your in-game changes **persist per game** across quit → relaunch. - **Solo or stack.** Run a single effect, or layer several at once. > ⚠️ **Stacking multiple effects? Add them a few at a time.** Each ReShade effect compiles on-device and costs GPU, so **selecting too many at once can stop a game from starting** — you'll get a **flat / blank screen** instead of the game. If that happens, go back into the per-game **ReShade effect** settings and **uncheck the effects one at a time** (or the specific heavy one) until the game boots correctly, then add more gradually. > > *(Colour effects today; depth effects such as SSAO / depth-of-field aren't supported yet. ReShade only affects DirectX games running through DXVK / VKD3D.)* ### ♻️ Existing containers auto-update the ReShade layer Updating from an earlier version? Your **existing containers pick up the new ReShade (vkBasalt) layer automatically** on their next launch — you don't have to recreate a container or reinstall the ImageFS for ReShade to work. ### 🛟 In-game drawer rail now scrolls On shorter screens the left icon rail could push **Exit** off the bottom edge. The rail is now **scrollable**, so every control — including **Exit** — is always reachable. ### 🎛️ FPS limiter now sticks between sessions Toggling the in-game **FPS limiter** for a game launched from a shortcut is now **saved to that game** — it no longer reverts every time you close and reopen the game. *(Fixes the second half of #46.)* ### 🎨 Fixes - **White on-screen controls** — setting the virtual-control colour to **white** now stays white instead of falling back to blue. *(#46)* - **Readable light / dark accents** — a very **light** custom theme accent no longer paints the `+` / Play / badges white-on-white, and a very **dark** one no longer goes dark-on-dark; the glyph colour is derived from the accent automatically. **AMOLED and the built-in presets are byte-identical to before.** - **Container creation could get stuck** — after deleting a container that still had a leftover shortcut, creating a new container could silently fail and stay broken. The stale entry is now cleaned up and no longer blocks creation. *(#45)*
Previously in 2.2 — the themeable-interface overhaul (already in your build if you're on 2.2) - **🎨 A themeable interface, redesigned** — your selected theme recolours the **whole app *and* the in-game side drawer**, with a rebuilt drawer and accent-driven buttons. - **🌈 9 new colour presets (16 total)** — Midnight Cobalt, Phosphor, Carbon & Ember, Amethyst, Crimson, Synthwave, Royal Gold, Frost and Monochrome, plus a custom HSV accent picker. **AMOLED stays the default.** - **🕹️ Per-game on-screen control colours** — controls follow your theme, or take a per-game colour you set in the Controls editor. - **⭐ File Manager — Favorites / bookmarked folders** — pin the folders you open most and jump to them from a dedicated **★ Favorites** list, each entry tagged by storage source (Internal / SD / a container's Drive C: or Z:) and full path. - **🎮 Controller-binding screen rebuilt** in the modern UI — readable under any theme, cards, live button feedback. - **🧰 In-game Task Manager** — "New Task" works on the Vulkan / Native renderers, and processes show as cards.
## 📥 Downloads | Variant | File | |---|---| | **Standard** | `Bannerlator-2.2.2-standard.apk` | | **PUBG** | `Bannerlator-2.2.2-pubg.apk` | | **Ludashi** | `Bannerlator-2.2.2-ludashi.apk` | > ℹ️ **Updating in-app?** This release is **entirely app-side** — the standard APK update covers everything in 2.2.2. There's **no ImageFS reinstall** required: your existing containers refresh their ReShade layer on next launch, and your selected theme, custom accent and per-game control colours carry over untouched. --- 🙏 Thanks to everyone in the Discord and on GitHub who tested the ReShade work and reported the control-colour, container-creation and FPS-limiter issues — your reports drove this release.

releases.name: Bannerlator 2.2.1 Pre-release 1
releases.body: ## Changelog | Commit | Title | |--------|--------| | `5bb33db` | docs(log): in-game rail scroll fix + 2.2.1 pre-release 1 | | `8c6298a` | chore(release): bump to 2.2.1 (versionCode 36) for pre-release 1 | | `332bd04` | fix(ingame-drawer): make the left icon rail scrollable on short screens | | `9f51ed7` | docs(log): release 2.2 description rewrite + UI-rewrite (Compose vs XML/Java) explainer | | `6355d0e` | docs(README): correct control-colour toggle label to "Follow app theme" | | `2ccfda8` | release: bump to 2.2 (versionCode 35) + README for the UI overhaul, themes, Favorites | | `b55ac24` | docs: progress-log — UI rebuild merged to main + 9 new theme presets | | `5d75439` | feat(theme): add 9 opt-in presets + preset-index migration | | `35c8a28` | Merge feat/ui-rebuild: UI rebuild (drawers, theme centralization, P2-P4b, controls, File Manager Favorites) | | `f6088c4` | docs: progress-log — File Manager Favorites (code done, CI green) | ### 🩹 Fix - **In-game side menu:** the left icon rail now **scrolls** when the screen is too short to fit every button, so the **Exit / Pause / Task Manager** buttons at the bottom are always reachable (previously they could overflow off-screen and you could not close the game). When the rail does fit, it looks exactly as before. _Pre-release for testers — only offered to users who enable **Settings → Include pre-releases**. Built on top of 2.2._

releases.name: Bannerlator 2.2
releases.body: # Bannerlator 2.2 Run Windows apps and games on Android — no PC and no root required. **2.2 is a big visual overhaul.** The whole interface — and the **in-game side drawer** — now follow your chosen theme, with a redesigned drawer, **nine new colour presets**, and **per-game on-screen control colours**. It also adds **Favorites** to the File Manager and **rebuilds the controller-binding screen** in the modern UI, alongside a batch of readability and consistency fixes. This release is entirely app-side — **no ImageFS reinstall needed**, and **AMOLED stays the default**, so updating won't change your look unless you pick a new theme. ## ✨ What's New ### 🎨 A themeable interface — redesigned The app drawer and the **in-game side drawer** were rebuilt with new icons and accent-driven buttons, and — the big change — **your selected theme now recolours the entire app *and* the in-game drawer**. Previously large parts of the UI stayed blue regardless of theme; now your preset or custom accent reaches the screens, dialogs, drawer, chips, sliders and overlays. ### 🌈 9 new colour presets — total of 16 Nine new opt-in presets join the lineup, bringing the total to **16** (plus the custom HSV accent picker): - **Midnight Cobalt** · **Phosphor** · **Carbon & Ember** · **Amethyst** · **Crimson** · **Synthwave** · **Royal Gold** · **Frost** · **Monochrome** - alongside the existing **Classic Dark**, **AMOLED**, **Ocean**, **Forest**, **Sunset**, **Rose** and **Steel**. > **AMOLED stays the default and is unchanged.** Your previously selected theme and custom accent are preserved across the update — nothing about your look changes unless you choose a new one. ### 🕹️ Per-game on-screen control colours — new The on-screen touch controls follow your app theme by default, and you can now **override their colour per game**. Turn off **"Follow app theme"** in the Controls editor (in the in-game drawer or the out-of-game Input Controls page), pick a colour, and it's **saved with that game's profile** — so each game can keep its own control colour while the rest of the app stays on your theme. ### ⭐ File Manager — Favorites / bookmarked folders — new Pin the folders you open most and jump straight to them. A **★ button** opens a dedicated **Favorites** list, and each entry shows **exactly where it lives**: - a colour-coded badge for the storage source — **Internal**, **SD card**, **Drive C:**, or **Drive Z:**, - the **container name** for a container's Drive C:, so two games' `C:\Program Files` are never confused, - and the **full path**. Pin from a folder's **⋮ menu** or with **"Pin current folder"**; unpin with the filled ★. Favorites persist across launches, and entries for a deleted container quietly drop off. ### 🎮 Controller-binding screen rebuilt The external controller-binding screen was rebuilt in the modern UI: **labels are clearly readable under any theme**, each binding is a card matching the rest of the app, and **buttons you press while binding appear instantly**. (Builds on the 2.1.1 readability fix — bindings still **persist**, and **Ludashi-format profile import** still works.) ### 🧰 In-game Task Manager **"New Task" now works on the Vulkan / Native renderers** — the dialog used to be invisible over those surfaces — and running processes are now shown as **cards**, matching the File Manager rows. ### 🧹 Consistency & readability The **Games** and **Containers** lists now share one card style with consistent depth on every theme, and the legacy dropdowns, spinners, dialogs and section headers follow your accent too — with a **luminance floor** so text never goes dark-on-dark even on a very dark custom accent. ## 🧱 Under the hood — the UI rewrite, in plain terms A few people asked **what the "UI rewrite" actually is** and how much of the app it covers. Honest answer: **What kind of UI it is.** The interface is **Jetpack Compose** with **Material 3** — Kotlin, declarative, no XML layouts. The whole out-of-game app is a **single Activity** driving a **Navigation-Compose** graph (so the drawer destinations are Compose routes, not separate XML activities), wired up with **Hilt** for dependency injection. That's what makes a theme able to recolour everything at once: the colours come from one `MaterialTheme` the screens all read, instead of hundreds of hardcoded XML values. **What's actually converted.** The **entire out-of-game app is now Compose** — there are no XML screens left in it: - **Library & setup** — Containers, Container detail/editor, Games (shortcuts), Contents, Input Controls, Adrenotools GPU Drivers, Saves, **File Manager**, Settings, **Appearance**, and the Splash/setup flow. - **Storefronts** — GOG, Epic, Amazon and Steam (main, login, QR sign-in, library and game-detail screens) are all Compose. - **New in 2.2** — the **external controller-binding screen** was the last big out-of-game holdout on the old toolkit; it's now Compose too. That's roughly **240 Compose UI functions across ~90 Kotlin files**. **What's deliberately *not* Compose.** The **in-game experience** is still a **classic Java activity** (`XServerDisplayActivity`) — and it stays that way on purpose, because it hosts the **native render surface** (the `SurfaceView` / `GLSurfaceView` the X11 server, Vulkan/OpenGL renderer and Wine draw into). You can't render a game *inside* a Compose tree. What 2.2 did do is turn the in-game **side drawer** and its **dialogs** into **Compose "islands"** layered on top of that surface — which is why the in-game drawer now follows your theme and the redesigned controls/Task-Manager UI looks like the rest of the app. **So "how much is Compose vs old XML/Java?"** Measured by *screens*, the app you tap through is **essentially 100% Compose**. Measured by *lines of code* it looks like more Java than Kotlin — but that Java is overwhelmingly the **emulation engine**, not UI: the X11 server, the Vulkan/OpenGL renderer, box64 / FEXCore, the Wine handler, the input-controls engine and container management. None of that is "screens," and none of it was ever meant to be Compose. What genuinely *remains* as legacy XML/Java UI is now a short list — the **performance HUD overlay**, **Big Picture** mode, the **on-screen control-element editor**, a couple of file/folder pickers, and a few native over-the-game dialogs — and it keeps shrinking each release. ## 📥 Downloads | Variant | File | |---|---| | **Standard** | `Bannerlator-2.2-standard.apk` | | **PUBG** | `Bannerlator-2.2-pubg.apk` | | **Ludashi** | `Bannerlator-2.2-ludashi.apk` | > ℹ️ **Updating in-app?** This release is **entirely app-side** — the standard APK update covers everything in 2.2. There's **no ImageFS reinstall** and no container changes required, and your selected theme, custom accent and per-game control colours carry over untouched. --- 🙏 Thanks to everyone in the Discord and on GitHub who suggested the theme presets and the File Manager Favorites, and who reported the controller-binding and Task Manager rough edges.

releases.name: Bannerlator 2.1.1
releases.body: # Bannerlator 2.1.1 Run Windows apps and games on Android — no PC and no root required. **2.1.1 adds a major new feature — ReShade post-processing — plus a low-latency mode for the OpenGL renderer, and fixes two community-reported bugs.** You can now run real ReShade `.fx` effects on top of your DXVK / VKD3D games, compiled on-device and tunable live in the in-game drawer; the direct-scanout **Native Rendering** low-latency path now works on the **OpenGL** renderer too; and the **controller-binding screen** and **container Duplicate / Export** issues are fixed. ## ✨ What's New ### 🎨 ReShade post-processing — new Real ReShade `.fx` effects now run on top of your games — colour grading, sharpening, film grain, CRT looks, tonemapping and more — compiled **on-device** through a bundled [vkBasalt](https://github.com/DadSchoorse/vkBasalt) layer, so there's no PC step. - **On-demand catalog** — pick from a built-in list of **~100 curated, license-safe effects** right in the container / shortcut editor. Each effect downloads on tap (the app itself stays small), then fills in and is ready to use; downloaded effects work offline. - **Drop-in folder** — drop your own self-contained effect folders into `Android/data//files/ReShade/` and they appear in the picker alongside the catalog. - **Dedicated in-game ReShade tab** — each effect's controls are **auto-generated from the shader's own parameters**, with the right control type for each: **sliders**, **on/off toggles**, **dropdowns** and **colour pickers**. Toggle an effect **on/off live** and **tune every parameter while the game runs**, with a **Reset to defaults** button. #### 🧩 Bringing your own effects (drop-in) — step by step Want an effect that isn't in the catalog? Add it yourself: 1. **Open this folder on your device** (create the `ReShade` folder if it isn't there): `Android/data/com.winlator.banner/files/ReShade/` *(Standard build. PuBG = `com.tencent.ig`, Ludashi = `com.ludashi.benchmark`.)* 2. **Make one folder per effect**, named whatever you want it called in the menu (e.g. `MySepia`). 3. **Put all of the effect's files inside that folder, next to the `.fx`:** the `.fx` itself, **any `.fxh` files it `#include`s** (e.g. `ReShade.fxh`, `ReShadeUI.fxh`), and any **textures** it uses. ``` ReShade/ MySepia/ MySepia.fx ReShade.fxh ← copy in any .fxh the .fx #includes ReShadeUI.fxh ``` 4. **In the app**, edit a **container** or **game shortcut** → **ReShade effect** picker → your folder now appears, select it. 5. Launch a **DirectX (DXVK / VKD3D) game** → in-game drawer → **ReShade tab** to toggle it and tune sliders live. > **Not showing up / no effect?** Make sure it's in **its own subfolder** with a `.fx` inside; copy in **every `#include`d `.fxh`** and texture next to the `.fx`; and remember it's **colour effects on DirectX games only** (no depth/SSAO/DOF, no OpenGL/WineD3D titles). Can't find `Android/data`? Many stock file managers hide it on Android 11+ — use one that can open it, or copy the folder over from a PC via USB. > 🎮 ReShade runs through the bundled **vkBasalt** Vulkan layer, which compiles `.fx` → SPIR-V on-device and feeds Turnip — so it affects **Vulkan-backed (DXVK / VKD3D)** games on any host renderer. **Colour effects work today; depth effects (SSAO / DOF) are not included yet.** A few catalog effects may not compile on every device. ### ⚡ Native Rendering (Low-Latency Mode) on OpenGL — new The direct-scanout **Native Rendering** path — which skips the compositor blit to cut input lag — now works on the **OpenGL** renderer, not just Vulkan. It's a per-container toggle; like the Vulkan path it's mutually exclusive with that renderer's post-processing effects / scaling (they grey out while it's on, since it bypasses the compositor). ### 🎮 Controller binding screen now readable — fixed (#37) On the external-controller binding screen the dropdown menus rendered **dark text on a dark background**, leaving the bindings effectively invisible. The text is now clearly readable. This completes the controller-binding fixes (bindings now **persist**, and **Ludashi-format profile import** works). ### 📦 Container Duplicate / Export — fixed (#39) Duplicating a container — especially a downloaded **Proton 11** one — could **silently produce nothing**, or a copy that lost its game drive-letters, lost its settings, or wouldn't boot. This turned out to be **five separate bugs**: - drive-letter **symlinks were dropped** instead of recreated (so the copy couldn't find your games), - a single unreadable file **aborted the whole copy**, - only a handful of settings were copied (**graphics driver, frame-gen, etc. were lost**), - a **crash** when a referenced graphics driver was missing, - and the wine socket directory was copied in a way that **stopped the copy from booting**. Duplicate and export now copy a container faithfully — it **appears in the list, keeps its drive mappings and all settings, and boots**. A failed duplicate now shows a clear message instead of silently doing nothing. ## 📥 Downloads | Variant | File | |---|---| | **Standard** | `Bannerlator-2.1.1-standard.apk` | | **PUBG** | `Bannerlator-2.1.1-pubg.apk` | | **Ludashi** | `Bannerlator-2.1.1-ludashi.apk` | > ℹ️ **Updating in-app?** The standard APK update covers everything in this release. ReShade effects download on demand from the in-app catalog the first time you pick them. ## 🙏 Credits — ReShade / post-processing | Feature | Source | Author | License | |---|---|---|---| | **ReShade `.fx` compiler + post-processing layer** | [vkBasalt](https://github.com/DadSchoorse/vkBasalt) | **DadSchoorse** | zlib | | **vkBasalt → Winlator integration** | [Winlator (vkBasalt to shortcuts)](https://github.com/Pipetto-crypto/winlator) | **Pipetto-crypto** | — | | **Vulkan rendering line carrying it forward** | [Winlator-Ludashi](https://github.com/StevenMXZ/Winlator-Ludashi) | **StevenMXZ** | — | | **Bundled / catalog `.fx` effects** | [ReShade shaders](https://github.com/crosire/reshade-shaders) · [prod80](https://github.com/prod80/prod80-reshade-repository) · [FXShaders](https://github.com/luluco250/FXShaders) · fubax | crosire · prod80 · luluco250 · fubax | MIT / CC0 | Bannerlator's ReShade feature is a **continuation of DadSchoorse's vkBasalt** — the bundled layer is built from its source, patched for live on-device effect toggle and slider control. Only MIT / CC0 effects are bundled or offered in the catalog. --- 🙏 Thanks to everyone who reported the controller-binding (#37) and container-duplication (#39) issues.

releases.name: Bannerlator 2.1
releases.body: # Bannerlator 2.1 Run Windows apps and games on Android — no PC and no root required. **2.1 is a smoothness & polish release.** The headline is **VRR / refresh-rate matching** — your screen's refresh rate can now follow your in-game frame rate for smoother, less-torn, lower-power gameplay — joined by a new **NIS** upscaler and a **debanding** pass on the Vulkan compositor, plus two notable fixes: the in-game **Task Manager** now works on the Vulkan renderer, and the install progress bar no longer hangs at 98%. ## ✨ What's New ### 🏎️ Match refresh rate to FPS (VRR) — new Your display's refresh rate can now **track your frame rate** instead of staying locked at the panel's maximum: - **Auto (match FPS)** — one toggle that drives the panel refresh to follow whatever the game is running at (and your FPS cap). - **Manual refresh-rate slider** — pin a fixed rate that snaps to your panel's supported modes: **Off / 60 / 90 / 120 / 144 Hz**, with a live readout of the rate the display actually settled on. - Available in both the **in-game drawer** and the **container settings editor**; **greys out automatically** on displays without variable-refresh support. - Works across **all three host renderers** — Vulkan, OpenGL, and SurfaceFlinger. The payoff: smoother motion, less tearing, and lower power draw whenever a game runs below your panel's max refresh. > 🎮 **Handheld note (AYANEO / AYASpace and similar):** set the system **Refresh Rate** to **Auto** in the device's own settings so the app is allowed to drive the panel — a fixed system rate will override it. ### 🟩 NIS upscaler — new scaling mode **NVIDIA Image Scaling (NVScaler)** joins **SGSR** and **FSR** in the in-game **Scaling mode** picker on the Vulkan renderer — a single-pass, aspect-fit upscaler with its own **sharpness** slider, for another option when a game renders below display resolution. ### 🎨 Debanding — smoother gradients A new **debanding** pass on the Vulkan compositor dithers away the visible "stripes" in smooth gradients, skies, and dark scenes on 8-bit output. Switch it on with an adjustable **strength** slider; it runs as the very last step before the image reaches the screen, with no meaningful performance cost. ### 🩹 In-game Task Manager fixed on Vulkan / ASR The in-game drawer's **Task Manager** — **End Process** and **Bring to Front** — was dead on the **default Vulkan renderer** (and the SurfaceFlinger / ASR path) and only worked on OpenGL. Both actions now work on every renderer, so you can kill a hung game from the drawer again. ### 🩹 Install progress no longer hangs at 98% On a fresh first-launch setup — and on **Settings → Reinstall ImageFS** — the progress bar used to **freeze at 98%** while the **Proceed** button was already showing, leaving people unsure whether to keep waiting. It now correctly reaches **100% / Installation complete** at the true end of setup. ### 🧪 Updated AIO Graphics Test (v1.6.1) New containers ship with **AIO Graphics Test v1.6.1**, adding dedicated **banding** and **scaling** test scenes for checking the debander and the upscalers on your own device. ## 📥 Downloads | Variant | File | |---|---| | **Standard** | `Bannerlator-2.1-standard.apk` | | **PUBG** | `Bannerlator-2.1-pubg.apk` | | **Ludashi** | `Bannerlator-2.1-ludashi.apk` | > ℹ️ **Updating in-app?** The standard APK update covers everything in this release. **NIS** is NVIDIA's open-source Image Scaling (MIT); **debanding** uses a terminal dither pass before the 8-bit swapchain; **VRR** votes the display refresh rate through Android's `Surface.setFrameRate` / `SurfaceControl` APIs. ## 💬 New: Ask the AI about Bannerlator You can now ask questions about how Bannerlator works directly on GitHub — an AI reads the actual source code and answers with file references. Just open a new issue and you'll get a reply within a couple of minutes. *(This is a repo/community feature; it isn't part of the app.)* --- 🙏 Thanks to everyone who reported the Task Manager and install-progress issues.

releases.name: Bannerlator 2.0
releases.body: # Bannerlator 2.0 Run Windows apps and games on Android — no PC and no root required. The graphics-enhancement program is now **complete across both renderers**, alongside a redesigned game/container UI, a few quality-of-life features, and broader device support. 1.9.2 brought real spatial upscaling and the full effect chain to the **Vulkan** renderer; **2.0 brings the same upscaler suite to the OpenGL renderer**, retunes every sharpness slider to a clean 0→max range, fixes the in-game magnifier, redesigns the game and container cards, auto-closes a session when the game quits, adds a FEXCore TSO preset, and — for older phones — **adds Turnip support on Android 10 / pre-11 devices**. ## ✨ What's New ### 🖼️ OpenGL renderer upscalers — full parity with Vulkan The same **Scaling mode** picker is now live in the in-game drawer on the **OpenGL** renderer too: - **SGSR** — Snapdragon Game Super Resolution 1.0 (edge-adaptive) - **FSR** / **FSR-Fit** — AMD FidelityFX Super Resolution 1.0 (EASU + RCAS), fill or aspect-preserving - **Sharpen** — RCAS-only sharpening, plus **Linear** / **Nearest** and a **Sharpness** slider The OpenGL path renders the scene at a reduced internal resolution and reconstructs it back up — so you get a sharper image *and* some performance headroom on OpenGL, not just Vulkan. ### 🎚️ Retuned sharpness sliders (both renderers) Every sharpness slider now spans a clean, useful range — **0 = off (no sharpening at all)** through **100 = maximum**: - **SGSR** sharpness range **doubled** for a much stronger effect at the top end. - **FSR** / **Sharpen** now pass through completely untouched at 0 (previously they always added a little sharpening). - **Sharpen** snaps to **5 clear steps** (0 / 25 / 50 / 75 / 100), matching its five discrete sharpening levels — no more dead slider travel. ### 🛠️ OpenGL renderer plumbing The **filter mode** (Nearest / Linear) is now a real, live setting on the OpenGL renderer — the in-game cursor stays sharp under Nearest — and trivial copy stages use a faster `glBlitFramebuffer` path. ### 🃏 Redesigned game & container cards The **Games** list and **Containers** list now share a cleaner, consistent card design: a primary row of chips (renderer · DXVK · frame-gen) over a muted secondary line (driver · VKD3D · backend), with the resolution in the subtitle. This also fixes long driver / component names crowding out or blanking the game title and pushing the ⋮ menu off-screen. ### 🚪 Auto-close on game exit When you launch a game from the library or a shortcut, the Wine session now **closes itself once the game quits** — no more black Wine desktop left behind. There's a per-container **"Close when game exits"** toggle (on by default) and a per-shortcut override. ### 🔍 Magnifier fixed The in-game **Magnifier** (zoom) overlay now works on the **default Vulkan renderer** (and the others). Previously its **+ / −** buttons did nothing unless you happened to be on the OpenGL renderer. ### ⚙️ FEXCore "Performance (TSO)" preset (new) A new built-in FEXCore preset: **Performance** settings with **Total Store Ordering** enabled — for the many games that need correct memory ordering without paying the full TSO cost. It appears alongside the existing presets in the FEXCore preset list. ### 📱 Turnip on Android 10 / older devices (new) A new **`turnip-26.1.0`** graphics-driver option loads Mesa Turnip as a **direct system Vulkan ICD** instead of through the adrenotools hook, so it works on **Android versions below 11** (e.g. Snapdragon 845 / Adreno 630) where the hook-based drivers silently fall back to the system driver. Pick it from the container's **Graphics Driver** list. The existing adrenotools drivers are unchanged — this is simply an additional option. ## ⚠️ Updating from an older version — please reinstall imageFS The new **`turnip-26.1.0`** driver is installed into **imageFS**, so a normal app update won't add it on its own. To get it: **Open the app's Settings → scroll to the bottom → Reinstall ImageFS.** A **fresh** install of 2.0 already includes it — no action needed. ## 📥 Downloads | Variant | File | |---|---| | **Standard** | `Bannerlator-2.0-standard.apk` | | **PUBG** | `Bannerlator-2.0-pubg.apk` | | **Ludashi** | `Bannerlator-2.0-ludashi.apk` | ## 🙏 Credits — graphics & drivers | Feature | Source | Author | License | |---|---|---|---| | **SGSR** upscaler | [snapdragon-gsr](https://github.com/SnapdragonStudios/snapdragon-gsr) | Qualcomm / Snapdragon Game Studios | BSD-3-Clause | | **FSR** EASU + RCAS, **Sharpen** | [FidelityFX-FSR](https://github.com/GPUOpen-Effects/FidelityFX-FSR) | AMD GPUOpen | MIT | | **Turnip 26.1.0** (Android-10 direct-ICD driver) | [Mesa / Freedreno](https://gitlab.freedesktop.org/mesa/mesa) — packaged in the direct-ICD form **[Winlator](https://github.com/brunodev85/winlator)** ships | Freedreno team @ Mesa · brunodev85 | MIT | The OpenGL upscalers reuse the same SGSR (BSD-3, Qualcomm) and FSR / CAS (MIT, AMD GPUOpen) shader code as the Vulkan path, ported to the GLES effect pipeline, with the upstream license headers retained.

releases.name: Bannerlator 1.9.2
releases.body:
# Bannerlator 1.9.2 Run Windows apps and games on Android — no PC and no root required. This release brings a **full graphics-enhancement suite to the default Vulkan renderer**. Previously the Vulkan path had only basic sampler scaling and no post-processing — every screen effect was limited to the OpenGL renderer. Now the Vulkan compositor gets **real spatial upscaling (SGSR + FSR1, both new)** *and* the **full screen-effect chain** (CAS, fake-HDR, FXAA, Toon, Color, CRT, NTSC). Credit where due: FSR1-on-the-Vulkan-compositor was pioneered by [GameNative](https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative) (the blueprint we built on), and SGSR appears on the Vulkan path of other Pluvia-based forks too. What Bannerlator adds is bringing **both** spatial upscalers — SGSR *and* FSR1 — together on the default path, alongside the effect chain. ## ✨ What's New ### 🖼️ Vulkan upscalers (new) — live in the in-game drawer Pick a **Scaling mode** live from the in-game drawer; it engages whenever a game renders below your screen resolution: - **SGSR** — Snapdragon Game Super Resolution 1.0 (single-pass, edge-adaptive) - **FSR** / **FSR-Fit** — AMD FidelityFX Super Resolution 1.0 (EASU + RCAS), fill or aspect-preserving - **Sharpen** — RCAS-only sharpening at native resolution - plus **Linear** / **Nearest** and an adjustable **Sharpness** slider ### 🔍 Supersampling (new) — set before launch - **Render scale** (Off / 1.25× / 1.5× / 2×) — a **container / per-game shortcut** setting (chosen *before* launch, since it changes the render resolution). The game renders *above* your screen resolution and the Vulkan compositor downsamples it with a Lanczos-2 filter — DSR / OGSSAA-style supersampling anti-aliasing. This is the opposite direction to the upscalers above. ### 🎨 Vulkan post-processing effects (new) — live in the in-game drawer Composable toggles and sliders in the in-game drawer, layered on top of any scaling mode: - **CAS** — AMD Contrast-Adaptive Sharpening, with a sharpness slider - **Fake HDR** — bloom + tone boost - **FXAA**, **Toon**, **CRT**, **NTSC** - **Color** grading — brightness / contrast / gamma ### ⚙️ Behaviour - **Native Rendering** and the post-processing presets are now **mutually exclusive** — enabling a preset turns Native off (Native does a direct scanout that bypasses the compositor), and enabling Native resets the presets, so the drawer never shows controls that silently do nothing. - **Linear** is now the default scaling mode. ### 🐛 Fixes - **On-screen controls opacity**: at low Overlay Opacity the filled "key" buttons (**MRB / BKSP / SPACE / ENTER**) kept a solid blue background while everything else faded out — the drop-shadow glow ignored the opacity setting. Fixed: the shadow now tracks opacity, so 0% truly hides every control. - Fixed an invisible black-on-black **"Native Rendering"** toast. ## 📥 Downloads | Variant | File | |---|---| | **Standard** | `Bannerlator-1.9.2-standard.apk` | | **PUBG** | `Bannerlator-1.9.2-pubg.apk` | | **Ludashi** | `Bannerlator-1.9.2-ludashi.apk` | ## 🙏 Credits — graphics enhancements The new Vulkan upscalers and effects port or reimplement the open-source work below. All sources are GPL-3.0-compatible, and the upstream license headers are kept inline in each shader at `app/src/main/cpp/winlator/*.frag`. | Feature | Source | Author | License | |---|---|---|---| | **SGSR** upscaler | [snapdragon-gsr](https://github.com/SnapdragonStudios/snapdragon-gsr) — `sgsr/v1/.../sgsr1_shader_mobile.frag` | Qualcomm / Snapdragon Game Studios | BSD-3-Clause | | **FSR** EASU + RCAS, **Sharpen** | [FidelityFX-FSR](https://github.com/GPUOpen-Effects/FidelityFX-FSR) — `ffx-fsr/ffx_fsr1.h` | AMD GPUOpen | MIT | | **CAS** sharpening | [FidelityFX-CAS](https://github.com/GPUOpen-Effects/FidelityFX-CAS) | AMD GPUOpen | MIT | | **FSR-Fit** + compositor present-slot blueprint | [GameNative](https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative) | utkarshdalal & GameNative | GPL-3.0 — *approach reimplemented, not copied* | | **Fake HDR · FXAA · Toon · CRT · NTSC · Color** | Bannerlator's existing OpenGL GLES2 effect set (inherited from the upstream Winlator-Ludashi renderer), ported to the Vulkan post chain | — | — | - **SGSR** (BSD-3) and **FSR / CAS** (MIT) shader code is bundled with the full upstream license headers retained. - The **GameNative** integration *shape* — FSR running in the compositor's present slot, plus the FSR-Fit aspect mode — was reimplemented from its approach, **not** copied verbatim (GameNative is GPL-3.0). General project credits (Winlator / Box64 / Wine / DXVK / Turnip / etc.) are in the [README](https://github.com/The412Banner/Bannerlator#-credits). ---
Changelog (commits since 1.9.1) | Commit | Title | |--------|--------| | `84b6bc1` | release: bump to 1.9.2 (versionCode 31) | | `1d9439e` | fix(controls): fade GameHub button drop-shadow with overlay opacity | | `1c9c576` | fix(vulkan): black-box native toast + default scaling mode to Linear | | `3ed78bb` | feat(vulkan): mutually exclude Native Rendering and post-pass presets | | `77c6b76` | fix(vulkan): scaling modes dropped the screen-effect chain | | `5dfcdbf` | feat(vulkan): Phase 2 — port 5 GL screen effects to the Vulkan post chain | | `4fecbc6` | feat(vulkan): composable CAS + fake-HDR post effects + real upscaler sharpness | | `0655d61` | fix(vulkan): enable Sharpen mode 6 + drop duplicate container filter-mode dropdown | | `c3cbe49` | feat(vulkan): sharpen-only mode + supersampling (render scale) downscale | | `28ab22d` | feat(drawer): show only the active renderer's graphics controls | | `5f5a4a0` | feat(vulkan): SGSR + FSR1 spatial upscaler on the Vulkan compositor |

releases.name: Bannerlator 1.9.1
releases.body:
# Bannerlator 1.9.1 Run Windows apps and games on Android — no PC and no root required. > 🩹 **This is a hotfix** — a small patch on top of [**1.9**](https://github.com/The412Banner/Bannerlator/releases/tag/1.9) addressing the multi-flavor install conflict below. No new features; everything in 1.9 (the SurfaceFlinger renderer and DXVK 3.0 / Vulkan 1.4 support) still applies. ## ✨ What's New ### 🐛 Fixes - **Multi-flavor install conflict**: the **PUBG** and **Ludashi** builds could fail to install alongside the **Standard** build with a *package conflict* error (`INSTALL_FAILED_CONFLICTING_PROVIDER`). The app's content-provider (FileProvider / shared-storage) authorities were hardcoded to one shared name across all three flavors; they are now keyed per-flavor, so each variant installs side-by-side without colliding. - **Save sharing**: sharing an exported save archive used a stale provider authority that didn't exist and would throw — it now resolves correctly on every flavor. - **Perf HUD** now renders correctly under the **SurfaceFlinger** and **Vulkan** host renderers. ## 📥 Downloads | Variant | File | |---|---| | **Standard** | `Bannerlator-1.9.1-standard.apk` | | **PUBG** | `Bannerlator-1.9.1-pubg.apk` | | **Ludashi** | `Bannerlator-1.9.1-ludashi.apk` | ## 🙏 Credits **Project & upstream** - **[brunodev85](https://github.com/brunodev85/winlator)** — original Winlator (Wine + Box64 + Turnip on Android), the foundation of this fork. - **[coffincolors](https://github.com/coffincolors/winlator)** — the `cmod` Winlator fork this codebase builds on. - **[Pipetto-crypto](https://github.com/Pipetto-crypto/winlator)** — Winlator Bionic + Box64 fix branch (the "Bionic" half of Star Bionic). - **[jacojayy](https://github.com/jacojayy/star)** & the **[star-emu](https://github.com/star-emu)** team — the Star Bionic / Winlator Frost lines this build continues, plus Turnip Timeline-Semaphore patches. - **[StevenMXZ](https://github.com/StevenMXZ/Winlator-Ludashi)** (Winlator-Ludashi) — the **Vulkan rendering** path and the bundled **Winlator File Manager**. - **[isygold](https://github.com/isygold/vegas-releases)** — Star Engine / VEGAS DXVK fork. 📖 **[VEGAS DXVK FAQ](https://htmlpreview.github.io/?https://github.com/The412Banner/Bannerlator/blob/main/docs/vegas_faq.html)** · 🚀 **Support VEGAS Development** — low-level graphics dev & vibecoder: debugging, refactoring & improving original DXVK code for Adreno. **[❤️ Sponsor →](https://github.com/sponsors/isygold)** - Frame generation: **[xXJSONDeruloXx](https://github.com/xXJSONDeruloXx/bionic-fg)** (bionic-fg) · **[PancakeTAS](https://github.com/PancakeTAS/lsfg-vk)** (lsfg-vk) · **[FrankBarretta](https://github.com/FrankBarretta/lsfg-vk-android)** (Android port). `Lossless.dll` is user-supplied ([Lossless Scaling](https://store.steampowered.com/app/993090/Lossless_Scaling/) by THS). -
