Deleting a memory card now recreates a fresh empty card in that slot instead of leaving it empty.

The settings menu now remembers the tab you were on and keeps sections expanded/collapsed the way you left them, instead of snapping back to the first tab every time you reopen it.

Custom analog-stick mapping is now more reliable, pick a target (any PS2 button including the D-pad, a hotkey, or Analog) directly from a list, instead of having to press a physical button. You can now map a stick direction to a button you've unbound, and every direction has a visible Clear button.

releases.name: ARMSX2 Refresh V28
releases.body:
Added on-screen Save State / Load State buttons (opt-in through the Touch Layout editor), tap the save/load button to open the slot picker and choose which slot to save/load from.

Tap-to-hold (latch) for on-screen face/shoulder buttons, tap once to hold (R1 to crouch), tap again to release, enable per-button in the touch layout editor.

Audio volume slider has been increased up to 150% for quiet games (above 100% can clip).

Multi-touch is now on by default, fixed not being able to move the analog stick and press buttons (or multiple buttons) at the same time. Toggle to turn it off still in the Touch Layout editor.

Pressure-modifier button hidden by default, and the Performance / Renderer / Pad settings tabs now start collapsed for a cleaner layout.

Settings Reset is now per-tab: Reset Tab clears only the current tab, Reset All / Reset Game does the full reset.

Macro-config dialog now uses the dark theme.

Touch stick + button now register at the same time (like move and jump together).

Clearing a D-pad or trigger (L2/R2) binding now takes effect in-game (previously only face buttons honored an unbind).

Single-button hotkeys now bind reliably on controllers that report two key codes per press.

releases.name: ARMSX2 Refresh V27
releases.body:
Performance updates to the EE recompiler (helps every device, especially weaker CPUs). 

1. DMA-wait spin elimination (BC0/CPCOND0): the EE now natively compiles the COP0 branch games use to busy-wait on DMA, and idle-skips the spin entirely. Was the single biggest EE cost in DMA-heavy games. (7M interpreter fallbacks before, now 0).

2. Multiply-accumulate native codegen (MADD family): MADD/MADDU/MADD1/MADDU1 + the pipeline-1 HI/LO moves (MFHI1/MTHI1/MFLO1/MTLO1) now compile natively instead of single-stepping. (2M interpreter fallbacks before, now 0). 

3. Interrupt-gating native codegen (EI/ERET): enable-interrupts and exception-return now compile as block terminators instead of single-stepping, notably faster boot/menu/loading across all games.

Performance updates for GS (OpenGL, all GPUs including Mali). 

OpenGL shader-cache fix: stopped per-frame shader recompilation (a selector-equality bug). Large GS-time reduction on the OpenGL-HW renderer, reduced GS usage from 90% to low 20% in Jackie Chan Adventures.

Arabic / RTL on-screen controls no longer mirrored. In a right-to-left system language the touch controls were flipped left-right, now forced to absolute layout.

You can now unbind a pad button, the "Clear" button on each Pad binding leaves a button blank, freeing that physical button to be assigned as a hotkey.

Added an on-screen fast-forward button, an optional fast forward touch button that toggles Turbo. This button is opt-in enable, you can enable and position it in the touch-layout editor (it's off by default, so existing layouts are unchanged).

Added Touch and physical controller Macro/combo buttons (M1–M4), one tap fires multiple buttons together (R1+R2+R3 for example), configure in Pad settings.

The Pad, Performance, and Renderer settings tabs now group their options under expand/collapse headers (like the Fixes tab), so they're much easier to scan instead of one long wall of settings.

releases.name: ARMSX2 Refresh V26
releases.body:
Valkyrie Profile 2: Silmeria now boots (was a long-standing black screen). Game-specific fixes from the GameDB are now on by default (matching desktop PCSX2), so the VU fix VP2 needs is applied. This also un-breaks any game that relies on that GameDB fix.

Skip MPEG now works, the FMV-skip hack was missing from the Android (ARM64) recompiler, now ported over.

For God of War II, walls no longer go transparent on the OpenGL renderer (fixed a GLES depth-precision bug, also sharpens depth for all games on OpenGL).

VP2 intro cutscene renders correctly when upscaling, GOW2, and LEGO Batman upscales cleanly both out of the box, no need to head to the fixes tab for these.

Audio now self-heals if the audio device is busy at launch (no more "no sound until I restart my device" if your audio device bugs out).

Added Stick Invert X / Invert Y / Swap X-Y (for pads with mirrored/rotated sticks).

Added Stick Anti-Deadzone, helps fix games that ignore the stick until ~45% then jump (Cold Fear, Area 51).

Bind stick directions to hotkeys or other pad mappings (Quick Save/Load, etc.).

Smoother analog triggers on non-Xbox pads (less jitter, full range).

Rumble / Vibration on-off toggle.

Added correct region flags for India / China / Korea / Hong Kong releases (reads the GameDB now, not just the serial).

Add a game to your home screen as a launcher shortcut, press and hold a game (or on controller Y/triangle), navigate to the Info section of settings (last tab in per-game settings) ---> Add to Home Screen button). (Let me know if this is not working). 

Added collapsible sections in the Fixes tab to reduce clutter.

Added the Gabominated patch collection as a 2nd source in the in-app import patches browser.

releases.name: ARMSX2 Refresh V25
releases.body:
Added faster anisotropic filtering from PCSX2, optimized AF shader (early isotropic fallback, cheaper math). Port of PCSX2 [https://github.com/PCSX2/pcsx2/pull/14465]
(https://github.com/PCSX2/pcsx2/pull/14465).

Added ROV (Rasterizer Ordered View) toggle for HW accurate blending in renderer settings, port of PCSX2 https://github.com/PCSX2/pcsx2/pull/14555. Exposed in Renderer for benchmarking mainly, it's Vulkan-only and applies live. Honestly it isn't as performant on mobile as it is on PC from my testing, and some extensions ROV needs are not present on Turnip. I didn't see gains on mobile, added for parity and benchmarking purposes.

Added "Accurate blending fast path (experimental)" in renderer settings, the mobile-native take on accurate blending via the Vulkan tile-memory framebuffer-fetch path, usually faster than ROV on a tiler. Vulkan only, restart to apply (Mali already uses it by default, so the toggle is for Adreno users only). Some drivers show artifacts with this setting, so be sure to test and benchmark your games.

Added Accurate Alpha Test toggle in renderer settings, applies live.

Added HW AA1 edge anti-aliasing toggle in renderer settings (applies on game restart).

GameDB now flags games that recommend Accurate Alpha Test / AA1 on-screen.

Higher internal resolution settings, upscaling now goes up to 8x (was 5x). Time to fry your GPU!

Rumble fallback to the device's built-in vibration motor for controllers with no rumble actuator (e.g. Odin 3), issue https://github.com/ARMSX2/ARMSX2/issues/241.

Controller skin import now supports the newer per-side analog filenames (analog_stick_left/right), older packs still work.

Renamed "Game Fixes" to "GameDB Fixes" for clarity.

Ported an upstream PCSX2 texture-cache Move correctness fix (https://github.com/ARMSX2/ARMSX2/commit/a6b1040caf9dd3c825a1894a36534212b59894f3).

releases.name: ARMSX2 Refresh V24
releases.body:
Added Custom controller skins, import your own on-screen button art from a folder or .zip (iOS skins done by Bagas work directly), and switch between them in a new Skins tab. Two built-in NetherSX2 skins have been added (NetherSX2 and NetherSX2 Old button skins, thanks @Trixarian).

Added Floating on-screen joystick in the Touch Layout editor, an optional mode where the analog stick re-centers under your thumb wherever you press down, instead of a fixed spot.

Added a Stick outer / anti-dead zone slider so short-throw sticks (Odin and similar handhelds) hit full tilt before the physical edge.

You can now edit the touch layout from the main menu, reach the touch layout editor from _Settings ---> Pad ---> Edit On-Screen Touch Layout_ without needing a game running. 

You can now Show/hide individual on-screen buttons in the Touch Layout Editor, toggle any button in or out per layout (hidden ones are greyed + crossed out while editing, and will not appear in game). Unhide them at any time. 

Added a confirmation popup before disabling Hardcore, turning Hardcore off now asks first, so you don't disable it by accident.

Added Mr Purple's driver repo (purple-turnip) as a second in-app driver download source alongside K11MCH1.

For Ace Combat 5 / Ace Combat Zero an automatic GameDB fix (partial texture preloading) to cut FMV/hash-cache stutter has been added.

releases.name: ARMSX2 macOS 2.5.2
releases.body:
- implemented MetalFX

Enjoy upscaled games!

releases.name: ARMSX2 macOS 2.5.1
releases.body:
This is a minor update but it's recommended to all users.

- Fixed a metal render issue that was leading to a crash in games like `Bloody Roar 3`

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/ARMSX2/ARMSX2/compare/MacOSv2.5.0...MacOSv2.5.1

releases.name: ARMSX2 Refresh V23
releases.body:
Added support for a second controller, so you can now play local co-op 2 player games with a friend, as well as per-player mapping in the Pad section of settings. 

First controller to press in-game = Player 1, the next distinct controller = Player 2 (auto-assigned, re-paired each game launch). Port-aware native pad input (setPadButtonForPort) routes each controller to its own PS2 port, per-port D-pad state so the two pads can't release each other's directions.

Per-player button mapping: button binds, stick modes, and custom stick directions are now per-player. Pad settings has a Player 1 / Player 2 selector, "Reset" is also per-player. Player 1 keeps your exact existing binds (P2 uses defaults until set).
Player 2 is enabled at game boot when a second controller is connected beforehand, second controller has rumble support as well (except DualSense, only works wired on a pc). 

Added NTSC / PAL per-region frame rate caps (NetherSX2-style, thanks @Trixarian for the help) as an alternative to the Max Display FPS cap setting. Separate Frame Rate (Hz) sliders for NTSC and PAL games. 

Added a List view as an alternative to the shelf view.

Added manual Rows control with cover-size adjustment (toggled from the top right buttons ---> More ---> Rows Auto (can be toggled to 2, 3, 4, or 5, and back to auto, depending on how zoomed out you'd like the shelves and covers to be. 

Expanded the setup wizard, after clicking the Let's Go button on initial setup, you will now see a new screen with optional recommended settings: aspect ratio, internal resolution, anti-blur, widescreen, deinterlacing, and an Optimal/Fast preset.
Renderer choice (OpenGL / Vulkan) + custom GPU driver browser in setup.

New Stick Deadzone, Sensitivity, and Acceleration sliders in the Pad section of settings. 

Added UI scaling per screen in the Overlay section of settings. Independent UI Size (borders) and UI Font Size sliders to fit 21:9 / 16:10 / 4:3 / handheld screens, scales the library + menus only. 

Added a "Reset global to defaults" (global) and "Reset this game to global" (per-game), with a confirmation tap. 

ARM64 recompiler performance improvements and game fixes (ported from @isztldav macOS branch)

Faster, more faithful EE recompilation, MIPS trap opcodes now use native block-conditional codegen, and SYNC/CACHE are handled inline instead of breaking blocks apart.

Fixed an after-BIOS hang by porting the proper interrupt-disable delay (games like Jak X now boot past it).

Squashed an MMI lane-permute codegen bug that could abort compilation outright.
Synced with upstream PCSX2:

Graphics fixes across HW/DX11/DX12/GL, AA1 rendering, DATE setup, depth feedback, and ROV reporting.

GameDB updates, and assorted stability fixes.

Import File for memory cards no longer renames or forces Slot 1 on a second import (unique name, no forced slot).

A deleted card no longer reappears at BIOS boot (case-insensitive slot match).

Added a Per-card Delete button and confirmation, added up/down/left/right controller navigation for memory card settings. 

Speed Limit % max raised to 1000, will only push as fast as your device can go, same as the Frame Limit off button. 

The cap/FPS stats line ("Display: 25 (cap 30)") is now hidden when the OSD is off for the Max FPS Display cap setting.

HostFS functionality has been added back, you can find the toggle to enable it in the Patches section of settings. (Let me know if you still have any issues). 

Added a per-player Test rumble button (Pad tab) that reports whether Android can drive that controller's rumble.

releases.name: ARMSX2 Refresh V22
releases.body:
Added support for custom game covers, set your own artwork for any game. Long-press a game ---> Info Section (Last tab in settings) ---> Set Custom Cover, or drop images into the covers/custom folder named after the game. Make sure to remove the previous custom cover first before changing it to another. 

Added a new hotkey for Fast Forward (Toggle) for controllers, press once to lock fast-forward on, press again to turn it off, an alternative to having to hold the button through long JRPG grinds. The original Fast Forward (Hold) hotkey is also still an option. 

Per-game Renderer settings no longer bleed through to other games. Your choices made in global settings will carry over automatically.

Added SPU2 SIMD audio (experimental) setting in the Audio section of settings, an optional NEON fast path for sound processing that can free up CPU on lower-end devices. Off by default; toggle it under Audio settings. Credit to @Phantomnameless for bringing the feature up to me.

Redesigned the library toolbar: the top-right buttons are now clean icons with labels underneath (Scan, BIOS, Memory Cards, Cover Art, plus a ⋮ More menu for the rest), replacing the cramped text buttons.

For Memory Cards, the manager now clearly shows which card is loaded in each slot (highlight + a check mark).

Cleaner pause menu: the exit (x) button is now red, and the green play button no longer shows while you're already in-game. Long-press a game in the game library and press the play button there, an alternative to launching games from the cover (touch only).

releases.name: ARMSX2 macOS 2.5.0
releases.body:
Another step forward for native ARM64 PlayStation 2 emulation on Apple Silicon! This build folds in the latest upstream PCSX2 improvements and continues sharpening the macOS ARM64 recompiler.

What's new on the ARM64 side:
- Faster, more faithful EE recompilation — MIPS trap opcodes now use native block-conditional codegen, and SYNC/CACHE are handled inline instead of breaking blocks apart.
- Fixed an after-BIOS hang by porting the proper interrupt-disable delay (games like Jak X now boot past it).
- Squashed an MMI lane-permute codegen bug that could abort compilation outright.

Synced with upstream PCSX2:
- Graphics fixes across HW/DX11/DX12/GL — AA1 rendering, DATE setup, depth feedback, and ROV reporting.
- A nicer UI — memory card sorting, smoother game-list backgrounds, and a Big Picture controller-navigation fix.
- GameDB updates, debugger/recompiler tooling improvements, and assorted stability fixes.

Grab the build, fire up your favorite game, and enjoy — and as always, if something misbehaves, bug reports and logs are hugely appreciated. 🎮

Happy gaming! ✨

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/ARMSX2/ARMSX2/compare/MacOSv2.4.0...MacOSv2.5.0

releases.name: ARMSX2 Refresh V20
releases.body:
Custom data folder is back for Github Builds (APKs). With All-Files Access you can now keep your memory cards, save states, and configs in any folder you choose, for data that survives an uninstall and you don't need to manually back up, and your games load correctly from it. Pick it in setup: App Data Folder ---> Custom Folder.
Google Play builds are unchanged, they stay on scoped storage (no Custom Folder), for policy compliance.

The pause overlay/settings menu will now automatically open if the app goes out of focus, (like your device falling asleep, or when changing to another app without closing ARMSX2), so resuming is now simpler. 

The gear icon now remains available when setting on-screen controls to never in Pad section of settings. Tap the top middle of the screen to reveal it as normal. 

CRC now shows in the Game Properties Info tab and can be copied like the rest.

Rumble small-motor fix: light rumble no longer comes through at full strength on single-motor controllers.

You can now map the left and right stick to D-Pad or Face buttons on controller.

Time Played / Last Played is now tracked per game, shown in the Info Tab (last section of settings). 

You can now map the Analog button on physical controllers in the Pad section of settings. Fixes games that need it to enable the sticks (like Driving Emotion Type-S)

Custom Speed Limit % and Display FPS Cap sliders (reverted frame rate control name), you can now pick any value instead of fixed presets, per game, for fine-tuning smoothness. Instead of presets like 60, 30, 20, 15, you can now choose any value from 1-60. For emulation speed, instead of presets like 100, 150, 200, you can now choose any value from 10% to 200%. Turning off the Frame Limit button in the Play section of settings will bypass this slider, and push the emulator as fast as your device can handle. 

Mali GPUs: set Renderer ---> GPU Profile ---> Adreno for more speed (V16-level) on heavy games like GT4, at the cost of possible blending glitches, keep Auto or Mali for correct graphics but slower speeds in some games. Testers reported DBZ and Black were fine with the Mali profile, but games like GT4 were slower. I do not have a Mali device to test, so it is difficult and time consuming for me to try and improve Mali without owning a Mali device, hopefully Dib can help in the future. Or if someone gifts me a Mali device ;)

That's all for a bit, enjoy!

releases.name: ARMSX2 Refresh V19
releases.body:
For per-game touch layouts you can now set a virtual controller layout per game instead of one global layout. Profiles are saved to an inputprofiles folder in your app folder.

Rumble now works on connected Bluetooth and USB controllers, (please let me know if this issue is still persisting for you). 

Added button in the game library info button menu to either copy the ARMSX2 app data folder location, or use a downloaded file manager to access it directly. 

Long-press a game in the library and scroll to the last section "Info" to see its Title, Serial, CRC, Region, compatibility and path. Tap any field to copy it.

Renamed Max FPS Cap to Frame Rate Control.

DDS texture packs no longer crash (such as Resident Evil 4). 

No more "freezing" after the menu: the game would stayed paused after closing the menu in some cases (for example after saving a controller layout), and you had to re-open the menu and hit Resume. The game now resumes whenever you leave the menu.

Global vs per-game settings no longer bleed into each other, switching the Global/Game scope and saving could write to the wrong one. Each tier is kept separate now.

Gamepad face buttons work immediately at game load, no more pressing X/Triangle a few times to "wake up" the controller.

Audio no longer goes silent when toggling heavy game fixes, sound keeps playing through the change.

Fixed storage selection during initial setup. Choosing Internal or SD Card now works and gives clear feedback, picking SD Card on a device with no memory card tells you instead of silently doing nothing. Changing the storage location after setup now prompts a restart so it actually takes effect.

releases.name: ARMSX2 Refresh V18
releases.body:
New Max FPS Cap in Settings ---> Performance ---> Off / 60 / 30 / 20 / 15. Caps the on-screen frame rate to save battery and heat without slowing the game; emulation keeps running at full speed and only displayed frames are dropped. It's fully independent of the Speed Limit %, so you can run, say, 150% speed and a 30 fps display cap together. When a cap is active the OSD shows Display: N (cap N) so you can see the real presented rate.

The OSD now shows Display: N (cap N) when a cap is active, so you can see the real presented frame rate (the FPS/VPS lines still show the internal/vsync rate).

Added individual Hardware info (CPU/GPU model) and Emulator version toggles in Settings ---> Overlay, previously these could only be hidden via the master OSD switch in the Play section of settings. 

Per-game settings are now written out as individual .ini files (one per game). They're stored in the gamesettings folder inside the app's data directory:
/storage/emulated/0/Android/data/com.armsx2/files/gamesettings/

Moved the settings gear from the top-right corner to the top-center, so it's easier to reach.

The game library no longer scrolls sideways, covers wrap onto more shelves to fill the screen instead.

Added a green Resume button next to the X (exit) in the in-game menu, so getting back into the game is obvious rather than it just being in the Play section of settings.

The library now re-scans your games automatically on boot, so anything you've added shows up without a manual refresh.

Settings toggle boxes in the performance section of settings are now 35% smaller. 

Play section of settings bubbles should no longer balloon on tall displays (so smaller screens will have all settings).

Taken from @isztldav work on Mac, PCSX2 2.7 upstream commits:

Improved line anti-aliasing (AA1): sharper, less-blurry thin lines when upscaling.
Texture-cache accuracy: more precise "exact target" matching (OverlapsValid), reducing some texture-cache misdetections.

Updated controller database (adds 8BitDo Ultimate 2 mappings).

Bumped the shader cache version.

releases.name: ARMSX2 Refresh V17
releases.body:
Fixed graphics corruption on Mali GPUs under Vulkan. Devices with ARM Mali graphics showed white bands and blown-out visuals on transparency, bloom, and reflection effects unless you forced Blending Accuracy to Maximum. The renderer now handles Mali's hardware blending correctly at default settings, so those games render properly without needing blending at max. Other GPUs are unaffected.

Added pressure-sensitive button support (DualShock 2 analog presses). Games like Metal Gear Solid 2/3 and the 3D Grand Theft Auto titles rely on soft/partial button presses that previously had no way to be performed. Hold the new Pressure Modifier (either the on-screen "P½" button (under the D-pad) or a button you bind under Settings ---> Hotkeys ---> "Pressure Modifier (hold)" for physical controllers, then press a face, shoulder, or D-pad button for a 50% press. (Please let me know if it doesn't take effect in a game that uses pressure.)

Added multi-touch for the rest of the on-screen controls. You can now press multiple face buttons and triggers at the same time (L2 + R2, or holding a trigger while pressing a face button). Enable it with the "Multi-Touch" toggle in the overlay edit toolbar, same as before.

Due to restrictions by the GooglePlay store, external app folders were borked, replacing that feature is a simple Internal / SD Card choice. On the Play release, picking a custom folder left you unable to proceed past setup, and on sideloaded builds games would crash afterward. Setup now lets you choose Internal storage (your main device) and the default private app folder, or SD Card for memory cards, this was the best option I could come up with to remain complaint with Google. If you are ever fresh installing, be sure to back up your data first. (Arbitrary folders elsewhere on storage aren't possible without "All files access," which was removed for Google Play compliance.)

Mapping the D-pad in Pad settings now works on handhelds. On devices that report the D-pad as an analog like the AYN Odin 3, Retroid handhelds, etc., trying to bind it would just navigate the menu instead of capturing the input. The D-pad now binds correctly, and normal navigation resumes afterward.

Fixed audio cutting out after using the in-game menu. Sound could go silent after loading a save state or applying fixes, it now keeps playing. (Please let me know if you still have this issue on your device.)

Game library scrolling is smoother overall.

Fixed the app launcher icon. The logo appeared oddly sized, it now sits correctly within the adaptive icon shape.

releases.name: ARMSX2 iOS 2.3.1
releases.body:
From @J1coding 
**Cheats & Patches manager:** Each game now has its own manager for `.pnach` cheats and patches, available from the game long-press menu or the in-game quick menu. You can view named entries, enable/disable them per game, remove individual entries, or delete full patch/cheat files without manually editing text files.

**Patch and cheat downloads:** The manager can download matching PCSX2 patches or community cheats using the game’s serial/CRC, then merge them into that game’s files. Downloads start disabled so you choose what to activate, and trusted custom source URLs can be configured.

**Safer `.pnach` handling:** Imports now merge instead of blindly replacing content. Matching named blocks are updated, unrelated entries are preserved, backups are made before managed writes, and URL comments like `http://` / `https://` are no longer broken by `//` comment parsing.

**Compatibility Lab retired:** The old Compatibility Lab has been removed from the iOS menus and help. This is separate from the new Cheats & Patches manager; it was not replaced by it.

**Virtual pad upgrades:** The D-pad can now support one-finger diagonals and smoother rolling/quarter-circle inputs. Face-button combo zones let overlapping button areas trigger two-button combos while normal multi-touch still works. Both features can be disabled in VPad settings.

**Touch reliability fixes:** Virtual buttons now release when their view disappears, helping prevent stuck inputs after orientation changes, menus, or screen transitions.

**VPad editor polish:** The per-game VPad editor has its skin picker back, and the maximum virtual-control scale was raised from 4× to 7× for larger custom buttons and touch areas.

**Latest PCSX2 core updates:** Synced upstream PCSX2 work includes renderer and texture-cache fixes, improved AA1 line rendering at higher resolutions including Metal, refreshed shader cache handling, and other low-level maintenance.

releases.name: ARMSX2 Refresh V16 Mali Hotfix
releases.body:
Fixes for more Mali devices on Vulkan, PLEASE FRESH INSTALL FOR MALI USERS!

I have already sent out the Google Play Store build, so Mali users use this hot fix for now, I will update the PlayStore again once the other update rolls out. Thanks!

releases.name: ARMSX2 Refresh V16
releases.body:
From @jpolo1224 Added RetroAchievements Hardcore support on Android, including Hardcore status states, settings UI, login recovery/status handling, and enforcement around save-state loading and restricted features. Thank you to @LiquifiedSnow and the rest of the RA team for working with us!

Fixed Vulkan crashing on Mali GPUs. Devices with ARM Mali graphics (many MediaTek phones) would crash when loading a game with the Vulkan renderer. Vulkan now works on Mali. Other GPUs are unaffected. This should improve performance for Mali users!

EE Cycle Rate / Cycle Skip no longer revert each other. Adjusting one setting then the other previously reset the first, sliders now always commit against the current values.

Pause overlay Play tab fits all screens. The action grid (Resume, Save State, etc.) could overflow and clip the bottom row on shorter screens, it now scales to always show all three rows. (Please let me know if this issue still persists on your device).

Library help text no longer pushes the gear off-screen on small phones. Replaced the long left-side text with an info button that opens a dedicated, scrollable Library Help screen.

Mapping controller buttons in Pad settings works for every button. Binding B or Y no longer exits the menu. Normal navigation resumes after the bind.

Added Two-button hotkey combos.  In the Hotkeys tab, press one button for a single bind or two together for a combo (Select + R1 for Save State for example).

Added new settings from PCSX2 2.7:

• HW Accurate Alpha Test
• Draw Buffering
• Goemon TLB (game fix)
• VU Sync (game fix)
• SW Thread Tile Height
• Disable Vertex Shader Expand (restart to apply)
• Use Blit Swap Chain (restart to apply)
• Disable Shader Cache (restart to apply)

Fixed Limit 24-bit Depth hack (if it still is not working, please let me know! It may need a game restart to apply.)

Game library now scans subfolders and no longer skips certain .chd files.

releases.name: ARMSX2 Refresh V15
releases.body:
The emulator can now fully be navigated with a controller. Full controller navigation across the pause overlay, game library, settings, the memory card manager, and the RetroAchievements screens with both D-pad and analog sticks.

Pause overlay now works with both the D-pad and the analog stick (handhelds with or without a stick).

Press the (X) button on a highlighted game to open its per-game settings.

Rightmost covers now fully scroll into view when navigating a shelf with a controller.

Added various new Hotkeys settings:

New dedicated Hotkeys tab (moved out of the Pad tab).

New bindable hotkeys: Toggle Texture Dumping, Cycle Save Slot, Fast Forward (hold), Increase/Decrease Resolution, Open Achievements, Close Game.

Quick Save/Load State now use the currently selected slot (cycle it with Cycle Save Slot).

Texture Dumping: Added a runtime controller Toggle Texture Dumping hotkey to flip dumping on/off mid-game (for example, to stop dumping during FMVs).

Added a RetroAchievements button at the top of the pause overlay; tap it or press Y on a controller to open it.

Sign-in, the login form (username/password/cancel/sign-in), and scrolling the achievement list now work with a controller.

Settings tabs now use the full screen width, so sections and long option rows are no longer cut off.

Added a dedicated exit button for the pause overlay, tapping empty space no longer closes the overlay by accident.

Added useful relevant information in various parts of the emulator.

Brought over new settings for further PCSX2 2.7 parity:

Display fixes: Anti-Blur, Screen Offsets, Show Overscan, Disable Interlace Offset, Sync to Host Refresh, Disable Framebuffer Fetch, Override Texture Barriers.

Hardware/software fixes: Skip Draw Start/End, Spin GPU/CPU for Readbacks, Auto-Flush (SW), Mipmapping (SW), Software Rendering Threads.

Display/quality: Integer Scaling, Dithering, Vsync Queue Size.

Performance: VU0 Round Mode, VU1 Round Mode.

Create new memory cards: Folder type, and File cards in 8 / 16 / 32 / 64 MB.
The memory card manager now scrolls, so the creation form is fully reachable with and without a controller.

Brought in a PR from @misantronic

[RAOfflineProxy](https://github.com/misantronic/RAOfflineProxy) is a local proxy that sits between the emulator and retroachievements.org to cache RA data and enable offline achievement tracking. It needs emulators to expose a host override so it can point them at the loopback proxy.

This adds that to the refresh line — a port of https://github.com/ARMSX2/ARMSX2/pull/164 (merged to master), re-implemented
for the refresh architecture (native-lib.cpp JNI, com.armsx2, the existing
[Achievements] Host setting).

What it does

network_security_config: allow cleartext to 127.0.0.1/localhost (targetSdk 36 blocks it by default).
RetroAchievementsHostOverrideReceiver: broadcast to set/clear a loopback host; validated, persisted, and replayed on next launch if set while the app is closed.
native-lib JNI: drives the existing [Achievements] Host setting, forces hardcore off while active (restored on clear), and rebuilds the client.
Usage

adb shell am broadcast -a com.armsx2.action.SET_RETROACHIEVEMENTS_HOST_OVERRIDE \
  -e host "127.0.0.1:8080" -n com.armsx2/com.armsx2.RetroAchievementsHostOverrideReceiver
Clear with CLEAR_RETROACHIEVEMENTS_HOST_OVERRIDE. Tested on device.

Related work from @misantronic thank you for the PR!

https://github.com/ARMSX2/ARMSX2/pull/164 (ARMSX2 master)
https://github.com/hrydgard/ppsspp/pull/21760
https://github.com/dolphin-emu/dolphin/pull/14671
https://github.com/rafaelvcaetano/melonDS-android/pull/1633
https://github.com/libretro/RetroArch/pull/19093
https://github.com/flyinghead/flycast/pull/2371

releases.name: ARMSX2 Refresh V14
releases.body:
Fixed the boot crash on certain handhelds (AYANEO Pocket S Mini, Retroid Pocket Mini, and other Adreno devices) where V9 and newer would crash when launching any game. Was caused by an ImGui style value going below the minimum the newer ImGui enforces; affected both OpenGL and Vulkan on those devices. Enjoy the fix!

Added new settings: 
FPU Multiply
OPH Flag
Emulate GIF FIFO
DMA Busy
Delay VIF1 Stalls
I-Bit
Full VU0 Sync
VU Add-Sub
VU Overflow
Extra XGKICK Delay
Skip Duplicate Frames
EE FPU Round Mode (Nearest / Negative / Positive / Chop)
EE/FPU Clamping and VU Clamping modes.

releases.name: ARMSX2 Refresh V13
releases.body:
Added the new standard universal Android APK build: one APK now includes both 4K-page and 16K-page ARM64 cores. This should fix instability and crashes for the app on a range of devices. (Although it does increase the apps size, worth it though). Runtime now automatically loads the correct native core for the device: emucore_4k or emucore_16k. Restored strict page-size matching per core, fixing crashes seen on some 4K devices while keeping 16K-device support.

Added separate Swap Disc and Boot Disc flows. Boot Disc functions as Change Disk

Added native live disc hot-swap support for multi-disc for things like CodeBreaker. (Please report if it's working!)

Fixed an ARM64 recompiler zero-register shift issue that could crash or miscompile some games. (Ratchet and Clank series of games are now fixed, worth testing to see what other games were fixed as well).

Improved import patches readability.

Below-native resolution: added 0.25x, 0.5x, and 0.75x internal resolution options for more performance on lower-end devices.

EE/FPU Clamping setting: None / Normal / Extra / Full, in the Performance tab.

VU Clamping setting: None / Normal / Extra / Extra+Sign, in the Performance tab.

Save Manager: the Load State screen now manages saves per game. Each slot shows its date and size, you can delete a slot, back up and restore all of a game's saves, and there is an auto-save-on-exit toggle.

releases.name: ARMSX2 Refresh V12
releases.body:
Save/Load state hotkeys: quick-save and quick-load now bind to physical controller buttons, so you can save/restore without opening the menu. Configurable under Settings → Controller → Controller hotkeys (alongside the existing Menu/Pause bind).

Added VSync option under Renderer settings. Restart the game to apply.

Added Speedhack startup profiles: Optimal (default) and Fast (aggressive: EE cycle skip + fast CDVD, for low-end devices) presets at the top of Performance. Tweaking any individual speedhack un-highlights both, indicating a custom setup.

Added Region flags in the library: each cover now shows a 🇺🇸 / 🇪🇺 / 🇯🇵 / 🇰🇷 / 🌏 flag underneath, so you can tell regional versions of the same game apart at a glance.

Added more audio panel settings to match parity with PCSX2:
Time Stretch: keeps pitch stable when speed varies
Audio Buffer: 20–200 ms
Output Latency: 5–100 ms
Fast-Forward Volume: 0–100%

EE Cycle Rate / Cycle Skip now actually takes effect, (broke in V11).

releases.name: ARMSX2 iOS 2.3.0
releases.body:
Added RetroAchievements Hardcore support on iOS, including Hardcore status states, settings UI, login recovery/status handling, and enforcement around save-state loading and restricted features. Thank you to @LiquifiedSnow and the rest of the RA team for working with us! 

Brought over recompiler changes I did on Android to fix Ratchet and Clank series of games (should fix other games as well!)

Added RetroAchievements in-game support polish: achievement toast notifications with badges, current-game achievements panel, notification/leaderboard/overlay toggles, and better overlay defaults for iOS screens.

Brought over major Mac/core ARM64 improvements into iOS from @isztldav, especially EE/VU recompiler work around COP2/VU0 sync, native VU macro paths, load/store/branch handling, cycle accounting, and VU clamp/IR fixes.

From @J1coding 

VPad sizing overhaul: on-screen controls now have adjustable visible width/height instead of one uniform scale. Aspect lock keeps normal resizing simple, while unlocked sizing allows wider or taller controls when a layout or skin needs it. Sizes are clamped to a safe range, art stays centered while resizing, and analog sticks still scale safely as square controls.

Separate hit boxes: the touch area can now be sized separately from the visible button art, with its own width/height. This makes it possible to keep a clean-looking skin while making controls easier to tap, or to tighten hit zones for precise layouts. The editor shows hit-box outlines and overlap warnings so bad placements are easier to catch before playing.

Saved VPad layouts: users can save named layouts, update them, rename them, duplicate them, delete them, and choose a global default. Missing or deleted layouts fall back safely instead of leaving broken references.

Per-game layouts: individual games can use their own VPad layout without changing the global default. This is useful for one-off control setups, accessibility/fat-finger layouts, and special layouts like Guitar Hero-style fret rows. Games can be reset back to the global layout at any time.

Layout sharing: layouts can be exported and imported as .layout.json files, including the layout currently being edited. Imported values are validated/clamped and duplicate names are handled automatically, making layouts easy to share, back up, or bundle with a skin.

Multiple named custom skins: imported skins now live in a library instead of replacing one generic custom skin slot. Skins show up by name, can be renamed or deleted, and missing/default cases fall back cleanly. Folder, loose image, and zip imports are supported, with PNG/JPG/JPEG/WebP accepted and normalized on import.

Skin-linked layouts: skin packs can include an optional recommended layout with positions, visibility, sizes, and hit boxes. Applying a skin stays art-only by default; layout changes only happen when the user explicitly chooses to apply the linked layout. This keeps normal skins safe while supporting GH3/fret-style skins where the art and layout are meant to go together.

Per-game skins: a game can use its own skin independently from the global skin. Deleting a skin that a game was using falls back safely, and per-game skin overrides can be reset back to global.

Skin compatibility: old custom skins still work, shared analog bases still work, legacy ic_controller_analog_button.png knob art still works, and missing art falls back safely instead of leaving a blank/broken control.

Analog skin fixes: per-side analog base art is supported, and separate left/right analog stick art now resolves correctly. This fixes the issue where _analog_stick_left/right.png or legacy _analog_button_left/right.png could collapse into one shared/right-side image. Side-specific files now win for their own stick only, with shared and legacy fallbacks.

Default layout polish: the fresh landscape default layout now spaces the right stick farther from the face buttons, so the built-in layout no longer starts with that awkward overlap. Existing saved layouts are not changed.

iOS touch input reliability: newer iOS/iPadOS versions use the restored UIKit press-surface path for virtual pad input, while older versions keep the existing touch path. This restores reliable multi-touch/button behavior on the newer OS path without claiming any unrelated home-indicator change.

Per-game boot tuning: EE Cycle Rate and Fast Boot can now be set per game with Global Default/reset options. This helps tune heavy or awkward games without changing global settings. Negative EE Cycle Rate is blocked during RetroAchievements Hardcore.

OSD / overlay polish: the Quick Menu OSD button now cycles Off → Simple → Detail → Full → Off instead of acting like a basic on/off switch. Toasts and overlays respect safe areas better, reducing clipping near the notch, Dynamic Island, or screen edges. The in-game skin reset/default behavior was cleaned up too.

Import reliability: corrupt layouts/skins fall back safely, invalid skin images are skipped/reported, broken linked layouts do not block skin import, duplicate names get auto-suffixed, delete cleanup removes stale references, large imports are capped, and save failures are logged.

From @youngdstbeats-dev

Extends the existing iOS Network screen with the full DEV9 Ethernet configuration, matching PCSX2's Network & HDD tab. Lets you set a custom DNS for online play

Intercept DHCP: PS2 address, subnet mask (+ auto), gateway (+ auto).
DNS: DNS1 / DNS2 with Auto / Manual / Internal modes.
Internal DNS: a per-host hostname to IP table (add / remove / enable) for redirecting game servers.
Enable/disable behavior mirrors PCSX2's DEV9SettingsWidget: fields grey out based on the Ethernet toggle and the device type (Sockets), and the DNS address field is editable only in Manual mode.

QuickLoad: boot PS2 games from an extracted-disc folder (iOS):

Boot a game's .elf straight from its extracted folder, with the matching ISO mounted as the disc. Big load-time win on disc-heavy games like Resident Evil Outbreak File 1 & 2 (door loads roughly 8s -> 2s), and lets their online modes work.
New Disc Path menu to link / change / remove an ISO for an ELF (stored per game by ELF CRC, mounted as cdrom0: at boot).

Case-insensitive host: file resolution on iOS. Fixes the black screen where the game requested host:0flist.dir but the file on disk was 0FLIST.DIR.
Review feedback:

Linked disc is now stored as a relative library path instead of an absolute app-container path, so links survive app reinstall / container migration.
Scoped the case-insensitive host: fix to iOS only (it was affecting every non-Windows build).
Bug fixes:

ELF per-game settings (graphics, cheats, EE, MTVU, volume, game fixes) now actually apply. They were being saved under the wrong key and ignored at boot.
Same-named ISOs in different folders no longer link to the wrong file.

releases.name: ARMSX2 Refresh V10
releases.body:
16 KB-page device support: fixed "armsx2 keeps stopping" on 2025 flagships (Snapdragon 8 Elite gen 5 / Poco F8 Ultra). The build was originally compiled for 4 KB pages and treated a mismatch as a fatal error, so 16 KB-page phones crashed at boot before any game loaded. Now compiled for 16 KB with a relaxed page-size check, so one build runs on both 4 KB and 16 KB devices. Validated on the RP6 (RE4 at full speed).

Real cheats database added: the PCSX2 DB has no cheats, so cheats now come from community sources (shadowninja826 + xs1l3n7x) with serial fallback matching, so games like GTA San Andreas get cheats even when the disc CRC differs from the cheat author's (use at your own risk as the warning implies). Cheats cover 628 games and 4,500 for patches, many games will have widescreen but no cheats (DB limitation, not a bug).

Added Manual frame skip (0–5) from [issue #203] real implementation for low-end devices; presents 1 of every N+1 frames to free up GPU. Live-adjustable in the Performance tab.

Bind the menu to a physical button: works with RP6 back buttons. Toggles the in-game overlay.

Added a On-screen controls visibility slider: Never (hides touch controls + the cog so nothing overlaps R1, for physical-controls devices), auto-hide after 1–10 s, or Auto.

Added a swap button between 2D / 3D box art:  "Art: 2D/3D" button in the library; 3D uses the rendered-case covers. 

Fixed per-game settings overlap: the "Not signed in" achievements card no longer floats up into the action grid on phones.

HDD image path is now an editable, persistent field (Network tab),  fixes the HDD path resetting on every boot, SO no more needing to hand-editing the .ini. (Please let me know if this issue still persists).

releases.name: ARMSX2 Refresh V9
releases.body:
Rebased Android work onto the more stable V7 source to avoid the broken V8 regressions.

Improved Settings Menu, instead of squished sections, you can now scroll to access each section. 

Gear icon no longer persists, it will appear only if you tap on the top right side of the screen.

Restored settings persistence through PCSX2-Android.ini, including memory card selections, OSD toggles, DEV9 settings, and other saved options.

Added Skip BIOS / fast boot toggle.

Added Game Fixes section with Skip MPEG, FMV Software, EE Timing, Instant DMA, and Blit FPS options.

Added CRT / TV Shader options.

Added Shadeboost with brightness, contrast, saturation, and gamma controls.

Added Hardware Fixes, Texture Inside RT, Upscaling Fixes, Round Sprite, Bilinear Upscale, GPU Target CLUT, and CPU Sprite Render options.

Added clearer descriptions for render and compatibility settings.

Added live apply support for all rendering settings, you no longer need to reset the game for your changes to take effect (other than graphics API of course). 

Fixed Scan button behavior in the library.

Added additional DEV9 Ethernet/HDD settings and ensured the ini can be generated/saved.

Fixed NASCAR Dirt to Daytona / Cricket 07 FPS halving. Restored two MTVU optimizations that V6 had dropped: spin-before-wait (WaitForWorkWithSpin) and batched semaXGkick.Post() 

New "Fixes" tab with full PCSX2 upscaling/HW-fix parity (16 fields: align/merge sprite, half-pixel offset, texture offsets, GPU/CPU palette conversion, depth/render-fix toggles, etc.). Upscaling glow/issue effects can now be fixed with these settings!

Added a Software renderer option. 

Added an online patch/cheat browser, fetches per-game from the PCSX2 patch DB with a per-entry checklist while in game and includes a patch-codes warning before proceeding. Be sure to reset your game after importing if doing so in game. 

Browse patches by game name without booting: 1. Long-press a library game ----> Patches ----> Browse; looks the game up by serial in the patch DB's file tree (CRC resolved from the filename).

releases.name: ARMSX2 macOS 2.4.0
releases.body:
This release delivers big improvements for many games and fixes mathematical issues.

VU macro mode ported from x86 to arm!

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/ARMSX2/ARMSX2/compare/MacOSv2.3.4...MacOSv2.4.0

releases.name: ARMSX2 Refresh V8
releases.body:
Added Limit 24-Bit Depth settings.

Changed blending default to Basic like NetherSX2 has, with migration from old Full, should improve performance out of the box! Can be adjusted as needed on a per-game basis. 

Fixed DEV9 HDD path and made PCSX2-Android.ini generate/save.

Added long-press game cards for per-game settings before launch.

Added custom background support for the game library, click the "Wall" button to import a custom background! Once imported, a "Reset" button will appear alongside it, in case you want to revert back to the default background. 

Added face-button multitouch/slide overlap option, use the Touch Layout setting button to enable while in game!

Improved pre-launch patch settings visibility.

Improved overall Menu readability. 

New Pad settings tab in the overlay for physical controller mappings. Tap an action, press a controller button to bind it. Includes reset controller mappings option.

Steam Controller Support! 

Improved texture pack loading.

releases.name: ARMSX2 Refresh V7
releases.body:
Added PNACH import/manual code entry fixes:

Auto-names PNACH files using active game serial + current ELF CRC.

Adds manual Execute code entry.

Auto-enables Cheats (PNACH) when importing/executing.

Added fractional upscale multipliers:
Native, 1.25x, 1.5x, 1.75x, 2.25x, 2.5x, 2.75x, 3.5x, 4x, 5x.

Improved setup screen 1 landscape support:

Downloaded covers now mirror into the app covers folder properly.

Improved lightweight disc probing for CHD/raw/multi-disk layouts.

Small performance improvements:

Mac ARM64 backend COP2 direct-dispatch optimization.

Enabled a new faster memory access path for the Android Refresh core. This should improve performance in some games, especially heavier titles that spend a lot of time on CPU-side memory access. Early testing looks good so far in GTA San Andreas, God of War 2, and Lego Batman. If any games show new crashes or weird behavior, this can be turned back off quickly, so please provide feedback!

releases.name: ARMSX2 Refresh V6
releases.body:
Potential small performance improvements. 

Added new settings/features: 

Speed limiter slider

Audio volume slider and mute toggle

PNACH/cheat/widescreen patch toggles and import PNACH feature

DEV9 networking/HDD settings fixes

Added boot ELF support

Added DEV9 auto HDD image creation

Can now manually toggle specific OSD settings, such as GPU OSD timing disabled when the GPU overlay is off, which can give some performance back. 

Fixed BIOS deletion issues after a fresh install. 

Also brought over work from the Mac side from @isztldav ARM64: EE rec — finish deferred microVU0 before non-interlocked COP2 ops (match x86 mVUFinishVU0), adds the VU0 finish path before non-interlocked COP2 transfers / LQC2 / SQC2, while avoiding the extra redundant finish for hot COP2 macro compute ops.

releases.name: ARMSX2 Refresh V5
releases.body:
MTVU can now actually be toggled on/off instead of being forced back on by saved config.

Added Deinterlacing options to renderer settings.

Added Hardware Download Mode toggle/options like OG ARMSX2, use at your own discretion. This setting can improve performance in some games and potentially break other games.

Added proper Instant VU1 runtime handling so the in-game toggle updates the native VM immediately instead of feeling forced/stuck.

releases.name: ARMSX2 Refresh V4
releases.body:
Fixed VM lifecycle handling around close/reset paths: exit without saving, and
save state and exit now work properly. 

Reset/apply-restart now works. 

The in-game pause menu now pauses the emulator like intended. 

Fixed pause/menu state getting confused and causing the VM to act sped-up instead of properly paused.

Fixed a variety of perf and renderer settings, they should now work as intended. 

Added display/aspect ratio options. 

Added texture pack support/import flow into textures/<serial>/replacements.

Improved memory card manager, folders now work.

Fixed BIOS path handling so BIOS follows the selected data root/system folder instead of being pinned to app-private storage.

Added external game launch intent support for launchers like ES-DE.

releases.name: ARMSX2 Refresh V3
releases.body:
Overhauled the UI for Android Refresh with a new setup screen, shelf-style game browsing based off the designs done by @Vivimagic, and improved/shifted around settings. A first implementation, so please let us know of any feedback!

Added boot intro done by @Vivimagic as a placeholder until the Refresh boot logo is completed.

Added Memory Card manager entry/UI.

Added Network settings tab from OG ARMSX2-style networking work.

Added renderer/backend settings section for OpenGL/Vulkan/custom driver/recompiler-related options in settings instead of the setup screen.

Performance improvements from experimental builds. 

Added Mali alpha blending path courtesy of @Dib1998

BIOS user preferences now save after the initial setup!

Known Issues: 

Pause does still not fully pause the emulator using the in game pause/settings menu. 

Using the exit game button in the game pause/settings menu still does not work. I could not figure out a fix with the time I had available to me, a tested PR from a capable dev would be more than appreciated!

releases.name: ARMSX2 iOS 2.2.2 Release 2
releases.body:
Updated latest iOS-refresh, including merged PRs #189 from @EricDraven2503 and #190 from @j1coding

PR#189: 

Geometrics out of buttons visible when pressed drastically improved, they now take the shape of buttons according to the current layout selected, I didn't verify whether it works with custom layouts as well but it should;

Italian and German now available on Settings > Language > App Language (only the same strings as every other language have been introduced);

Gesture Bar appearing during emulation for iOS 17/iOS 18 has been resolved for non-touch gameplay. 

PR#190: 

This PR adds emulator volume controls to the iOS app, making it easier to lower game audio while using Discord, calls, music, or other system audio.

It adds a new Emulator Volume slider in the global emulator settings. The slider controls emulator/game audio only, while leaving iOS system volume and other apps unchanged. The selected volume is saved between launches and applies to both normal gameplay and fast-forward audio so volume stays predictable.

This PR also adds per-game volume overrides. Users can choose a custom volume for individual games, or leave a game set to use the global emulator volume. Per-game overrides can be reset back to global at any time without affecting other per-game settings.

What changed

- Added a global Emulator Volume slider under Emulator settings.
- Added support for volume values from 0% to 100%.
- Saved emulator volume between app launches.
- Applied the same volume value to normal and fast-forward audio.
- Added per-game custom volume support.
- Added a Use Custom Volume toggle in per-game settings.
- Added a Using Global: XX% state when no custom volume is set.
- Added Reset to Global behavior for per-game volume.
- Preserved unrelated per-game settings when disabling custom volume.
- Added a small native bridge path for applying emulator volume settings.
- Added accessibility-friendly labels and visible percentage values for the new controls.

releases.name: ARMSX2 Refresh Experimental Release 2 Hotfix
releases.body:
Quick hot fix to Experimental Release 2: 

Settings now usable outside of a game. 

Fixed in game settings crashing the app.

releases.name: ARMSX2 Refresh Experimental Release 2
releases.body:
PSA: This is an experimental test build done outside of @zeruth main refresh branch. 

Made the mac ARM64 recompiler work done by @isztldav the default for Android refresh, for this test build the old arm64 recompiler is not active. 

Brought over recent shared ARM64 correctness/performance fixes from macOS/iOS work.

Improved EE recompiler behavior with better branch/memory handling, cycle accounting, GPR cache retention, logical-immediate fast paths, and native memory-path improvements, taken from macOS/iOS work.

Added VU/microVU fixes for PQ lane preservation, FTOI NaN handling, SQRT clamp behavior, jump-cache handling, and VU reserve safety, taken from macOS/iOS work

Added GT4/OpenGL GameDB fix (game already works fine with Vulkan + custom drivers such as Turnip).

Added the in-game settings button so users do not have to long-press the screen.

Updated Android app icon assets to the new icon set.

Synced shared GS/audio/core fixes that were brought over from the macOS side.

This update may potentially improve performance, it's worth letting us know about any feedback through testing! I tested this build on a Retroid Pocket 6 with a SD8gen2, so performance results may vary. Thank you.

releases.name: ARMSX2 iOS 2.2.2
releases.body:
Fixed Sonic Unleashed iOS Metal rendering by allowing the game’s required OI_SonicUnleashed GameDB beforeDraw callback through the iOS Metal callback safety filter.

Brought over @isztldav ARM64 EE timing fixes: flushes cpuRegs.cycle before inline COP2/VU0 macro ops so VU timing is current,
applies the COP0 Config.DIE EE cycle multiplier like x86 PCSX2. Crash Twinsanity is now fixed thanks to these changes! 

Added Apple Airplay support, ARMSX2 iOS can now be played on a TV! 

Hardcore RetroAchievements is now in the review stage for iOS! I will update when the RA team has approved us, it will take some time. For the time being the hardcore toggle has been hidden. Softcore RetroAchievements works as intended.

From @j1coding PR: 

Added Settings > Appearance.

Added custom Game Library backgrounds.

Added optional landscape-specific backgrounds.

Added a Background Dim control for readability.

Improved background centering and rotation behavior.

Improved List view readability when custom backgrounds are active.

Improved Cover Flow layout in landscape and on wider screens.

Added a supported-formats hint to the empty library state.

Improved VoiceOver labels for Game Library controls and status indicators.

Localized the new Game Library and Appearance strings.

releases.name: ARMSX2 iOS 2.2.1 Release 4
releases.body:
Fix RetroAcheivements login, fix acheivements not popping up in game, improved UI for RetroAcheivements (now available in quick settings to view the list for the current game being played), (still no Hardcore yet, waiting to be approved)

This release fixes an important bug in memory management that could lead to a crash mid game. Carried over from Mac work done by @isztldav. (Sync ARM64 microVU reserve fix from macOS.)

releases.name: ARMSX2 macOS 2.3.4
releases.body:
ARM64: EE rec — charge each op the COP0 Config.DIE memory-speed multiplier (cycles double when the i-cache bit is clear), matching the x86 rec's per-op accounting
ARM64: EE rec — flush cpuRegs.cycle before inline COP2/VU0-macro ops so the VU kicks at a current EE time (fixes Crash Twinsanity object pop-in/overlap)

Enjoy Crash Twinsanity!

releases.name: ARMSX2 macOS 2.3.3
releases.body:
This release fixes an important bug in memory management that could lead to a crash mid game. 

Enjoy!

releases.name: ARMSX2 iOS 2.2.1 Release 3
releases.body:
Upstream with PCSX2 where applicable: 

Shared GS RT/DS sampling-hazard sample-area fix.

GPU timing stats update fix.

SPU2 volume / fast-forward volume safety guards.

Thank you to @isztldav

releases.name: ARMSX2 iOS 2.2.1 Release 2
releases.body: Fixes MGS2 Graphical Bugs, have fun! Thank you to @isztldav for the fix!

releases.name: ARMSX2 iOS 2.2.1
releases.body:
Midnight Club 3 Graphical Smoke issue in the garage fixed. 

Further ARM64 EE Performance improvements. 

Potential fix for Silent Hill 2 graphical issues at the intro, (the flashlight bug still remains). 

Brought over from @isztldav work on the Mac Side: 

Ported ARM64 microVU fix for FTOI NaN-pattern handling.

Ported ARM64 microVU SQRT clamp handling fix.

Ported fix for frozen cpuRegs.cycle when taken branches fall back to single-step interpreter execution.

Ported microVU jump-cache optimization to skip unnecessary emit setup on jump-cache hits.

Burnout 2001 now works. 

From @j1coding PR: 

Improved the iOS on-screen controller layout, especially in portrait mode

The game now stays easier to see in portrait, with the controls placed in a cleaner bottom controller area

The controller background is now transparent by default, so it no longer blocks the game view unless you are using a full custom skin

You can now move individual D-pad and face buttons instead of only moving whole groups

You can now hide or show individual controls, including separate toggles for X, O, Triangle, and Square

Added an Undo button in the layout editor for accidental moves or changes

Cancel now properly discards unsaved layout edits

Save now clearly commits your layout changes

Skin changes in the editor now roll back if you cancel instead of saving

The layout editor now shows a clear message if you try to edit the wrong orientation, like editing landscape layout while holding the device in portrait

The editor no longer shows squeezed or misleading button positions when the selected layout does not match the device orientation

Added a cleaner top toolbar for layout editing with quick access to undo, save, visibility, reset, and skin options

Improved touch handling in the editor so taps do not accidentally pass through to the game underneath

Reset options now work more reliably with the new per-button visibility and placement system

Portrait and landscape controller layouts should now feel more consistent and easier to customize. 

Thank you to @Vivimagic for the new app icon!

releases.name: ARMSX2 macOS 2.3.2
releases.body:
ARM64: microVU — EFU scalar ops were zeroing mVU_xmmPQ's Q lanes (MGS2 broken polygons)

Enjoy MGS2 bug free!

releases.name: ARMSX2 iOS 2.2 Release 2
releases.body:
Added in-game experimental Fast Forward controls to Quick Settings, this may increase device heat while using.

Added new on-screen controller skins and made White Colored the new default.
Preserved the previous default controller skin as Legacy Refresh.

Added skin choices for Black, Black Colored, Black DS, Black White, Full White, White DS, White Full Color Button, Liquid Glass, and Xbox.

Added controller skin selection to Virtual Pad settings, layout editor, and in-game Quick Settings.

Thank you to Bagas on discord for creating the controller skins!

releases.name: ARMSX2 iOS 2.2
releases.body:
Updated OSD/FPS branding to ARMSX2 iOS 2.2.

Fixed controller rumble. 

Improved the ARM64 EE recompiler: GPR cache expansion, const folding, cached loads/stores, branch-likely support, wait-loop handling, full-clamp FPU path, and unaligned load/store native paths. This should improve performance quite a bit. Thank you to @zeruth for assisting.

ARM64: Port x86 iFPUd full clamp mode (eeClampMode=3) for EE FPU, fixes NFS Carbon hang courtesy of @isztldav

releases.name: ARMSX2 macOS 2.3.1
releases.body:
ARM64: Skip microVU emit setup on jump-cache hits @archie2x
ARM64: Add native EE unaligned load/store + branch-likely codegen, wait-loop detection, and GPR-cache improvements
ARM64: Fix frozen cpuRegs.cycle in single-step fallback for taken branches (Burnout CPCOND0 halt)
ARM64: microVU — fix FTOI NaN-pattern inputs (saturate by sign, not 0) and SQRT clamp NaN propagation (Fminnm)

releases.name: ARMSX2 MacOS 2.2 Release 1
releases.body:
ARM64: Port x86 iFPUd full clamp mode (eeClampMode=3) for EE FPU, fixes NFS Carbon hang @isztldav 
ARM64: Add cached LQ/SQ fast path to the EE GPR-cache translation layer @jpolo1224 
ARM64: Fold const-known EE ALU ops into the GPR cache as immediate Movs @jpolo1224 

This release further improves the performance on apple devices.
Floating point now also supports mode 3 from x86, adding support for NFS Carbon and potentially other games.

releases.name: ARMSX2 Refresh V21
releases.body:
Performance and stability improvements this update (will vary by device). 

The Getaway is fixed. It was crashing on launch before, now it boots and ran at full speed in gameplay on my RP6, (still slow in cutscenes). 

You can now map each analog stick direction to any PS2 button. Open Pad settings, set a stick to Custom, tap a direction, and press the button you want it to act as. 

A new toggle that makes the physical D-pad drive the left analog stick at full deflection. Perfect for games that only read the analog stick and ignore the D-pad.

Added a new "Close Game & Quit" controller hotkey, plus: games launched from another app (ES-DE, iiSU, etc.) now return to that app when you close them instead of dropping back to the ARMSX2 library, (as long as you have the "Exit to launcher on close" setting enabled.)

The Setup screen for the GitHub build's app folder step is now a single "Choose Data Location" button instead of the SD/Internal split.

Added a toggle to turn on game titles under your cover art with the "Aa" button on the left side of the library. Each title now also shows a small version tag (disc version or serial), so if you've got two copies of the same game you can finally tell them apart.

Restored saving states and patches (widescreen, etc.) in Hardcore mode, cheats and loading states remain disabled in Hardcore mode.

releases.name: ARMSX2 Refresh V11
releases.body:
Brought over the improvements done on MacOS by @isztldav 

This release delivers big improvements for many games and fixes mathematical issues.

VU macro mode ported from x86 to arm! Major performance improvements and games fixed. Enjoy!

releases.name: ARMSX2 Refresh
releases.body:
ARMSX2 Android Refresh Changelog

Updated emulator core with newer PCSX2 2.7-based changes.

Improved ARM64 JIT behavior and overall performance.

Added recent GameDB/core compatibility fixes.

Improved graphics compatibility for several games.

Added hardware/game-specific fixes from recent refresh work.

Improved MTVU/fastmem behavior where supported.

Better default settings for smoother gameplay.

General stability fixes and cleanup.

Improved cover/game metadata handling.

New UI designed by the wizard @vivimagic that will continue to be improved 

Ongoing work: more EE/VU optimization and game-specific fixes are still planned.

Thank you to @zeruth and @isztldav for the major work done for ARMSX2 refresh! It will only get better from here, please enjoy and provide feedback.

releases.name: ARMSX2 iOS 2.1 Release 4
releases.body:
Major iOS ARM64 performance improvement, including EE/VU JIT work and the VU jump-cache fast path that fixed big slowdowns testers reported.

Improved ARM64 EE register/cache handling and memory translation paths to reduce CPU pressure and device heat.

Fixed RetroAchievements launch crashes when RA was left enabled across app relaunches.

Softcore RA remains usable, Hardcore is disabled/unavailable for now until we get approval from the RA team.

Fixed iOS 18 performance. 

Added iOS 27-specific virtual button fixes.

Added/updated GameDB and hardware-renderer fixes brought over from the Mac-side work.

Reduced hot-path telemetry/device-stat overhead and cached OSD device stats.

Thank you to @isztldav for his contributions

releases.name: ARMSX2 MacOS 2.1 Release 3
releases.body:
This release brings important bug fixes and memory improvements.  

An important bug affecting MTVU games was addressed. Games like `Rayman 3` or `Scarface` now run and play. @isztldav 

Removed unneeded new/delete VIXL MacroAssembler, BeginCodeWrite/EndCodeWrite on VU cache hit. This potentially improves many games, one being `Ratchet & Clank` @archie2x

Reverted official PCSX2 qt UI until new ARMSX2 UI native for macOS is ready.

releases.name: ARMSX2 iOS 2.1 Release 3
releases.body:
Universal JIT now supported. 

Universal / UTM-Dolphin JIT script selector in Emulator settings, make sure to fully close/relaunch the app between switching Universal and UTM-Dolphin, because the code memory registration happens once when the VM thread is first created.

Universal script path using brk #0xf00d prepare + detach.

Legacy UTM-Dolphin path using brk #0x69.

Potential iOS 27-specific virtual button touch fix.

Thanks to @j1coding for assisting.

releases.name: ARMSX2 iOS 2.1 Release 2
releases.body:
Brought over new changes from MacOS:

Ported the updated controller database.

Ported shared fullscreen UI string/resource updates.

Improved iOS 18 performance.

releases.name: ARMSX2 iOS 2.1 Hotfix
releases.body:
Performance is now much better for iOS 18 users:

Fixed iOS 18 Legacy JIT performance by using page-aligned range RW->RX protection instead of flipping the entire 0xa100000 code cache every emitted block.

Wrapped ARM64 block emission, constant pool trampolines, and literal writes with the new range write-protection path.

Kept the pre-VM MTVU/fastmem sync path that avoids the earlier startup crash/settings side effects.

Set Fast Boot default to OFF across iOS UI settings, boot bridge sync, PCSX2 core defaults, and fullscreen UI defaults (testers reported improved performance).

releases.name: ARMSX2 MacOS 2.1 Release 2
releases.body:
Fixed macOS local release signing

Merged latest PCSX2 master updates into the macOS branch.

Updated controller database with newer mappings.

Updated Qt base translations.

@isztldav

releases.name: ARMSX2 MacOS 2.1 Hotfix
releases.body:
Fixes duplicate game entries from adding the same game by file and folder.

Bootstraps backend settings so the PCSX2 setup wizard should not keep coming back.

releases.name: ARMSX2-iOS 2.1
releases.body:
Updated ARMSX2 iOS metadata and OSD branding to ARMSX2 iOS 2.1.

Kept the current fast iOS ARM64 JIT path and integrated the recent EE-side optimization work: fastmem, MTVU, block linking, const propagation, and GPR cache work.

Restored native-resolution iOS Metal GameDB hardware fixes after the PCSX2 2.7 settings reload path, so safe fixes like HPO/native scaling/align sprite survive properly.

Brought over the mac/PCSX2 graphics fixes used to improve GTA, God of War, and related hardware-renderer behavior:
ARM64: microVU — fix scalar FMAC zeroing ACC's other lanes
GS: Fix dirty reg handling
GS: Fix temp rect handling
GS: Fix some more draw buffering edgecases
GS/TC: Add new HW Readback option to force full download
GS/TC: Don't pre-downscale if native palette draw is enabled
GS/HW: Only expand overlaps on page boundaries
GS/HW: Fix draw offsets in shuffles
GameDB: Various fixes

Performance is improved as well as device heat.

From @j1coding PR: Cleans up the in-game quick menu and runtime UI code, making the menu behavior more consistent and easier to maintain. 

This also improves small things like status messages, save-state labels, reset confirmation behavior, and disc-swap menu organization.

Fixes .pnach imports when files include bracketed cheat names like [Cheat Name], so common cheat files are handled correctly instead of being rejected.

Fixes the OSD toggle so turning it off and on again restores the previous Detail/Full mode instead of resetting to Simple.

Fixes fullscreen behavior so the app better remembers what fullscreen is actually doing. The fullscreen toggle should no longer look backwards, get stuck, or show the wrong state after rotating the device or reopening the in-game menu.

Enables iOS Game Mode support through the app plist, allowing iOS to recognize ARMSX2 as a game where supported.

Refreshes the home indicator state while emulating, so the gesture bar can auto-hide more reliably during gameplay.

Fixes skip-draw settings so global and per-game skip-draw changes are applied live instead of only saving without taking effect.

releases.name: ARMSX2-MacOS 2.1
releases.body:
Updated app and in-game OSD branding to ARMSX2-MacOS 2.1.

Added PCSX2 upstream core updates.

Added GameDB fixes from the newer branch.

Added graphics/core fixes affecting GTA, God of War, MGS, and other hardware-renderer edge cases:
GS dirty register handling fix
GS temp rect handling fix
draw buffering edge-case fixes
HW readback full-download option
native palette / pre-downscale fix
overlap expansion page-boundary fix
shuffle draw-offset fix

Added ARM64 microVU scalar FMAC fix for GoW2 black screen / broken graphics.

Ported newer ARM64 EE improvements from the iOS work to macOS.

Enabled GameDB core fixes by default.
Enabled GameDB hardware fixes by default.

Added one-time migration so existing installs also get the correct GameDB defaults.
Added fullscreen/game-list cover download support from the newer branch.

Improved macOS keyboard behavior in Qt/ImGui.
Added clipboard support and modifier-key fixes.
Improved controller binding labels, including multitap slot naming.
Fixed game list view toggling when the game list is empty.

@isztldav

releases.name: ARMSX2-iOS 2.0
releases.body: Experimental test build for ARMSX2 on iOS. Uses PCSX2 2.7 Core and now has a native arm64 jit instead of a x86 translation layer. Built off the work off the original iOS port. Thanks to @Zeruth and @isztldav for the native arm64 support.

releases.name: ARMSX2-MacOS V1
releases.body: Experimental test build for ARMSX2 Mac OS. Uses PCSX2 2.7 Core and does not rely on Rosetta, instead using an experimental arm64jit. Thanks to @Zeruth and @isztldav for the native arm64 support.

releases.name: ARMSX2-iOS 1.3.2
releases.body:
Added external USB/SSD game support so PS2 games can be scanned and launched without copying them into ARMSX2 storage.

Added external game file/folder management under Storage settings, with saved access so external entries remain after restarting the app.

Improved external game launching stability, including fixes for file picker access, security-scoped paths, and returning from gameplay to the main menu.

Improved cover handling for external games, including better persistence and refresh behavior.

Merged the latest settings/graphics compatibility improvements from recent PR work, including cleaner compatibility controls.

Cleaned up the External Games info text to focus on USB/SSD storage only.

Updated app metadata and OSD/FPS counter version display to 1.3.2.

Added external USB/SSD game support so PS2 games can be scanned and launched without copying them into ARMSX2 storage.

Added external game file/folder management under Storage settings, with saved access so external entries remain after restarting the app.

Improved external game launching stability, including fixes for file picker access, security-scoped paths, and returning from gameplay to the main menu.

Improved cover handling for external games, including better persistence and refresh behavior.

Merged the latest settings/graphics compatibility improvements from recent PR work, including cleaner compatibility controls.

Cleaned up the External Games info text to focus on USB/SSD storage only.

This update focuses on better settings, easier per-game tweaking, and safer in-game testing.

Graphics settings are easier to understand now, with clearer help/warnings for risky options like high resolution, Null renderer, texture preloading, texture dumping, VSync queue, and advanced hacks.

Added Advanced Upscaling Hacks on iOS:
Half-pixel Offset, Round Sprite, Align Sprite, Merge Sprite, Wild Arms Offset, Texture Offset X/Y, Trilinear Filtering, and Skipdraw Start/End.

Texture Offset X/Y are now typeable, so exact values like 420 or 440 can be entered directly instead of tapping hundreds of times.

Added Skipdraw Start/End for games where broken fog, overlays, or post-processing can hide parts of the scene.

Skipdraw input is clearer now:
“Skipdraw 1” means Start 1 / End 1, and confusing ranges like Start 1 / End 0 are corrected automatically.

Per-game manual hacks now clearly show when they can actually apply:
Use Per-Game Overrides ON + GameDB Graphics Fixes OFF.

Per-Game Settings got a cleaner UI in both the game library and the in-game quick menu.

Runtime Per-Game Settings is safer during gameplay now.
Opening it, pressing Done, or saving now avoids scanning the active game/disc while the game is running.

This should prevent issues where streamed music breaks after using Per-Game Settings while sound effects still continue.

iPhone landscape layout was improved for Games, BIOS, Help, and Settings, with better spacing around the notch/Dynamic Island area.

Added Copy Troubleshooting Info in Help > About.
It copies privacy-safe app/device info only, without game names, BIOS names, file paths, saves, logs, or personal info.

Added Enable Host File System in Settings > Emulator > Host Filesystem

Thank you to @J1coding for contributing and xxcrimson and funi for testing.

releases.name: ARMSX2-iOS 1.3.1
releases.body:
Fixed Black’s black sky on iOS Metal.

Updated app metadata, OSD, and build marker to 1.3.1.

MTVU and deinterlacing are now available in Per-Game Settings.

The Virtual Pad can now automatically hide when an external controller is connected.

Silent Hill 3 graphics issues are also fixed by disabling gamedb core fixes.

releases.name: ARMSX2-iOS 1.3
releases.body:
Added the Burnout graphics skyfix path present in 1.2.4 to Burnout 3: Takedown.

Improved EE performance behavior by removing old iPSX2 diagnostic overhead from hot emulation paths. Overall emulator performance is now improved, and device heat may be improved as well. 

Various games have received corrected graphics thanks to @Dib1998 VU fix in 1.2.4

Fixed custom PNG texture replacements appearing too dark on iOS thanks to @yuliangtao

Updated app and OSD version display to ARMSX2 iOS 1.3.

Fixes from @J1coding 

Large imported files now use a safer fallback when the normal file copy path fails. Instead of loading the whole imported file into memory at once, the app now streams the file in small chunks through a temporary file before moving it into place. This should make large ISO, CHD, and other game imports less likely to hit memory problems on iOS.

BIOS imports now give clearer feedback when a copied BIOS-related file is not actually bootable. If a companion ROM or unsupported BIOS dump is imported, the app explains that it cannot be used to boot games instead of making the import look fully successful.

Game and BIOS imports now warn before replacing existing files with the same name. Cancelling keeps the current files untouched, while Replace imports the selected batch intentionally.

Cover download failures now give clearer guidance about what to check next. Local cover selection has also been improved: users can choose covers from Photos, and the Files picker now uses the same reliable picker flow as the game and BIOS import screens.
