Reached from the Explore tab's GOG card or the Banner Tools → GOG tile. - **BannerHub Explore homepage** — the bottom-nav "Explore" tab opens a BannerHub-owned homepage instead of the stock online discovery feed: wordmark/logo hero, a "What's New" article that auto-syncs from the release notes, the GOG store card, and an in-app **update banner + installed-vs-latest version readout** with a settings cog. Content can refresh live via an external JSON override; still fully offline-capable. - **Recording-compatible audio** — optional toggle (Banner Tools → Audio) that routes PulseAudio through the mixer so screen recorders capture in-game sound. - **In-game performance overlay** *(root)* — a draggable edge pill → slide-out panel for live CPU-governor + Adreno min-clock toggles while gaming; off by default, root-gated, auto-reverts on exit. Toggle from Banner Tools → In-game Overlay. - **9 side-by-side variants** — different package names + launcher labels so multiple builds coexist on one device (`Original` replaces stock GameHub; the rest install alongside it). - **Optional Lite builds** — a ~34.5 MB-smaller counterpart of every variant. - **Muted UI sounds** — menu/button click sounds silenced; in-game audio untouched. - **Root-free file manager access** — a content provider lets MT Manager and similar apps browse GameHub's data dir without root. - **Portrait PC-settings** — per-game PC settings render in both landscape and portrait (side benefit of the API redirect). - **Installs in place** — stable signing keystore; future BannerHub v6 stables upgrade without uninstalling. - **BannerHub v6 branding** — custom app icon, Wine container header, and auth/splash artwork. - **Triage-friendly** — debuggable build with logcat markers along the import path.
### What's new in 1.8.0-604 On top of `v1.7.0-604` (same keystore — installs in place): ### ⚡ In-game performance overlay (root) A new in-game overlay for on-the-fly performance tuning without leaving your game. A draggable edge **pill** slides out a panel with root-gated toggles: - **CPU governor → performance** — pins the CPU governor while you play. - **Adreno GPU min-clock boost** — raises the kgsl minimum frequency so the GPU stops downclocking under load. The overlay is **off by default** and the whole feature is **root-gated** — non-rooted devices (and anyone who doesn't enable it) see nothing. Turn it on from **Banner Tools → In-game Overlay**, with a separate **Root** tile to grant/verify su. Includes a **pill opacity slider** so you can make it as subtle as you like, and it **auto-reverts** the CPU/GPU changes when you exit the game. ### 🛠️ Banner Tools now in the Explorer screen The **Banner Tools** option now lives in the **Explorer** tab/screen. Its settings are **per-game options only** — you open Banner Tools from a game in the Explorer view and the changes you make apply to that game alone, not app-wide. ### Variants Each variant has its own package name + launcher label, so the variants install **side-by-side** with each other. `Original` replaces an installed GameHub; the rest install alongside it. **Full vs Lite:** a Lite APK shares its full counterpart's package name and cert — installing Lite **replaces** the matching full build (and vice versa). The version string is `BannerHub v6` (product) + our semver + `-604` (the GameHub base, `6.0.4`) — e.g. `1.8.0-604`. ### Install notes - **Signing**: stable BannerHub test keystore. Online behaviour byte-identical; fully fail-safe (any error falls back to stock). | | **PC-accurate vibration** | Routes XInput rumble into Android's VibratorManager (independent dual-motor). Preload-free: an on-disk `winebus.so` patch defeats SDL2's ~1 s auto-stop with no `libevshim.so` / `LD_PRELOAD` (fixes the x86_64/box64 launch-death). Default on at 100%. | | **PC Vibration Settings (activity + menu row)** | Per-game mode (off / device / controller / both) and intensity (0–100%), plus a **PC Vibration Settings** row in both per-game popups. | | **GPU Spoof** | Per-game GPU identity reported to DXVK via a per-game `dxvk.conf` — Off (default), a preset NVIDIA/AMD/Intel picker, or fully custom vendor/device/name. Fixes "unsupported video card" refusals (e.g. CryEngine — Crysis 2). | | **Legacy renderer (GLES2) toggle** | Per-game opt-in to the proven 6.0.2 GLES2-era `libxserver.so`/`libwinemu.so` pair for games that regressed under 6.0.4's Vulkan X-server rewrite. Default New = stock, zero patch effect. | | **Strict per-game settings store** | GPU Spoof / Legacy renderer / PC Vibration each save strictly per-game in BannerHub's own store with explicit Save/Cancel — no host clobber, no app-wide leak; resolved across the `:wine` process boundary. | | **Show PC Game Settings row** | Keeps the per-game **PC game settings** row visible in Explorer view (XiaoJi's UX hid it for Steam-linked games). | | **Privacy hardening** | Upstream telemetry/analytics channels stripped at the bytecode level (Firebase Analytics/Crashlytics, GMS Measurement, Mob Push, heartbeat, OTA, Ad-ID, cert-pinning override, etc.). Full list + honest leftovers in `PRIVACY.md`. | | **Change app icon** | BannerHub v6 branding — adaptive icon, Wine container header, auth/splash assets. | | **Change package & app name** *(per variant)* | Per-variant package + launcher label so all 9 variants install side-by-side (`Original` keeps `com.xiaoji.egggame`). | | **Lite size-reduction strips** *(Lite only)* | Strips the dead 20 MB font, Aliyun carrier-login lib, Haima cloud-gaming stack, and AVIF/HEIC codecs. ~34.5 MB smaller; no effect on full variants. | *Per-patch mechanics, smali anchors, and version-by-version letter maps are in the [README's Patches applied section](https://github.com/The412Banner/bannerhub-revanced/blob/gamehub-604-build/README.md#patches-applied) and the [patch sources](https://github.com/The412Banner/bannerhub-revanced/tree/gamehub-604-build/patches/src/main/kotlin/app/revanced/patches/gamehub).*
### Credits PC-accurate controller vibration (including the preload-free rework) is built on **[TideGear](https://github.com/TideGear)**'s [GameHub-Vibration-Fix](https://github.com/TideGear/GameHub-Vibration-Fix) (PR #80 + PR #91), adapted with explicit permission, deriving from [GameNative](https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative) (PR #1214). Full project credits (DXVK, VKD3D-Proton, Box64, FEX, Mesa Turnip, ReVanced, etc.) are in the [README Credits section](https://github.com/The412Banner/bannerhub-revanced/blob/gamehub-604-build/README.md#credits).

releases.name: BannerHub v6 1.7.0-604
releases.body:
## BannerHub v6 1.7.0-604 > ## ⚠️ Important — please read before installing > > **BannerHub v6 does NOT replace BannerHub 3.7.x or BannerHub Lite — they are SEPARATE projects.** > > - **BannerHub** 3.7.x — built from the GameHub 5.3.5 ReVanced project by **PlayDay**. > - **BannerHub Lite** — built from GameHub Lite 5.1.4 by **Producdevity**. > - **BannerHub v6** *(this repo)* — built from XiaoJi GameHub 6.0.x on a brand-new pipeline. > > **None of the three are to be updated over by any of the others.** Each ships with its own package names, its own keystore, and its own component / Steam-client backend — Android won't accept an in-place update between them, and forcing one will leave you with a broken install. Uninstall first, then install the new project if you want to switch. > > Keep in mind **BannerHub v6 is still a work in progress** and will re-release frequently as new base GameHub versions come out from XiaoJi upstream. **Compatibility is different** — don't expect every game that works on BannerHub 3.7.x or Lite to work on v6, and vice versa. v6 uses a **new component system and new Steam clients** and, thus far, has been **barely tested in general**. > > **USE AT YOUR OWN RISK.** A patched build of **XiaoJi GameHub 6.0.4** that removes the login requirement (import and play games with no account) and redirects the catalog to the BannerHub Cloudflare Worker. Ships **9 variants that install side-by-side**, each with an optional **Lite** build (~34.5 MB / ≈32% smaller). **18 APKs attached** — 9 full + 9 Lite. Steam game launches work end-to-end. 🔒 **Privacy-hardened** — upstream telemetry/analytics channels stripped at the bytecode level. Full list of killed channels (and the honest leftovers) in [`PRIVACY.md`](https://github.com/The412Banner/bannerhub-revanced/blob/gamehub-604-build/PRIVACY.md).
✨ Everything BannerHub v6 offers (click to expand) - **No login required** — launch straight to the home screen and import/play games without a XiaoJi account. - **Custom catalog API** — the catalog is redirected to the BannerHub Cloudflare Worker for **curated component delivery** (hand-picked DXVK, VKD3D-Proton, Box64, FEX, Mesa Turnip driver builds, etc.) instead of the stock upstream set. - **Privacy-hardened** — upstream telemetry/analytics channels stripped at the bytecode level (see the `PRIVACY.md` link above for the full list). - **PC-accurate controller vibration** — real XInput rumble routed into Android's VibratorManager with independent dual-motor support, configurable **per-game** (mode + intensity) via the **PC Vibration Settings** menu row. Preload-free — no `libevshim` / `LD_PRELOAD`. - **GOG integration** — sign in to GOG, browse your owned library, and download + install GOG games in-app; they're added to your GameHub library and launch like any other PC game. Reached from the Explore tab's GOG card or the Banner Tools → GOG tile. - **BannerHub Explore homepage** — the bottom-nav "Explore" tab opens a BannerHub-owned homepage instead of the stock online discovery feed: wordmark/logo hero, a "What's New" article that auto-syncs from the release notes, the GOG store card, and an in-app **update banner + installed-vs-latest version readout** with a settings cog. Content can refresh live via an external JSON override; still fully offline-capable. - **Recording-compatible audio** — optional toggle (Banner Tools → Audio) that routes PulseAudio through the mixer so screen recorders capture in-game sound. - **9 side-by-side variants** — different package names + launcher labels so multiple builds coexist on one device (`Original` replaces stock GameHub; the rest install alongside it). - **Optional Lite builds** — a ~34.5 MB-smaller counterpart of every variant. - **Muted UI sounds** — menu/button click sounds silenced; in-game audio untouched. - **Root-free file manager access** — a content provider lets MT Manager and similar apps browse GameHub's data dir without root. - **Portrait PC-settings** — per-game PC settings render in both landscape and portrait (side benefit of the API redirect). - **Installs in place** — stable signing keystore; future BannerHub v6 stables upgrade without uninstalling. - **BannerHub v6 branding** — custom app icon, Wine container header, and auth/splash artwork. - **Triage-friendly** — debuggable build with logcat markers along the import path.
### What's new in 1.7.0-604 On top of `v1.6.0-604` (same keystore — installs in place): ### 🧭 Redesigned Explore homepage The **Explore** tab is now a full BannerHub-owned homepage instead of a single card — a wordmark/logo hero, a **What's New** article that auto-syncs from these release notes, and the GOG store card on the hero. Content can refresh **live via an external JSON override**, so news and highlights can change without shipping a new build. Still fully offline-capable, with a top badge row (📥 Downloads · Discord · Reddit) and a back button. ### 🆕 In-app update check + version readout Explore now shows your **installed version vs. the latest stable**, with an **amber update banner** when a newer release is out and a **⚙ settings cog** for Explore options. The version comes from a build-stamped manifest baked into the APK (not the host GameHub version), so the readout is accurate per BannerHub release. ### 🐛 GOG path fix Fixed a **doubled-directory bug** in GOG **"Set .exe"** and **"Copy to Downloads"** — the install path was being re-wrapped through the game's own directory, producing a broken nested path. Also now prompts for **storage permission** before copying on pre-Android-10 devices. ### Variants Each variant has its own package name + launcher label, so the variants install **side-by-side** with each other. `Original` replaces an installed GameHub; the rest install alongside it. **Full vs Lite:** a Lite APK shares its full counterpart's package name and cert — installing Lite **replaces** the matching full build (and vice versa). They do **not** coexist; pick one per row. | Variant | Package | Full APK / Lite APK | | --- | --- | --- | | Normal | `banner.hub` | `…-Normal.apk` / `…-Normal-Lite.apk` | | Normal-GHL | `gamehub.lite` | `…-Normal-GHL.apk` / `…-Normal-GHL-Lite.apk` | | PuBG | `com.tencent.ig` | `…-PuBG.apk` / `…-PuBG-Lite.apk` | | AnTuTu | `com.antutu.ABenchMark` | `…-AnTuTu.apk` / `…-AnTuTu-Lite.apk` | | alt-AnTuTu | `com.antutu.benchmark.full` | `…-alt-AnTuTu.apk` / `…-alt-AnTuTu-Lite.apk` | | PuBG-CrossFire | `com.tencent.tmgp.cf` | `…-PuBG-CrossFire.apk` / `…-PuBG-CrossFire-Lite.apk` | | Ludashi | `com.ludashi.aibench` | `…-Ludashi.apk` / `…-Ludashi-Lite.apk` | | Genshin | `com.miHoYo.GenshinImpact` | `…-Genshin.apk` / `…-Genshin-Lite.apk` | | Original | `com.xiaoji.egggame` | `…-Original.apk` / `…-Original-Lite.apk` | Filenames follow `BannerHub-V6-1.7.0-604-Patched-{variant}[-Lite].apk`. The version string is `BannerHub v6` (product) + our semver + `-604` (the GameHub base, `6.0.4`) — e.g. `1.7.0-604`. ### Install notes - **Signing**: stable BannerHub test keystore. A one-time uninstall is only needed if upgrading from `v1.0.0-604` or older (cert changed then); every release since installs in place. Fingerprints in [`keystore/README.md`](https://github.com/The412Banner/bannerhub-revanced/blob/gamehub-604-build/keystore/README.md). - Base APK is unmodified XiaoJi GameHub 6.0.4 (versionCode 114) — only the bytecode/resources the patches need are touched. - The `.rvp` / `.rve` bundles are attached for use with `revanced-cli` directly. Built with ReVanced CLI 6.0.0; full APKs from `gamehub-604-build`, Lite from `feature/lite-variant-tier1`.
📦 Patches applied (click to expand) | Patch | What it does | | --- | --- | | **Bypass login** | Launch straight to the home screen; import and play games with no account. | | **Disable Firebase Crashlytics** | Prevents a launch-time `VerifyError` crash. | | **Debug logging** | Debuggable APK + logcat markers along the import path for triage. | | **File manager access** | Content provider so external file managers (MT Manager etc.) can browse the data dir without root. | | **Rewrite custom permissions per variant** | Stops two installed variants colliding on Android 7+. | | **Mute UI sounds** | Silences menu/button clicks; game audio untouched. | | **Redirect catalog API** | Points the catalog at the BannerHub Worker; also fixes PC-settings rendering in portrait. | | **Prefix API path with /v6** | Lets the Worker serve 6.0-only responses without affecting 5.x clients. | | **Offline component picker — local list** | Offline, every per-game picker (GPU driver, DXVK, VKD3D, FEXCore/Box64, **and the Wine/Proton container**) lists the components you've already downloaded, catalog-ordered. Online behaviour byte-identical; fully fail-safe (any error falls back to stock). | | **PC-accurate vibration** | Routes XInput rumble into Android's VibratorManager (independent dual-motor). Preload-free: an on-disk `winebus.so` patch defeats SDL2's ~1 s auto-stop with no `libevshim.so` / `LD_PRELOAD` (fixes the x86_64/box64 launch-death). Default on at 100%. | | **PC Vibration Settings (activity + menu row)** | Per-game mode (off / device / controller / both) and intensity (0–100%), plus a **PC Vibration Settings** row in both per-game popups. | | **GPU Spoof** | Per-game GPU identity reported to DXVK via a per-game `dxvk.conf` — Off (default), a preset NVIDIA/AMD/Intel picker, or fully custom vendor/device/name. Fixes "unsupported video card" refusals (e.g. CryEngine — Crysis 2). | | **Legacy renderer (GLES2) toggle** | Per-game opt-in to the proven 6.0.2 GLES2-era `libxserver.so`/`libwinemu.so` pair for games that regressed under 6.0.4's Vulkan X-server rewrite. Default New = stock, zero patch effect. | | **Strict per-game settings store** | GPU Spoof / Legacy renderer / PC Vibration each save strictly per-game in BannerHub's own store with explicit Save/Cancel — no host clobber, no app-wide leak; resolved across the `:wine` process boundary. | | **Show PC Game Settings row** | Keeps the per-game **PC game settings** row visible in Explorer view (XiaoJi's UX hid it for Steam-linked games). | | **Privacy hardening** | Upstream telemetry/analytics channels stripped at the bytecode level (Firebase Analytics/Crashlytics, GMS Measurement, Mob Push, heartbeat, OTA, Ad-ID, cert-pinning override, etc.). Full list + honest leftovers in `PRIVACY.md`. | | **Change app icon** | BannerHub v6 branding — adaptive icon, Wine container header, auth/splash assets. | | **Change package & app name** *(per variant)* | Per-variant package + launcher label so all 9 variants install side-by-side (`Original` keeps `com.xiaoji.egggame`). | | **Lite size-reduction strips** *(Lite only)* | Strips the dead 20 MB font, Aliyun carrier-login lib, Haima cloud-gaming stack, and AVIF/HEIC codecs. ~34.5 MB smaller; no effect on full variants. | *Per-patch mechanics, smali anchors, and version-by-version letter maps are in the [README's Patches applied section](https://github.com/The412Banner/bannerhub-revanced/blob/gamehub-604-build/README.md#patches-applied) and the [patch sources](https://github.com/The412Banner/bannerhub-revanced/tree/gamehub-604-build/patches/src/main/kotlin/app/revanced/patches/gamehub).*
### Credits PC-accurate controller vibration (including the preload-free rework) is built on **[TideGear](https://github.com/TideGear)**'s [GameHub-Vibration-Fix](https://github.com/TideGear/GameHub-Vibration-Fix) (PR #80 + PR #91), adapted with explicit permission, deriving from [GameNative](https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative) (PR #1214). Full project credits (DXVK, VKD3D-Proton, Box64, FEX, Mesa Turnip, ReVanced, etc.) are in the [README Credits section](https://github.com/The412Banner/bannerhub-revanced/blob/gamehub-604-build/README.md#credits).

releases.body:
## BannerHub v6 1.5.1-604

> ## ⚠️ Important — please read before installing
>
> **BannerHub v6 does NOT replace BannerHub 3.7.x or BannerHub Lite — they are SEPARATE projects.**
>
> - **BannerHub** 3.7.x — built from the GameHub 5.3.5 ReVanced project by **PlayDay**.
> - **BannerHub Lite** — built from GameHub Lite 5.1.4 by **Producdevity**.
> - **BannerHub v6** *(this repo)* — built from XiaoJi GameHub 6.0.x on a brand-new pipeline.
>
> **None of the three are to be updated over by any of the others.** Each ships with its own package names, its own keystore, and its own component / Steam-client backend — Android won't accept an in-place update between them, and forcing one will leave you with a broken install. Uninstall first, then install the new project if you want to switch.
>
> Keep in mind **BannerHub v6 is still a work in progress** and will re-release frequently as new base GameHub versions come out from XiaoJi upstream. **Compatibility is different** — don't expect every game that works on BannerHub 3.7.x or Lite to work on v6, and vice versa. v6 uses a **new component system and new Steam clients** and, thus far, has been **barely tested in general**.
>
> **USE AT YOUR OWN RISK.**

**Hotfix on top of `v1.5.0-604`** — drop-in update, stable keystore unchanged, installs in place. **18 APKs attached** (9 full + 9 Lite) + 3 `.rvp` bundles.

### What's fixed in 1.5.1-604

- **🪪 Imported / Epic / GOG games now launch from Beacon, ES-DE, RetroHRAI, NeoStation, and Daijishou.** Previously couldn't be launched from external front-ends — fixed. See the [front-end launcher setup guide](https://github.com/The412Banner/bannerhub-revanced/blob/gamehub-604-build/beacon-setup.md) for per-variant `am` commands and the intent contract.
- **🧰 New Banner Tools menu.** PC Vibration, GPU Spoof, Renderer, and Show Game ID are now grouped under one **Banner Tools** entry to keep the per-game menu tidy.

Everything else — the full v6 feature set, variants table, signing notes, and patches-applied list — is unchanged from [v1.5.0-604](https://github.com/The412Banner/bannerhub-revanced/releases/tag/v1.5.0-604).

### 📺 Video walkthroughs

- **Beacon** — <https://youtu.be/hyjjs-ffpw4?si=Lp6CCGhwFKGR0tAA> (also covers creating PC-import game `.txt` / `.iso` files with GameID numbers)
- **RetroHRAI** — <https://youtu.be/tcYGLLRtCPY?si=0oEWYZo-8QopFQey>
- **NeoStation** — <https://youtu.be/mTn7La43LpQ?si=4PPV_gpKl_AwTchM>

📖 **Front-end launcher setup (Beacon / ES-DE / RetroHRAI / NeoStation / Daijishou)** → [`beacon-setup.md`](https://github.com/The412Banner/bannerhub-revanced/blob/gamehub-604-build/beacon-setup.md) — per-variant launch commands, `localGameId` / `steamAppId` / `autoStartGame` extras, and how to find a game's ID.

🔒 **Privacy-hardened** — full list of killed channels (and the honest leftovers) in [`PRIVACY.md`](https://github.com/The412Banner/bannerhub-revanced/blob/gamehub-604-build/PRIVACY.md).

🙏 **Credits & lineage** — see the [README Credits section](https://github.com/The412Banner/bannerhub-revanced/blob/gamehub-604-build/README.md#credits).

releases.name: BannerHub v6 1.5.0-604
releases.body:
## BannerHub v6 1.5.0-604 > ## ⚠️ Important — please read before installing > > **BannerHub v6 does NOT replace BannerHub 3.7.x or BannerHub Lite — they are SEPARATE projects.** > > - **BannerHub** 3.7.x — built from the GameHub 5.3.5 ReVanced project by **PlayDay**. > - **BannerHub Lite** — built from GameHub Lite 5.1.4 by **Producdevity**. > - **BannerHub v6** *(this repo)* — built from XiaoJi GameHub 6.0.x on a brand-new pipeline. > > **None of the three are to be updated over by any of the others.** Each ships with its own package names, its own keystore, and its own component / Steam-client backend — Android won't accept an in-place update between them, and forcing one will leave you with a broken install. Uninstall first, then install the new project if you want to switch. > > Keep in mind **BannerHub v6 is still a work in progress** and will re-release frequently as new base GameHub versions come out from XiaoJi upstream. **Compatibility is different** — don't expect every game that works on BannerHub 3.7.x or Lite to work on v6, and vice versa. v6 uses a **new component system and new Steam clients** and, thus far, has been **barely tested in general**. > > **USE AT YOUR OWN RISK.** A patched build of **XiaoJi GameHub 6.0.4** that removes the login requirement (import and play games with no account) and redirects the catalog to the BannerHub Cloudflare Worker. Ships **9 variants that install side-by-side**, each with an optional **Lite** build (~34.5 MB / ≈32% smaller). **18 APKs attached** — 9 full + 9 Lite. Steam game launches work end-to-end. 🔒 **Privacy-hardened** — upstream telemetry/analytics channels stripped at the bytecode level. Full list of killed channels (and the honest leftovers) in [`PRIVACY.md`](https://github.com/The412Banner/bannerhub-revanced/blob/gamehub-604-build/PRIVACY.md).
✨ Everything BannerHub v6 offers (click to expand) - **No login required** — launch straight to the home screen and import/play games without a XiaoJi account. - **Custom catalog API** — the catalog is redirected to the BannerHub Cloudflare Worker for **curated component delivery** (hand-picked DXVK, VKD3D-Proton, Box64, FEX, Mesa Turnip driver builds, etc.) instead of the stock upstream set. - **Privacy-hardened** — upstream telemetry/analytics channels stripped at the bytecode level (see the `PRIVACY.md` link above for the full list). - **PC-accurate controller vibration** — real XInput rumble routed into Android's VibratorManager with independent dual-motor support, configurable **per-game** (mode + intensity) via the **PC Vibration Settings** menu row. Preload-free — no `libevshim` / `LD_PRELOAD`. - **9 side-by-side variants** — different package names + launcher labels so multiple builds coexist on one device (`Original` replaces stock GameHub; the rest install alongside it). - **Optional Lite builds** — a ~34.5 MB-smaller counterpart of every variant. - **Muted UI sounds** — menu/button click sounds silenced; in-game audio untouched. - **Root-free file manager access** — a content provider lets MT Manager and similar apps browse GameHub's data dir without root. - **Portrait PC-settings** — per-game PC settings render in both landscape and portrait (side benefit of the API redirect). - **Installs in place** — stable signing keystore; future BannerHub v6 stables upgrade without uninstalling. - **BannerHub v6 branding** — custom app icon, Wine container header, and auth/splash artwork. - **Triage-friendly** — debuggable build with logcat markers along the import path.
### What's new in 1.5.0-604 On top of `v1.4.0-604` (same keystore — installs in place): - **🎮 External launcher integration (Beacon / ES-DE / Daijishou).** PC and Steam games can now be launched directly from external front-ends. Intents accept both `--ei` (int) and `--es` (String) extras to match Beacon's actual call shape. **Beacon: device-confirmed working.** **ES-DE and Daijishou: untested** — the implementation is shared so they *should* work, but neither has been validated on-device yet (please report results). Epic Games library was attempted but the upstream `GameDetailViewModel` ignores the `app_nav_epic_app_name` route — **Epic remains unsupported**; PC + Steam ship working end-to-end. **📖 [Beacon setup guide for all 9 variants → `beacon-setup.md`](https://github.com/The412Banner/bannerhub-revanced/blob/gamehub-604-build/beacon-setup.md)**. - **🪪 Show Game ID menu row + View All Games dialog.** A new **Show Game ID** row in the per-game menus pops up a dialog with the gameId (with a **Copy** button) so you can configure Beacon / ES-DE / Daijishou entries without grepping a logcat. The dialog's **View All Games** button opens the full library list backed by `db_game_library.db` — tap any row to copy its id. - **🖼️ Proper menu row icons.** Vibration / GPU Spoof / Renderer rows previously shared the Remove-from-Library trash icon because of a wrong icon-field read. Each row now reads its correct field (`zz4.b0` / `zz4.v` / `zz4.c0`) and renders its own icon in both per-game menus. Thanks @TideGear (PR #6). - **📱 PC Vibration Settings dialog — portrait layout.** Dialog detects orientation and reshapes for portrait phones: Mode + Intensity stack vertically, width caps at `min(360dp, screenW - 24dp)`, intensity slider caps at 220dp. Landscape unchanged (480dp side-by-side). Thanks @TideGear (PR #6). ### Variants Each variant has its own package name + launcher label, so the variants install **side-by-side** with each other. `Original` replaces an installed GameHub; the rest install alongside it. **Full vs Lite:** a Lite APK shares its full counterpart's package name and cert — installing Lite **replaces** the matching full build (and vice versa). They do **not** coexist; pick one per row. | Variant | Package | Full APK / Lite APK | | --- | --- | --- | | Normal | `banner.hub` | `…-Normal.apk` / `…-Normal-Lite.apk` | | Normal-GHL | `gamehub.lite` | `…-Normal-GHL.apk` / `…-Normal-GHL-Lite.apk` | | PuBG | `com.tencent.ig` | `…-PuBG.apk` / `…-PuBG-Lite.apk` | | AnTuTu | `com.antutu.ABenchMark` | `…-AnTuTu.apk` / `…-AnTuTu-Lite.apk` | | alt-AnTuTu | `com.antutu.benchmark.full` | `…-alt-AnTuTu.apk` / `…-alt-AnTuTu-Lite.apk` | | PuBG-CrossFire | `com.tencent.tmgp.cf` | `…-PuBG-CrossFire.apk` / `…-PuBG-CrossFire-Lite.apk` | | Ludashi | `com.ludashi.aibench` | `…-Ludashi.apk` / `…-Ludashi-Lite.apk` | | Genshin | `com.miHoYo.GenshinImpact` | `…-Genshin.apk` / `…-Genshin-Lite.apk` | | Original | `com.xiaoji.egggame` | `…-Original.apk` / `…-Original-Lite.apk` | Filenames follow `BannerHub-V6-1.5.0-604-Patched-{variant}[-Lite].apk`. The version string is `BannerHub v6` (product) + our semver + `-604` (the GameHub base, `6.0.4`) — e.g. `1.5.0-604`. ### Install notes - **Signing**: stable BannerHub test keystore. A one-time uninstall is only needed if upgrading from `v1.0.0-604` or older (cert changed then); every release since installs in place. Fingerprints in [`keystore/README.md`](https://github.com/The412Banner/bannerhub-revanced/blob/gamehub-604-build/keystore/README.md). - Base APK is unmodified XiaoJi GameHub 6.0.4 (versionCode 114) — only the bytecode/resources the patches need are touched. - The `.rvp` / `.rve` bundles are attached for use with `revanced-cli` directly. Built with ReVanced CLI 6.0.0; full APKs from `gamehub-604-build`, Lite from `feature/lite-variant-tier1`.
📦 Patches applied (click to expand) | Patch | What it does | | --- | --- | | **Bypass login** | Launch straight to the home screen; import and play games with no account. | | **Disable Firebase Crashlytics** | Prevents a launch-time `VerifyError` crash. | | **Debug logging** | Debuggable APK + logcat markers along the import path for triage. | | **File manager access** | Content provider so external file managers (MT Manager etc.) can browse the data dir without root. | | **Rewrite custom permissions per variant** | Stops two installed variants colliding on Android 7+. | | **Mute UI sounds** | Silences menu/button clicks; game audio untouched. | | **Redirect catalog API** | Points the catalog at the BannerHub Worker; also fixes PC-settings rendering in portrait. | | **Prefix API path with /v6** | Lets the Worker serve 6.0-only responses without affecting 5.x clients. | | **Offline component picker — local list** | Offline, every per-game picker (GPU driver, DXVK, VKD3D, FEXCore/Box64, **and the Wine/Proton container**) lists the components you've already downloaded, catalog-ordered. Online behaviour byte-identical; fully fail-safe (any error falls back to stock). | | **PC-accurate vibration** | Routes XInput rumble into Android's VibratorManager (independent dual-motor). Preload-free: an on-disk `winebus.so` patch defeats SDL2's ~1 s auto-stop with no `libevshim.so` / `LD_PRELOAD` (fixes the x86_64/box64 launch-death). Default on at 100%. | | **PC Vibration Settings (activity + menu row)** | Per-game mode (off / device / controller / both) and intensity (0–100%), plus a **PC Vibration Settings** row in both per-game popups. | | **GPU Spoof** | Per-game GPU identity reported to DXVK via a per-game `dxvk.conf` — Off (default), a preset NVIDIA/AMD/Intel picker, or fully custom vendor/device/name. Fixes "unsupported video card" refusals (e.g. CryEngine — Crysis 2). | | **Legacy renderer (GLES2) toggle** | Per-game opt-in to the proven 6.0.2 GLES2-era `libxserver.so`/`libwinemu.so` pair for games that regressed under 6.0.4's Vulkan X-server rewrite. Default New = stock, zero patch effect. | | **Strict per-game settings store** | GPU Spoof / Legacy renderer / PC Vibration each save strictly per-game in BannerHub's own store with explicit Save/Cancel — no host clobber, no app-wide leak; resolved across the `:wine` process boundary. | | **Show PC Game Settings row** | Keeps the per-game **PC game settings** row visible in Explorer view (XiaoJi's UX hid it for Steam-linked games). | | **Privacy hardening** | Upstream telemetry/analytics channels stripped at the bytecode level (Firebase Analytics/Crashlytics, GMS Measurement, Mob Push, heartbeat, OTA, Ad-ID, cert-pinning override, etc.). Full list + honest leftovers in `PRIVACY.md`. | | **Change app icon** | BannerHub v6 branding — adaptive icon, Wine container header, auth/splash assets. | | **Change package & app name** *(per variant)* | Per-variant package + launcher label so all 9 variants install side-by-side (`Original` keeps `com.xiaoji.egggame`). | | **Lite size-reduction strips** *(Lite only)* | Strips the dead 20 MB font, Aliyun carrier-login lib, Haima cloud-gaming stack, and AVIF/HEIC codecs. ~34.5 MB smaller; no effect on full variants. | *Per-patch mechanics, smali anchors, and version-by-version letter maps are in the [README's Patches applied section](https://github.com/The412Banner/bannerhub-revanced/blob/gamehub-604-build/README.md#patches-applied) and the [patch sources](https://github.com/The412Banner/bannerhub-revanced/tree/gamehub-604-build/patches/src/main/kotlin/app/revanced/patches/gamehub).*
### Credits PC-accurate controller vibration (including the preload-free rework) is built on **[TideGear](https://github.com/TideGear)**'s [GameHub-Vibration-Fix](https://github.com/TideGear/GameHub-Vibration-Fix) (PR #80 + PR #91), adapted with explicit permission, deriving from [GameNative](https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative) (PR #1214). Full project credits (DXVK, VKD3D-Proton, Box64, FEX, Mesa Turnip, ReVanced, etc.) are in the [README Credits section](https://github.com/The412Banner/bannerhub-revanced/blob/gamehub-604-build/README.md#credits). --- ### 🔮 Upcoming features A heads-up on what's queued for the next stable: - **GOG game store support** — first-class GOG library + launch integration alongside the existing Steam / Epic / PC paths. GameHub 6.0.4's `app_nav_gog_*` routes are stubs upstream; the work involves porting a GOG account/library/download backend (≈ scope of the existing Epic-EOS integration). Tracking branch: see the `bannerhub-revanced-gog-*` notes once it lands on `gamehub-604-build`. - **Banner Tools menu consolidation** — the per-game menu is getting long (PC Vibration / GPU Spoof / Renderer / Show Game ID, plus the upstream rows). The next stable will collapse all of our added rows into a **single "Banner Tools"** entry that opens a BannerHub-owned dialog containing the same options, restoring the original short menu shape and freeing room for future features (including the GOG row above) without further bloat. These are work-in-progress and not yet in any build. As always, follow the [PROGRESS_LOG](https://github.com/The412Banner/bannerhub-revanced/blob/gamehub-604-build/PROGRESS_LOG.md) for day-to-day status.

Uninstall first, then install the new project if you want to switch. > > Keep in mind **BannerHub v6 is still a work in progress** and will re-release frequently as new base GameHub versions come out from XiaoJi upstream. **Compatibility is different** — don't expect every game that works on BannerHub 3.7.x or Lite to work on v6, and vice versa. v6 uses a **new component system and new Steam clients** and, thus far, has been **barely tested in general**. > > **USE AT YOUR OWN RISK.** A patched build of **XiaoJi GameHub 6.0.4** that removes the login requirement (import and play games with no account) and redirects the catalog to the BannerHub Cloudflare Worker. Preload-free — no `libevshim` / `LD_PRELOAD`. - **9 side-by-side variants** — different package names + launcher labels so multiple builds coexist on one device (`Original` replaces stock GameHub; the rest install alongside it). - **Optional Lite builds** — a ~34.5 MB-smaller counterpart of every variant. - **Muted UI sounds** — menu/button click sounds silenced; in-game audio untouched. - **Root-free file manager access** — a content provider lets MT Manager and similar apps browse GameHub's data dir without root. - **Portrait PC-settings** — per-game PC settings render in both landscape and portrait (side benefit of the API redirect). - **Installs in place** — stable signing keystore; future BannerHub v6 stables upgrade without uninstalling. - **BannerHub v6 branding** — custom app icon, Wine container header, and auth/splash artwork. - **Triage-friendly** — debuggable build with logcat markers along the import path.
### What's new in 1.4.0-604 On top of `v1.3.0-604` (same keystore — installs in place): - **🎛️ Per-game settings, properly isolated.** GPU Spoof / Legacy renderer / PC Vibration each save to BannerHub's own per-game store with explicit **Save / Cancel** buttons — no more silent reset when GameHub rewrites its own settings file, and a choice for one game never leaks into another or app-wide. Settings now correctly cross the `:wine` process boundary so launch-time scoping actually works. - **🪪 GPU Spoof (per game).** A new **GPU Spoof** menu row + dialog reports a chosen GPU identity (preset NVIDIA/AMD/Intel list spanning legacy → modern, or a fully custom vendor/device/name) to DXVK via a per-game `dxvk.conf`. Fixes titles that refuse to start on an "unsupported" adapter (e.g. CryEngine — Crysis 2). Off by default; zero effect when Off. - **🖼️ Legacy renderer toggle (per game).** Per-game opt-in to the proven 6.0.2 GLES2-era `libxserver.so`/`libwinemu.so` pair for games that regressed under 6.0.4's Vulkan X-server rewrite. Default **New** = stock 6.0.4, zero patch effect. - **🎮 Rumble no longer cuts out after ~2 s.** The winebus duration patch is now actually applied at launch (the v1.3.0 hook landed as dead code on this base); sustained rumble holds indefinitely. Online behaviour is byte-identical and the patch is fully fail-safe (any error falls back to stock).
