releases.name: BannerHub Lite v1.0.2
releases.body:
## BannerHub Lite v1.0.2

Adds the BannerHub AI Frame Generation menu to BHL, plus fixes the firmware-on-first-game-add path so the URGENT warning from v1.0.1 should no longer be needed.

> 🆕 **Firmware fix is server-side.** The "Failed to initialize the simulator" / "firmware still on 1.3.3" issue from v1.0.1 was a stale row in the BannerHub API's `executeScript` endpoint — the Add-Game path was advertising the old `imagefs.zst` (1.3.3) even though the upgrade-check endpoint was on 1.3.7. Fixed in `bannerhub-api@45c3d2f` (2026-05-12) so every BHL APK, including older v1.0.0/v1.0.1 installs, now gets imagefs 1.3.7 on first-game-add without any client change.

### Known issues

- **Proton 11 hangs on "Initializing…" when launching Steam games with a Steam client.** Carried over from v1.0.1 — the launch never proceeds past the initializing screen. Workaround: use a different Wine/Proton component for Steam-client launches until this is resolved.

### What changed since v1.0.1

- **AI Frame Generation menu (ported from BannerHub 3.7.x)** — adds a new "Frame Generation" row to the in-game sidebar Controls tab (just below the Winlator HUD row) with a switch + gear button. Tapping the gear opens a settings dialog with:
  - 6 presets: **ECO / FLOW / BAL (default) / BOOST / CLEAR / MAX** — picking one auto-loads its `flowScale` and AI model values; ECO/FLOW/BAL/BOOST use the Standard model (cheaper), CLEAR/MAX use the Clear model (heavier optical-flow path with less ghosting)
  - Multiplier picker (2x / 3x / 4x — the underlying `gamescope.control` mmap protocol)
  - `flowScale` slider (0.20–1.00) — overrides the preset's flowScale
  - FPS limit input (caps the source FPS so framegen has thermal headroom)
  - Settings live in `bh_framegen` SharedPreferences, applied at `WineActivity.onCreate` via a one-line smali hook into `BhFrameGenWriter.applyFromPrefs(Context)`.
- **Per-package ICD JSON** — `BhFrameGenWriter.ensureIcdJsonForCurrentPackage()` writes the `libGameScopeVK` ICD manifest at launch using `ctx.getPackageName()`, so framegen works for every BHL variant package (banner.hub.lite, gamehub.lite, com.tencent.ig, etc.) without per-variant patching.
- **Reflection-based fragment binding** — the smali hook into `SidebarControlsFragment.onResume()` passes `Object` (not a typed Fragment) so the extension Java code doesn't pull androidx onto the apktool compile classpath. Wiring resolves `getView()` reflectively at runtime.

### Server-side fix shipped alongside this build

- `bannerhub-api@45c3d2f` (2026-05-12): bumped `simulator/executeScript/{generic,qualcomm}{,_steam}` imagefs row from 1.3.3 → 1.3.7. The c8d7f21 commit (2026-05-11) bumped `data/imagefs.json` + `simulator/v2/getImagefsDetail` but missed the four executeScript variants, leaving the Add-Game path on the old 1.3.3 binary for ~22 hours. Verified live: BHL Add-Game now returns `imagefs_137.zst` md5 `82e98261…`.

### Upgrade

Same package names as v1.0.1 — install over the existing variant; no uninstall needed. The new sidebar row appears on next game launch.

### Variants

| APK | Package | App label |
|-----|---------|-----------|
| Normal | `banner.hub.lite` | BannerHub Lite |
| Normal(GHL) | `gamehub.lite` | BannerHub Lite |
| PuBG | `com.tencent.ig` | BannerHub Lite PuBG |
| AnTuTu | `com.antutu.ABenchMark` | BannerHub Lite AnTuTu |
| alt-AnTuTu | `com.antutu.benchmark.full` | BannerHub Lite AnTuTu |
| PuBG-CrossFire | `com.tencent.tmgp.cf` | BannerHub Lite PuBG CrossFire |
| Ludashi | `com.ludashi.aibench` | BannerHub Lite Ludashi |
| Genshin | `com.miHoYo.GenshinImpact` | BannerHub Lite Genshin |
| Original | `com.xiaoji.egggame` | BannerHub Lite |

### Legacy EmuReady builds

The `Build APK` workflow remains available via manual workflow_dispatch for anyone who still needs an EmuReady-flavored APK.

releases.name: BannerHub Lite v1.0.1
releases.body:
## BannerHub Lite v1.0.1

First stable on the BannerHub API.

> ⚠️ **URGENT — read before first launch.** If you see **"Failed to initialize the simulator"** when launching a game, your firmware is still on the old version. Open **Settings → Components → Firmware** and **manually update / install firmware 1.3.7**, then launch the game again. The auto-update prompt may not fire on every install, so do this manually if the error appears.

### Known issues

- **Proton 11 hangs on "Initializing…" when launching Steam games with a Steam client.** The launch never proceeds past the initializing screen. Workaround: use a different Wine/Proton component for Steam-client launches until this is resolved.

### What changed since v1.0.0

- **BannerHub API switch** — all 9 variants now point at `https://bannerhub-api.the412banner.workers.dev/` instead of the EmuReady worker. Components, firmware, and the in-app catalog are all served from the BannerHub Components release on this org.
- **Firmware bumped to 1.3.7** — the in-app firmware update prompt will offer `imagefs_137.zst` (171.9 MB). 5.x clients (BHL) and 6.0 clients (BannerHub 3.7.x) now receive the same firmware.
- **HID-mode launch dialog fix** — GameHub Lite 5.1.4 backports a touch/HID picker dialog from 5.3.5 that ships broken in 5.1.4 (transparent close button, unfocusable checkbox, no way to dismiss with controller). EmuReady's worker happened to 404 the underlying notice endpoint so the dialog never appeared; with BannerHub API the dialog would otherwise appear and block launches. The smali method that shows it (`IMappingServiceImpl.b()`) is rewritten to a no-op so games launch directly.

### Upgrade

Same package names as v1.0.0 — install over the existing variant; no uninstall needed.

### Variants

| APK | Package | App label |
|-----|---------|-----------|
| Normal | `banner.hub.lite` | BannerHub Lite |
| Normal(GHL) | `gamehub.lite` | BannerHub Lite |
| PuBG | `com.tencent.ig` | BannerHub Lite PuBG |
| AnTuTu | `com.antutu.ABenchMark` | BannerHub Lite AnTuTu |
| alt-AnTuTu | `com.antutu.benchmark.full` | BannerHub Lite AnTuTu |
| PuBG-CrossFire | `com.tencent.tmgp.cf` | BannerHub Lite PuBG CrossFire |
| Ludashi | `com.ludashi.aibench` | BannerHub Lite Ludashi |
| Genshin | `com.miHoYo.GenshinImpact` | BannerHub Lite Genshin |
| Original | `com.xiaoji.egggame` | BannerHub Lite |

### Legacy EmuReady builds

The `Build APK` workflow remains available via manual workflow_dispatch for anyone who still needs an EmuReady-flavored APK.

releases.name: BannerHub Lite v1.0.0
releases.body:
> ⚠️ **Install stable releases only.** Do not install pre-release builds or CI artifacts — they are untested snapshots used for development. Only stable releases (like this one) are debugged and verified across devices.

## New since v0.4.0

- **Export / Import Game Config** — save and share per-game Wine settings as JSON; configs are cross-compatible with BannerHub
- **Extra Detailed HUD** — second HUD mode (enable "Winlator HUD" + "Extra Detailed" in Performance sidebar); shows per-core CPU%, per-core MHz, GPU%, GPU/CPU temps, RAM%, SWAP, FPS graph, battery wattage; draggable, tap to toggle horizontal/vertical
- **D-pad focus highlights** — focused UI elements highlighted when navigating with a controller
- **GOG Akamai CDN fix** — chunk URLs with CDN tokens in query string no longer break downloads
- **Download manager sync** — GOG/Epic/Amazon download managers synced from BannerHub: 6-parallel chunks, speed display, filename progress
- **Config metadata** — exported configs tagged with `app_source="bannerhub_lite"` for community config site filtering
- **9 APK variants** — dedicated `banner.hub.lite` package added; BHApi variants removed; Genshin package name corrected

## Config Cross-Compatibility with BannerHub

Configs exported from BannerHub Lite are fully compatible with BannerHub, and vice versa.

**Why it works:** Both apps are built on the same GameHub engine. Per-game Wine settings are stored in SharedPreferences under the same keys (`pc_g_setting<gameId>`). The Export feature reads those keys and writes them to a JSON file. The Import feature reads that same JSON and writes the keys back — the app that originally exported it doesn't matter.

**How to transfer:** Export a config in one app (saved to `/sdcard/BannerHub/configs/`), copy the JSON to the same folder on the device running the other app, then Import it from the game's settings menu. The same folder path is used by both apps intentionally so configs are found automatically without moving files.

The `app_source` field in the JSON is only used by the community config site to filter by origin — it has no effect on import behavior.

> 💬 **Community game configs:** Share and download per-game settings at [the412banner.github.io/bannerhub-game-configs](https://the412banner.github.io/bannerhub-game-configs). The library grows with every contribution.

## Installation

**Choose your APK** — pick the variant matching your existing GameHub Lite package:

| APK | Package |
|-----|---------|
| Normal | `banner.hub.lite` |
| Normal(GHL) | `gamehub.lite` |
| PuBG | `com.tencent.ig` |
| AnTuTu | `com.antutu.ABenchMark` |
| alt-AnTuTu | `com.antutu.benchmark.full` |
| PuBG-CrossFire | `com.tencent.tmgp.cf` |
| Ludashi | `com.ludashi.aibench` |
| Genshin | `com.miHoYo.GenshinImpact` |
| Original | `com.xiaoji.egggame` |

**Uninstall** your existing GameHub Lite before installing — BannerHub Lite is signed with a different certificate and cannot update over the original.

releases.name: BannerHub Lite v0.4.0
releases.body:
> ⚠️ **IMPORTANT — Install stable releases only.**
> Do NOT install pre-releases or raw CI artifacts. Pre-releases are work-in-progress builds used during development and may crash, corrupt data, or behave unexpectedly. Only stable releases (like this one) are tested end-to-end across multiple devices before publishing.

---

## New since v0.3.7 (last stable)

- **Wine Task Manager sidebar tab** — tap the three-bar icon in the in-game sidebar to open the Task Manager
  - **Container Info**: CPU cores, RAM (used / total), VRam limit, device model, Android version
  - **Applications tab**: Wine infrastructure processes with PID and Kill button
  - **Processes tab**: Windows .exe processes with PID and Kill button
  - **Launch tab**: file browser rooted at WINEPREFIX/dosdevices — navigate drives and directories (yellow ▶), tap any .exe / .msi / .bat / .cmd to launch it directly via the running Wine binary
  - 3-second auto-refresh on Applications and Processes tabs

---

## Full Feature Set

### Game Store Tabs
- **GOG Games** — OAuth2 login, 3-gen download pipeline (Gen2/Gen1/installer fallback), 3 view modes (List/Grid/Poster), SteamGridDB cover art, A-Z sort, search, ✓ install indicator, Add to Launcher, Uninstall, Copy to Downloads, Set .exe picker, detail dialog
- **Amazon Games** — PKCE OAuth2, GetEntitlements library sync, manifest.proto (protobuf + LZMA/XZ), 6-parallel chunk download, SHA-256, fuel.json launch, FuelSDK + AmazonGamesSDK DLLs, ✓ indicator, Uninstall
- **Epic Games Store** — OAuth2, catalog sync, chunked manifest (hex file sizes, decimal group subfolders), Fastly/Akamai CDN (no auth token on chunks), 6-parallel download, exe picker + launch, ✓ indicator, Uninstall

### In-Game Sidebar
- **Wine Task Manager** — Applications / Processes / Launch tabs; Kill button; Container Info (CPU, RAM, VRam, Device, Android); 3s auto-refresh; WINEPREFIX file browser with exe launcher
- **Winlator HUD Overlay** — API / GPU% / CPU% / RAM% / BAT / TMP / FPS + rolling graph; opacity slider (0–100%); toggle in Controls tab
- **Performance Toggles** (root required) — Sustained Performance Mode (`setSustainedPerformanceMode` + CPU `performance` governor); Max Adreno Clocks (KGSL DVFS floor = max)

### Component System
- **Component Manager** — color-coded cards; type/source badges; Add / Inject+Replace / Backup / Remove / Remove All
- **Online Component Downloader** — 6 repos: Arihany WCPHub, The412Banner Nightlies, K11MCH1, StevenMXZ, MTR, Whitebelyash; three-level nav (Repo → Type → Asset)
- Formats: `.wcp` (zstd-tar), `.wcp` (XZ-tar), `.zip` (GPU drivers)

### Settings & System
- **VRAM Limit Unlock** — adds 6 GB / 8 GB / 12 GB / 16 GB to the per-game VRAM picker
- **Per-Game CPU Core Affinity** — checkbox dialog (Efficiency / Performance / Prime cores)
- **GPU System Driver Default** — new games default to System Driver to prevent first-launch crashes
- **Grant Root Access** — in Settings → Advanced; stored result, no repeated prompts
- **Offline Steam Skip** — bypasses login screen when Steam is unreachable at cold start
- **Launch Fix (Hardware Whitelist Bypass)** — treats HTTP 404 device-check as pass
- **BCI Launcher Button** — top-right toolbar shortcut to BannersComponentInjector
- **"My Games"** tab label (renamed from "Dashboard")
- **Japanese translations** — 3,534 strings from Crowdin

---

## Installation

BannerHub Lite is signed with AOSP testkey — **uninstall your existing GameHub Lite before installing** (your game library and containers in app data will be lost unless backed up).

Pick the APK matching your current GameHub Lite package name:

| APK | Package |
|-----|---------|
| Normal / Normal-BHApi | `gamehub.lite` |
| PuBG / PuBG-BHApi | `com.tencent.ig` |
| AnTuTu / AnTuTu-BHApi | `com.antutu.ABenchMark` |
| alt-AnTuTu / alt-AnTuTu-BHApi | `com.antutu.benchmark.full` |
| PuBG-CrossFire / PuBG-CrossFire-BHApi | `com.tencent.tmgp.cf` |
| Ludashi / Ludashi-BHApi | `com.ludashi.aibench` |
| Genshin / Genshin-BHApi | `com.mihoyo.genshinimpact` |
| Original / Original-BHApi | `com.xiaoji.egggame` |

**Regular** = connects to the real GameHub API. **BHApi** = connects to the self-hosted [bannerhub-api](https://github.com/The412Banner/bannerhub-api) worker.

releases.name: BannerHub Lite v0.3.7-pre4
releases.body:
**feat: HUD opacity slider**

A SeekBar ("HUD Opacity: N%") appears below the Winlator HUD toggle whenever the HUD is enabled. Drag it to adjust background transparency 0–100% live — no restart needed. Value persists in bh_prefs as `hud_opacity`.

releases.name: BannerHub Lite v0.3.7-pre3
releases.body:
**fix: Winlator HUD FPS reading**

In Lite 5.1.4, `WineActivity.j` is a Map — not an IHudDataProvider — so `readFps()` silently returned 0.

Fix: `BhFrameRating.readFps()` now reads from `WineActivity.i` (HUDUpdater) → `g()F`, which is the same averaged frame-list source the native HUD uses. Falls back to the original `j` path for BH 5.3.5 base APKs.

releases.name: BannerHub Lite v0.3.7-pre2
releases.body:
Fix: Winlator HUD toggle injection

Changes in this build:
- **HUD injection fix**: Fragment context IS WineActivity — no longer uses fragile WineActivity.t1 reflection; cast ctx directly to Activity
- **ContentView target**: HUD now added to android.R.id.content + bringToFront() to ensure it renders above Wine surface
- **Verbose logging**: every step in the HUD toggle path logs to BannerHub tag for debugging

To test: open a game in Wine, open the Controls sidebar, toggle 'Winlator HUD'. The HUD overlay should appear in the top-right corner.

releases.name: BannerHub Lite v0.3.6
releases.body:
> ⚠️ **INSTALL STABLE RELEASES ONLY**
> Do **not** install pre-release builds (`-pre`) or workflow artifacts. Pre-releases are unstable work-in-progress builds that may crash, corrupt data, or behave unexpectedly. Only stable releases (like this one) have been tested and are safe to install.

---

## New since v0.3.5 (last stable)

- **Install confirmation dialog** — tapping Install now shows the game's download size and your available storage before anything downloads. Size is fetched from the GOG manifest (Gen 2: sum of depot sizes; Gen 1: total_size field).
- **Cancel download** — during an active download the Install button turns red and becomes **Cancel**. Tapping it stops the download thread and deletes all partial files from the install directory, then resets the card to its pre-install state.
- **Exe picker post-install** — after install completes, BannerHub Lite scans the install directory for qualifying executables (excluding redist/setup/unins/crash/helper/dotnet/vcredist/directx paths). If exactly one is found it is auto-selected. If two or more are found, a picker dialog lets you choose the correct one before the Add to Launcher button appears.
- **Set .exe in detail dialog** — long-pressing an installed game now shows the current launch executable filename and a **Set .exe…** button. Tapping it re-scans the install directory and shows the exe picker, letting you correct a wrong selection at any time.

---

## Full Feature Set

### GOG Games Tab
- OAuth2 login (WebView), auto token refresh, persistent session
- Library cache — instant load + background sync
- Real-time search bar
- A-Z sort
- **3 view modes:** List (collapsible cards) / Grid (5-col tiles 105dp) / Poster (5-col tiles 176dp portrait)
- SteamGridDB cover art (600×900); fallback GOG icon → background
- Gen badge (Gen 1 orange / Gen 2 blue)
- Installed ✓ indicator on all views
- Tap → action row: Install / Add to Launcher + live progress bar
- **Confirmation dialog** before download (size + available storage)
- **Cancel download** button (red during download — stops thread + deletes partial files)
- **Exe picker** after install (auto-selects if 1 candidate; dialog if 2+)
- Long-press → detail dialog: description, current .exe name, **Set .exe…** button, Uninstall, Copy to Downloads
- Gen 2 / Gen 1 / installer fallback download pipeline
- Per-file filename + percentage counter during download
- Add Game auto-fills GameHub's import dialog
- Uninstall (deletes folder + clears prefs)
- Copy to Downloads (MediaStore, no storage permission needed)

### Component Manager
- Color-coded cards (DXVK / VKD3D / Box64 / FEXCore / GPU Driver)
- Live search, install count header
- Add New Component (file picker → inject)
- Inject / Replace, Backup to Downloads, Remove, Remove All (BannerHub-managed only)

### Online Component Downloader
- 3-level nav: Repo → Type → Asset list
- 6 built-in sources: Arihany WCPHub, The412Banner Nightlies, Kimchi (K11MCH1), StevenMXZ, MTR, whitebelyash Turnip

- **Max Adreno Clocks** toggle (root) — locks KGSL min_freq = max_freq

releases.name: v0.3.3
releases.body:
> [!WARNING]
> **Only install APKs from stable releases (tagged `vX.Y.Z`). Do NOT install pre-release builds or CI artifacts.**
> Pre-releases are active development snapshots — they may contain unfinished features, broken pipelines, or instability. Stable releases are fully tested, signed (v1/v2/v3), and verified to have passed a clean CI build with correct versionName and app labels.

### New since v0.3.2 (last stable)

- **GOG Games tab** — full integration via side menu → GOG
  - OAuth2 login via WebView, session stored in `bh_gog_prefs` SharedPreferences
  - Auto token refresh; login persists across restarts
  - Library cache — displayed instantly on open; background sync updates it silently
  - ↺ Refresh button in header; 5-thread parallel sync (~3–5× faster)
  - Game cards with square cover art (icon image), Gen 1/Gen 2 badge, developer, ✓ Installed checkmark, progress bar, live download percentage
  - Gen 2 download — content-system manifest → depot files → chunk download + assembly; paths normalized for Android
  - Gen 1 download — Range-based byte download for legacy pre-Galaxy games
  - Installer fallback — for very old games with no content-system builds; downloads Windows `.exe` installer directly from GOG's download API
  - Add Game flow — after install, tapping "Add Game" auto-closes GOG screen and opens GameHub's import dialog with exe path pre-filled
  - ✓ Installed checkmark appears immediately on completion, persists on relaunch
  - Uninstall via detail dialog — deletes folder, clears prefs, resets card immediately
  - Copy to Downloads — detail dialog → copies game folder to `Download/GOG Games/<title>/` via MediaStore (no storage permission required on Android 10+)

### Full Feature Set

- **GOG Games tab** — full OAuth2 + library cache + parallel sync + Gen2/Gen1/installer download + cover art + Add Game + Uninstall + Copy to Downloads
- **My Games tab** — renamed from "My"
- **BCI launcher button** — top-right toolbar shortcut to BannersComponentInjector
- **Component Manager** — card UI with color-coded type badges, live search, Add / Inject / Backup / Remove / Remove All
- **Online Component Downloader** — 6 community repos, 3-level navigation (repo → type → asset), auto-install
- **Performance Sidebar** — Sustained Performance Mode (setSustainedPerformanceMode + CPU `performance` governor) + Max Adreno Clocks (KGSL min_freq = max_freq); both root-only
- **Grant Root Access** — Settings → Advanced; result stored so Performance tab never prompts unsolicited
- **VRAM Limit Unlock** — 6 GB / 8 GB / 12 GB / 16 GB options added
- **Per-Game CPU Core Affinity** — Efficiency / Performance / Prime core labels, custom bitmask
- **GPU System Driver Default** — new games default to System Driver
- **Offline Steam Launch** — skips Steam login screen when offline
- **Launch Fix** — hardware whitelist bypass (404 treated as pass)
- **RTS Touch Controls** — built into GameHub Lite 5.1.4 base, no patch needed

### Installation

Choose the APK matching your existing GameHub Lite package name:

| APK | Package | App Label |
|-----|---------|-----------|
| `Bannerhub-Lite-v0.3.3-Normal.apk` | `gamehub.lite` | BannerHub Lite |
| `Bannerhub-Lite-v0.3.3-PuBG.apk` | `com.tencent.ig` | BannerHub Lite PuBG |
| `Bannerhub-Lite-v0.3.3-AnTuTu.apk` | `com.antutu.ABenchMark` | BannerHub Lite AnTuTu |
| `Bannerhub-Lite-v0.3.3-alt-AnTuTu.apk` | `com.antutu.benchmark.full` | BannerHub Lite AnTuTu |
| `Bannerhub-Lite-v0.3.3-PuBG-CrossFire.apk` | `com.tencent.tmgp.cf` | BannerHub Lite CrossFire |
| `Bannerhub-Lite-v0.3.3-Ludashi.apk` | `com.ludashi.aibench` | BannerHub Lite Ludashi |
| `Bannerhub-Lite-v0.3.3-Genshin.apk` | `com.mihoyo.genshinimpact` | BannerHub Lite Genshin |
| `Bannerhub-Lite-v0.3.3-Original.apk` | `com.xiaoji.egggame` | BannerHub Lite |

**If you have GameHub Lite installed with the same package name as your chosen APK, you must uninstall it first** — BannerHub Lite is signed with a different certificate (AOSP testkey) and cannot overwrite it. APKs with a different package name will coexist alongside your existing installation without conflict. Your game library and containers will be lost on uninstall unless backed up first.

All APKs are signed with AOSP testkey (v1/v2/v3). App label and versionName are set correctly per variant.

releases.name: GameHub 5.3.5 Lite V2-hotfix
releases.body:
Changelog:
• Ported SD Card support from recent [GameHub 5.3.3 Release Candidate](https://github.com/Producdevity/gamehub-lite/releases/tag/v5.3.3-RC1) releases
• Merged two APKs into one - added API switch into settings, by default it's set to EmuReady API, can be switched to official GameHub API, restart the app after changing API

Hotfix:
• Included Documents Provider patch (access to /data/data without root)

releases.name: GameHub 5.3.5 Lite V1
releases.body:
Ported [GameHub Lite 5.3.3](https://github.com/Producdevity/gamehub-lite/releases/tag/v5.3.3) modification to 5.3.5, two versions included, general changes:
- Disabled Discover and Free games tabs (as they don't work in both versions)
- DocumentsProvider patch included (app's /data/ can be accessed through file managers)
- Preserved original GameHub resources (icon and stuff, lazy to replace)
- Used package name - emuready.gamehub.lite
- Signed with testkey

Official API version:
- Uses GameSir server for downloading firmware/drivers/other components, usually up to date, may have privacy concerns
- More cluttered My Games list, includes PC/PS Link (idk if it is working)
- Can import Android games
- No login required
- Offline is working

EmuReady API version:
- Uses EmuReady server to download firmware/drivers/other components, more private
- Has more GPU drivers to download, but may not have the latest components to download
- Clean My Games list (only Windows emulation and Steam)
- Can't import Android games
- No login required
- Offline is working

Only difference in APK files are https server strings, everything else is the same!

releases.name: Computers Are Totally Screwed 27
releases.body:
# Changelog

### Core
* Components list by [StevenMXZ](https://github.com/StevenMXZ) and [Xnick417x](https://github.com/Xnick417x/winlator-nightly-wcp)
* Runtime download content by [Arihany](https://github.com/Arihany/WinlatorWCPHub#-runtime-packages) and [ajay9634](https://github.com/ajay9634/Ajay-prefix)
* Store feature from [Ludashi-plus](https://github.com/The412Banner/Ludashi-plus/releases/tag/v3.1.2-pre2)
* Win components from [Winlator 11](https://github.com/brunodev85/winlator/releases/tag/v11.0.0)

### Drivers
* Add DD7to9 v1.7 wrapper
* Add new Qualcomm and Turnip drivers
* Defaults for Pico and Quest headsets updated
* DXVK framesync, MangoHUD, DXVK framerates updated
* Improvements to global default values
* Installing and updating content on app start fixed
* Set default DXVK frame limit to refresh rate

### Modding tools
* Improved game support for Reshade
* Injecting Reshade from Z drive fixed
* TrackIR emulation using opentrack_wxr integrated

### Updated UI
* Box64 RC manager moved into settings
* Close Controller Manager dialog on app pause/close
* Controller notice removed
* Controller tweaks moved from settings into Controller manager
* Default container resolutions cleanup
* Do not show Proton in contents type list
* DXVK guide into selection dialog added
* Move wheel mapping option into motion controls dialog
* Move left-handed mapping option into controller manager
* New cleaned settings UI
* Shortcut option to autoclose container added

### XrAPI v0.5
* Cache framesync mapping between the sessions
* Controller grip quaternions exposed
* Container resolution in Z:\tmp\xr\system
* HMD altitude exposed
* XServer input APIs added

### XR support
* CAS screen effect
* Controller updates mouse buttons only if state changed
* Custom keyboard instead of OS keyboard
* Gray out passthrough when in immersive mode
* Immersive mode with user defined distance
* Improve snapturn mapping
* Increase controller thumbstick deadzone
* Pixmap update frequency increased to 72Hz
* Virtual wheel mapping now uses analog joystick input

# Credits
* Drivers and wrappers were updated and tested by @GmoLargey
* The store integration has been polished by @lvonasek, @Gilleece
* TrackIR integration has been done by @bigelod

### Bug fixes

releases.name: Exiled-Kingdoms-Multiplayer-2.0
releases.body:
# 🛡️ [MOD] Exiled Kingdoms LAN Multiplayer — 2.0!

Hey everyone! Another update to the **LAN Multiplayer** mod for Exiled Kingdoms.

Here's the full summary:

---

## ⚔️ NEW — LAN Multiplayer Bug Fixes

### 🐛 Bug: Crash when entering Cities
- **Problem:** When entering some cities, the game would crash and close.
- **Fix:** Problem fixed, and you can now teleport and enter cities perfectly.

### 🐛 Bug: Monsters ignored the CLIENT when the HOST was far away
- **Problem:** Monsters only attacked when the HOST was nearby. If only the CLIENT approached, the monster would remain still.
- **Fix:** Detection now compares the HOST and all CLIENTs present on the same map and returns the **closest** to the monster as the target.

### 🐛 Bug: Incorrect monster level scaling in co-op
- **Problem:** Monsters scaled based on 0+ (extra players). × 2), resulting in level 2 Goblins in a session with 2
- **Correction:** Now uses the Bestiary's `minlevel` as a base before applying the LAN difficulty multiplier. Example: Goblin (base level 4) + 2 players = level 6. Yeti (base level 18) + 2 players = level 22.

### 🐛 Bug: CLIENT couldn't reduce HOST's HP in the PVP Arena
- **Problem:** In the PVP arena, the CLIENT attacked the HOST but the HOST's HP didn't decrease. The reverse worked correctly.

- **Correction:** CLIENTs can now post damage to peers in any context where the attack is valid. Outside the arena, the game's faction system already prevents attacks between allies naturally.

### 🐛 Bug: PVP Arena weapons remained in inventory upon exiting
- **Problem:** After PVP combat, the weapons provided by Arena Master items would remain in the player's inventory even after speaking with them to leave.

- **Correction:** When speaking with the Arena Master to leave (victory, defeat, or opponent disconnection), the game now explicitly removes all arena items before restoring the player's original inventory and using the **teleport** item to leave the Arena (Requires INT 2) to use the item.

---

## 🔧 Previous Improvements (summary)

| Improvement | Description |
|---|---|
| ✅ Rebalanced Gems | Cleaned containers + gems as monster drops |
| ✅ Gold -75% | 291 loot entries reduced for a more balanced economy |
| ✅ Weapon and Item Upgrades | Upgrade system implemented and corrected |
| ✅ Monsters +3 levels | 1364 scaled monsters, guaranteed minimum level = 4 |
| ✅ Translation | 95% complete All languages ​​translated |
| ✅ Loot | Loot fixed for chests, removing gems that were constantly appearing |

---

## 👹 **Added gem drop from specific monsters** with low chances:
| Monster | Gem | Chance (%) |
|---------|-----|-----------|
| Bosses (Minotaur, Dragon, etc.) | Ruby, Emerald, Sapphire | 15-25% |
| Elite mobs (Orc Leader, Dark Elf, etc.) | Small Ruby/Emerald/Sapphire | 5-10% |
| Strong Undead (Skeleton Champion, Lich) | Small Diamond | 3-8% |
| Dragons | Pearl, Purple Diamond | 2-5% |
| Common mobs (Orc, Bandit) | Small Ruby, Small Emerald | 1-3% |

---

## 📋 How Co-op Works Now

- **Same Map:** Monsters attack the nearest player (HOST or CLIENT) ✅
- **Different Maps:** Each player has their own monsters roaming independently ✅
- **PVP Arena:** Real PVP works — both players attack each other and lose HP ✅
- **Authoritative HOST:** Monsters and NPCs are controlled exclusively by the HOST, with no state conflict ✅

---

⚠️ **Tested in local LAN sessions with 2 players.**

⚠️ **Multiplayer performance depends on your connection; ensure a stable connection!!!**

⚠️ **Uninstall the previous version and install this one from scratch.**

---

**Thank you for the feedback; this improvement was thanks to your testing 🤜🤛**

releases.name: Exiled-Kingdoms-Multiplayer-1.0
releases.body: # ⚔️ Exiled Kingdoms — Multiplayer Hoje estou lançando uma versão muito especial do meu projeto para **Exiled Kingdoms**: um mod focado em **multiplayer Wi-Fi/VPN aprimorado**, **Arena PVP**, **alguns conteúdos inéditos**, **nova interface medieval** e várias correções importantes. Essa release foi construída para entregar uma experiência mais divertida, mais estável e muito mais completa para quem sempre quis ver Exiled Kingdoms com um lado mais social e competitivo. --- ## 🔥 Destaques desta versão ### 🎮 Multiplayer Wi-Fi/VPN muito mais sólido O sistema multiplayer foi retrabalhado para oferecer uma experiência melhor em coop, com: - sincronização de sprites em tempo real - movimentação remota mais suave - Companion AI funcionando para clientes Wi-Fi/VPN - chat funcional com suporte multilíngue - sons espaciais de combate por proximidade - limpeza correta de peers desconectados - proteção contra corrupção de save **MODO LAN NO CANTO SUPERIOR DIREITO** **UM DISPOSITIVO É O HOST E OUTRO É O CLIENT QUE VAI ADICIONAR O IP DO HOST OU BUSCA LAN E SE CONECTA** **CHAT LAN PARA OS JOGADORES PODEREM CONVERSAR E SABER ONDE ESTÁ CADA JOGADOR** --- ### 🎮 Aumentar e Diminuir a dificuldade quando quiser --- ### ⚔️ Nova Arena PVP 1v1 Agora o mod conta com uma **Arena PVP completa** para duelos entre jogadores em sessões "LAN". 📍 **Local:** Vale de Lannegar 👤 **NPC:** Mestre da Arena --- ### 🧙 Conteúdo exclusivo Esta release também adiciona conteúdo inédito ao jogo. #### 🏟️ Mestre da Arena Novo NPC responsável por iniciar e gerenciar o sistema PVP. --- ### 🎨 Nova interface medieval O lobby Wi-Fi/VPN e o chat foram redesenhados com uma identidade visual medieval dark fantasy, incluindo: - fundos escuros com tons de cobre/bronze - botões com bordas douradas - painéis estilizados - visual temático mais imersivo Tudo foi implementado programaticamente para manter compatibilidade e desempenho. --- ### 📦 Sistema de loot melhorado Diversos containers do mundo agora possuem tabelas de loot mais temáticas e mais equilibradas. O balanceamento foi pensado para enriquecer a exploração sem quebrar a progressão natural do jogo. --- ## 🌍 Idiomas Suporte para: - English - Español - Português (Brasil) - Русский - Deutsch - Polski - Čeština Os conteúdos exclusivos principais possuem suporte completo em **EN**, **ES** e **PT-BR**. --- ## ⚠️ Observações ### Requisitos do PVP - ambos os jogadores devem estar na mesma rede Wi‑Fi/VPN - é recomendado já ter visitado **Kingsbridge** - o PVP funciona em formato **1v1** ou **até 6 jogadores** ### Compatibilidade - Android 5.0+ - base Exiled Kingdoms --- ## 💬 Feedback Se encontrar bugs ou quiser mandar sugestões, abra uma issue ou envie seu feedback com o máximo de detalhes possível. --- ## 🛡️ Agradecimentos Obrigado a todo mundo que acompanha o projeto e apoia essa ideia de levar Exiled Kingdoms para um nível totalmente novo. Espero que gostem :)
